docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2654:docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:14:trait and consumes it — no cycle. This is consistent with D0: the kernel defines the seam,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2664:docs/design/nip29-crate.md:63:| `GroupDiscussion` | 11 (when `h` present and `["t","discussion"]` marker) | `(host_relay_url, group_id, event_id)` | Threaded discussion *roots only*. Replies today are public NIP-22 kind:1111 *without* `h`, owned by `nmp-nip22` (verified per `kinds.md` §2.1 against Highlighter's `comments.rs::publish_comment`). `nmp-nip29::GroupComment` exists for *future* h-tagged in-room comments (Highlighter post-M11.5 may attach `h` to in-room composers; until then `GroupComment` is forward-looking and not consumed by the M11.5 Highlighter rebuild). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2702:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:218:./docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2703:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:241:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:88:- Zero open TODO/FIXME/unimplemented in FFI/actor/relay/kernel/iOS bridge
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2704:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:244:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:603:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2743:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:367:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1791:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6425:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1388:Review: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 + D2 — composite reverse index + working-set bound must remain enforced through the trait); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance; (4) consistency of EventStore trait surface across the 6 sub-docs; (5) the choice of nostr-lmdb as on-disk byte store vs roll-our-own — is the gap analysis honest?; (6) the GC eviction algorithm + budget; (7) the migration plumbing's testability; (8) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2744:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:368:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1972:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8173:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2750:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:378:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2783:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:526:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6081:+docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2808:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:666:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:8173:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2847:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:905:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1552:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2848:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:909:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1783:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:1611:+Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2927:docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2932:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e8b1726.md:27:All four codex issues are resolved. No new TODO/FIXME/unimplemented! introduced. All changed files remain under 300 LOC soft limit (gates.md: 303 LOC, marginally over soft limit — acceptable). No FFI surface changed in code; no new ADR required. Doctrine compliance intact.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2935:docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:48:8. **Relay-count optimization (greedy set-cover).** Stage 3 currently assigns interests to every declared write relay. Applesauce's `selectOptimalRelays` (`docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md` §3, `relay-selection.ts:14-93`) shows a greedy set-cover heuristic that caps to `maxConnections` relays while maximizing author coverage. NMP should add an equivalent cap policy in a future ADR, after M2 proves the basic per-relay fan-out is correct. Referenced by `compiler.md` §3.3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2943:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2108:/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"TODO|FIXME|XXX|HACK|DEFERRED|deferred|M9|M11|M11\\.5|M12|Wallet|DM|framework-magic|ADR-0009|D0|D1|D2|D3|D4|D5\" docs/research/applesauce docs/research/ndk" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2952:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2246:docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2953:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2247:docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2960:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2298:docs/aim.md:99:The architectural delta is the core idea of this project: use the Rust Nostr SDK family for protocol primitives, then build a new Rust application kernel above it. We are not forking the Rust SDK and we are not porting Applesauce or NDK APIs. We are creating the missing multiplatform app layer: actor-owned state, bounded FFI projections, canonical store semantics, subscription and action lifecycle, storage/metrics/test harnesses, and extension seams for later product modules.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2984:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2454:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:2999:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2495:docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:186:The M2 `PlannerHarness` (`subscription-compilation/tests.md` §9.3) is deliberately scoped to the planner subsystem: it tests the compile function in isolation, with no actor, no FFI seam, no storage backend. Several framework-magic bullets (C7 publish flow, C11 onboarding, C12 account switch, C13 in-place refinement) require the full actor and the full FFI emission path. The `ContractHarness` is therefore a strict superset.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3023:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3403:docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3025:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3405:docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3049:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:4093:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3096:docs/design/ffi-hardening/ci.md:198:            fails.append("FFI grep yielded TODO/FIXME tokens; see §7.1")
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3267:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17146:1419:docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:396:The FFI and iOS bridge code is **clean of blocking debt markers** (TODO/FIXME/unimplemented/panic). All 20 code inspection findings are either justified by design or actionable as documentation cleanup. All cardinal doctrines (D0–D5) are upheld.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3268:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17147:1440:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3269:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f765f60.md:17148:1451:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3432:docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:216:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3477:docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:376:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1257: | 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3480:docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:411:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:88:- Zero open TODO/FIXME/unimplemented in FFI/actor/relay/kernel/iOS bridge
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:414:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:603:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3513:docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:586:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9fead0e.md:1988:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3055:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3520:docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:648:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:218:./docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3568:docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:808:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:378:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3629:docs/perf/codex-reviews/446a212.md:1130:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2477372.md:700:./docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3055:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:3983:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5924:- `810d0f8:README.md:28-31` mislabels doctrine. It says D2 = reactivity, D3 = errors-never-FFI, D5 = capabilities-report, but canonical §1.5 says D2 = negentropy, D3 = outbox, D5 = bounded snapshots in [docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8c0acab50f455ada/docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:54). This contradicts the doctrine and plan. Current HEAD appears to have a later fix in `3f5a2b3`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:5059:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:3055:docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:5186:    12	- **The seam that emits REQs is parameterized by `RelayRole`.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:530-556` (`req()`) inserts a `WireSub { role, .. }` keyed by a string sub-id and emits `OutboundMessage { role, text }`. The role *is* the routing decision; there is no relay-URL field on `WireSub` or `OutboundMessage`. Any compiler that fans an interest out across N URLs has to replace this helper.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:5187:    13	- **Startup REQs ignore mailboxes by construction.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106` (`startup_requests`) issues six fixed REQs, each pinned to `Content` or `Indexer`. The seed-bootstrap timeline (line 65–70) fans seven hundred-author future timelines through one relay. The exit-gate test for M2 ([`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)) requires that this fan exactly equal the union of those authors' write relays.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:5191:    17	- **No publish path exists yet.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:30` (no occurrences of `EVENT` outbound) and `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:42-45` (`OutboundMessage` carries only role + text). The first publish action (M6 `SendNote`) will hit this same `req()`-style seam. M2 must establish the planner shape before M6 builds the first user of it; the doctrine "no developer-supplied relays for a publish" (`docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 row "Publish leaked to wrong relays") needs a structural enforcement point.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:5354:   180	The boundary is intentional: an interest is anything that asks the planner to *keep a REQ open*. Everything else routes through a different seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:5482:   124	(capping to `maxConnections`) is a future extension. For M2, every declared write relay gets
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:5483:   125	a REQ; relay-count optimization is open question 8 (future ADR).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:5497:   139	    pub canonical_filter_hash: String,         // for ADR-0007 WireSubscriptionStatus
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:5501:   143	The wire-emitter renders each `SubShape` as exactly one `REQ` on `relay_url` with a sub-id of `c{plan_id}-r{relay_idx}-s{shape_idx}`. The sub-id is meaningful only to the kernel; diagnostics use `canonical_filter_hash` for stable identity across re-emission.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:5556:   198	| `firehose_requests` (requests.rs:357-372) | Replaced as described above for `open_firehose_tag`. The `diag-firehose-N` sub-id scheme goes away — `canonical_filter_hash` plus `plan_id` give stable identity. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:5651:    69	This is a "shape of the API" assertion, not a behaviour assertion. If a future PR adds a relay field to `SendNote`, the test fails.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:7519:   147	- `Provenance → NIP-65 hint refresh.` If we observe many `ProvenanceRelayFact { relay_url: R, author: A }` records but no `Nip65RelayFact { pubkey: A, relay_url: R }`, the operator diagnostic can suggest "we are receiving A's events from R but A has not declared R; their kind:10002 may be stale." This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:8224:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:153:Atomicity invariant: an action's local event-store commit, side-effect intent, and ledger transition happen as actor messages with one parent action id. The action future runs on the tokio runtime, but all state mutation happens in `handle_message`. There is no public API that lets a developer publish, upload, sign, or issue an NWC request without a renderable action-ledger row.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:8242:docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:176:The `MailboxCache` trait is the seam M3 (LMDB persistence) replaces with a backing-store-aware implementation. The compiler does not know which backend it is reading.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:8267:docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:348:If the M3 (LMDB) milestone graduates the mailbox cache to a real backend, this test continues to exercise the trait surface via the `InMemoryMailboxCache` impl — no changes required. That is the seam `nmp-nip65::cache::MailboxCache` exists for.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:8269:docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:120:| 6. v1 release | Publish primitive kernel crates, minimal examples, migration notes, explicit future extension points | A developer can build a small event viewer without writing cleanup, dedup, replaceable handling, or FFI batching |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/92b8260.md:8336:   176	The `MailboxCache` trait is the seam M3 (LMDB persistence) replaces with a backing-store-aware implementation. The compiler does not know which backend it is reading.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:88:-The repository is a Cargo workspace plus per-platform shells. The layout below is the long-term workspace shape. v1 publishes only the kernel subset described in `docs/plan.md`; product crates remain placeholders or later milestones until the kernel proves its invariants.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:89:+The repository is a Cargo workspace plus per-platform shells. The layout below is the long-term workspace shape. v1 publishes only the kernel subset described in [`docs/plan.md`](plan.md); product crates remain placeholders or later milestones until the kernel proves its invariants.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:143: M11 adds **nine** new `CapabilityModule`s to the kernel's reusable set. Each is generic (no podcast nouns in request or result types) so a future Pika-like, Highlighter, or messaging app could reuse them. Each is **idempotent** (start/stop/restart safe per RMP bible commandment #7) and has **bounded native state** (the bridge holds only the OS handle; no caches, no derived state, no policy).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:215: - **The seam that emits REQs is parameterized by `RelayRole`.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:530-556` (`req()`) inserts a `WireSub { role, .. }` keyed by a string sub-id and emits `OutboundMessage { role, text }`. The role *is* the routing decision; there is no relay-URL field on `WireSub` or `OutboundMessage`. Any compiler that fans an interest out across N URLs has to replace this helper.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:216:-- **Startup REQs ignore mailboxes by construction.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106` (`startup_requests`) issues six fixed REQs, each pinned to `Content` or `Indexer`. The seed-bootstrap timeline (line 65–70) fans seven hundred-author future timelines through one relay. The exit-gate test for M2 (`docs/plan.md` lines 134–137) requires that this fan exactly equal the union of those authors' write relays.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:217:+- **Startup REQs ignore mailboxes by construction.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106` (`startup_requests`) issues six fixed REQs, each pinned to `Content` or `Indexer`. The seed-bootstrap timeline (line 65–70) fans seven hundred-author future timelines through one relay. The exit-gate test for M2 ([`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)) requires that this fan exactly equal the union of those authors' write relays.

The seam M2 lands here must make that test possible. Specifically:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:290: By design (these belong to other M2 gates or later milestones):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:327:-- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:379:-3. **Capability layering is strict.** Each milestone adds exactly one new architectural ingredient on top of the previous demo. No "we'll wire it up later" — wiring is the milestone.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:393:-**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:661:-**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:679:-- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2151: Atomicity invariant: an action's local event-store commit, side-effect intent, and ledger transition happen as actor messages with one parent action id. The action future runs on the tokio runtime, but all state mutation happens in `handle_message`. There is no public API that lets a developer publish, upload, sign, or issue an NWC request without a renderable action-ledger row.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2154:Review ONLY: (1) are per-milestone files actually <=300 LOC, (2) is any content dropped in the split vs the original plan.md, (3) are M9/M12 deferred properly (not in active v1 ladder), (4) any TODO/FIXME, (5) internal consistency. Be terse and produce your final findings directly - do NOT do extensive file reading.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2156:I’ll do a narrow doc review against the requested checks: LOC, split coverage, M9/M12 active/deferred state, TODO markers, and link/consistency errors.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2316:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2321:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:295:`nmp-wallet` is staged. NIP-47 Wallet Connect lands first because it is a bounded client protocol over the action ledger and secret capabilities. Cashu/NIP-60 and nutzaps/NIP-61 come later because they require a durable wallet state machine, mint metadata cache, proof/token indexes, redemption state, and repair flows.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2350:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:483:1. Phase 1a.1 (kernel substrate prototype) ships with one fixture module (`fixture-todo-core`) demonstrating each of the five trait families. Codegen produces a working `nmp-app-fixture` crate. Desktop iced app renders a TODO list, no business logic in Swift / iced.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2351:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:485:3. A future Highlighter-lite, TENEX-lite, or podcast-lite module can be added without changes to `nmp-core` traits. Demonstrated on paper for v1; demonstrated in code post-v1.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2368:docs/design/podcast/risks.md:105:- **Context**: V4V/Lightning splits could feed into a future zaps integration (M12). Today we have nowhere to put them.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2372:docs/design/podcast/podcast-llm.md:284:The rig.rs route through `rig-core` is **required to land and pass its end-to-end test in M11**. It is not deferred to M15 or any later milestone. Rationale: M11 is the kernel-boundary check; the rig.rs path is the proof that `podcast-llm` is genuinely multiplatform — not an iOS wrapper around Apple Intelligence with a Rust veneer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2375:docs/design/podcast/lessons.md:74:Their `FINAL_PLAN.md §11` ("Migration Strategy") was deferred to M12 (penultimate milestone). They later learned that **schema-level domain modeling needs to be right before view wiring begins**, because the view payloads are direct projections of the domain shape. We make the inverse choice: [`podcast-core.md`](podcast-core.md) §B is the **first** thing built; views come after.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:367:++**Statement.** Every display-bearing field of every view payload is **non-`Option`** and carries either an authoritative value or a defined placeholder. When the authoritative value later arrives — a kind:0 for an author, kind:9735 zap receipts for a note, the decrypted body for a DM — the same payload re-emits with the field updated in place. The platform's reactive primitive (`@Observable` / `Flow` / signals) sees the change and only the affected cell re-renders. **No spinner ever gates an already-rendered cell, and no view module ever exposes a `loading: bool` to the platform.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:461:++| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:599:++- **Mute-list changes (kind:10000).** The mute list is structurally analogous, but the user's scope-adjustments doc explicitly names kind:3. Mute-list auto-tracking would be a C5-shaped sibling bullet (potential C14 future addition); not in the v1 contract surface.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:660:++**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]**. M2 lands the `PublishPlanner` trait + `Nip65PublishPlanner` + the `PublishWithOverride` action (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` §7.1, §7.2, §7.4). M6 lands `SendNoteAction` as the first concrete consumer. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M2 planner + M6 first consumer"]`. Sub-paths 3 and 4 of the test exercise the planner in isolation (M2-completable); 1, 2, and 5 require M6's action consumer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:676:++- NIP-77 sync routing — that's C10 in `sync.md`. Sync and live REQ should share relay policy (`ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §6 last paragraph), but the symmetric assertion lives with C10.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:725:++**Statement.** A signature-verified kind:5 event from author X referencing event ids `[e1, e2, ...]` and/or replaceable coordinates `[a1, a2, ...]` removes any matching events the store holds that are *authored by X*; the deletions persist as tombstones so the same events cannot be re-inserted later.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:737:++3. Re-inserts `e1` (simulating a later relay redelivery); asserts the store rejects it and the timeline payload does not re-emit.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:977:++- **NIP-77 sync vs live REQ split.** C10 in `sync.md` covers the sync side; C8 covers the live tail only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:987:++# Framework Magic §C9–§C10 — Sync, Provenance, Watermarks
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1014:++## C10. Watermarks gate backfill; cache miss becomes authoritative; NIP-77 is the default
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1032:++**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M4]**. M4 is the NIP-77 milestone (per `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` v1 ladder). The watermark *schema* lands earlier in M3 (`docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md`); M4 lands the engine that reads/writes them and the capability negotiation. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M4 sync engine"]`. Sub-path 2 (authoritative-miss given a populated watermark) is the structural assertion that does not require NIP-77 — it could be flipped to non-ignored as soon as M3 lands the schema and a stub engine.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1036:++C9 is **what** the store remembers about each event's provenance. C10 is **what** the store remembers about each `(filter, relay)` pair's coverage. Together they answer the question `ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §9.8 raises: *"Having an event in the local store does not prove that a view is complete."* C9 is "we have this event"; C10 is "we have everything matching this filter from this relay up to this timestamp." The framework needs both to render correctly without fetching needlessly.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1126:++    reconciler_log:   Vec<AppUpdate>,             // every AppUpdate emitted across the FFI seam
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1168:++`WireFrameAuditLog` is a new `nmp-testing` primitive that captures every outbound frame the relay-worker emits. The M2 design has an audit log on the planner side; this harness has it on the wire side — both must agree, and a separate harness invariant could later assert that agreement.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1244:++The M2 `PlannerHarness` (`subscription-compilation/tests.md` §9.3) is deliberately scoped to the planner subsystem: it tests the compile function in isolation, with no actor, no FFI seam, no storage backend. Several framework-magic bullets (C7 publish flow, C11 onboarding, C12 account switch, C13 in-place refinement) require the full actor and the full FFI emission path. The `ContractHarness` is therefore a strict superset.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1254:++| M4 | C10 (full sync engine) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1277:+ - `grep -RnE '// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO' ios/NmpPodcast/Views/ | wc -l` is the work-remaining counter for Step 7.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1325:+ | 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1473:++- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1476:++This composition pattern is the actual deliverable for NMP. Reproducing the kind:3 special case alone misses the point — what NMP needs is the **mixin + share() + claim() + switchMap-into-models** substrate that allows users to write `MailboxesModel`, `OutboxModel`, and ten future models without each one wiring up its own insert/remove/dedup/share machinery.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1544:++**Reading `signers/password-signer.ts:39-74`** (no specific bugfix commit, design observation). `requestUnlock()` creates a deferred and stores it, but `unlock()` doesn't resolve it. Instead, the next call to a method that called `requestUnlock()` re-checks `if (this.key) return`, which now returns immediately. This works because all signing methods `await this.requestUnlock()` *then* re-read `this.key`, but it's a subtle invariant — if a future refactor caches `unlockPromise` and awaits it without re-checking `this.key`, the queued operations will hang forever.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1776:++1. **B1 (outbox-by-default on publish)** — it's the spec's strongest differentiator from Applesauce and not free to bolt on later because every publish call site would need rewriting.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1868:++The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2133:++Without a cache: sub A saw event X; sub B opened later requesting X; relay sent X; was dropped as "already seen" globally. Fix: removed global early-return; per-sub `eventFirstSeen` is the dedup. **Implication**: cache is no longer required for sub-overlap correctness.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2484:++Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2514:++`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2518:++`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2764:++Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2860:++The `ndk.signer = ...` setter (in `core/src/ndk/index.ts`) has an **async side effect** that queues a promise to resolve `activeUser`. This was the root of the logout race (`7d25cd76`): subscription handlers re-setting `ndk.signer = undefined` correctly, but a pending promise from a stale set would resolve later and re-set `activeUser`. Fixed by clearing NDK state *before* triggering subscriptions during `removeSession`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2945:++- `at-most` — send no later than N ms; collect what's there.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3164:+5. TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking into prose or code?
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3299:+/bin/zsh -lc 'git diff --name-only edc17b0~..51120cb | xargs rg -n "TODO|FIXME|unimplemented|TBD-from-research|pending agent landing|proposed|M_n|NIP-XX"' in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3321:+docs/design/podcast/copy.md:156:- The placeholder block is fenced by `// MARK: NMP-WIRE` (start) and either `// MARK: NMP-WIRE — wired` (after wiring) or `// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO` (still pending).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3322:+docs/design/podcast/copy.md:158:- `grep -RnE '// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO' ios/NmpPodcast/Views/` is part of the M11 exit gate (must be zero).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3323:+docs/design/podcast/copy.md:167:// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3324:+docs/design/podcast/copy.md:281:- **`// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO` blocks are the only allowed change** to a view file before it is wired. Any other source change requires an explicit ADR + a parity-screenshots.md note with reviewer sign-off.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3325:+docs/design/podcast/copy.md:288:- `grep -RnE '// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO' ios/NmpPodcast/Views/ | wc -l` is the work-remaining counter for Step 7.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3327:+docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3330:+docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md:138:- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3331:+docs/research/ndk/outbox.md:98:Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3332:+docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:36:Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3333:+docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:66:`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3334:+docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:70:`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3335:+docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md:84:The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3478:+docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:110:`WireFrameAuditLog` is a new `nmp-testing` primitive that captures every outbound frame the relay-worker emits. The M2 design has an audit log on the planner side; this harness has it on the wire side — both must agree, and a separate harness invariant could later assert that agreement.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3481:+docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:186:The M2 `PlannerHarness` (`subscription-compilation/tests.md` §9.3) is deliberately scoped to the planner subsystem: it tests the compile function in isolation, with no actor, no FFI seam, no storage backend. Several framework-magic bullets (C7 publish flow, C11 onboarding, C12 account switch, C13 in-place refinement) require the full actor and the full FFI emission path. The `ContractHarness` is therefore a strict superset.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3526:+    38	- **Empirical iOS proof before podcast rebuild (M10.5 gate).** No "we'll wire it up later" — the FFI surface ships rock-solid (stress harness + simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI fleet + Instruments leaks audit + iPhone-12 baseline) before any line of M11 code is written.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3590:+    18	- [Sync & provenance — watermarks, NIP-77 backfill, redelivery merge](framework-magic/sync.md) (C9, C10)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3608:+    36	| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3611:+    39	| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3688:+    27	| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3723:+docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3790:+    43	Apps built with this framework **never withhold cached data and never block on fetches**. Every view payload field carries a value, not a "loading" status. Missing display names default to a shortened npub; missing pictures default to a deterministic identicon URI; missing timestamps default to "now". When a more authoritative value (e.g., the author's kind:0) arrives later, the view payload updates in place and the affected cell re-renders. The UI never sees a spinner gating already-renderable content.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3805:+    58	This is not a product feature you opt into later; it is a subscription policy built on explicit coverage metadata. See §7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3873:+    52	**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]**. M2 lands the `PublishPlanner` trait + `Nip65PublishPlanner` + the `PublishWithOverride` action (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` §7.1, §7.2, §7.4). M6 lands `SendNoteAction` as the first concrete consumer. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M2 planner + M6 first consumer"]`. Sub-paths 3 and 4 of the test exercise the planner in isolation (M2-completable); 1, 2, and 5 require M6's action consumer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3889:+    68	- NIP-77 sync routing — that's C10 in `sync.md`. Sync and live REQ should share relay policy (`ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §6 last paragraph), but the symmetric assertion lives with C10.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3939:+    45	- **NIP-77 sync vs live REQ split.** C10 in `sync.md` covers the sync side; C8 covers the live tail only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3947:+     1	# Framework Magic §C9–§C10 — Sync, Provenance, Watermarks
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3974:+    28	## C10. Watermarks gate backfill; cache miss becomes authoritative; NIP-77 is the default
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3992:+    46	**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M4]**. M4 is the NIP-77 milestone (per `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` v1 ladder). The watermark *schema* lands earlier in M3 (`docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md`); M4 lands the engine that reads/writes them and the capability negotiation. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M4 sync engine"]`. Sub-path 2 (authoritative-miss given a populated watermark) is the structural assertion that does not require NIP-77 — it could be flipped to non-ignored as soon as M3 lands the schema and a stub engine.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:3996:+    50	C9 is **what** the store remembers about each event's provenance. C10 is **what** the store remembers about each `(filter, relay)` pair's coverage. Together they answer the question `ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §9.8 raises: *"Having an event in the local store does not prove that a view is complete."* C9 is "we have this event"; C10 is "we have everything matching this filter from this relay up to this timestamp." The framework needs both to render correctly without fetching needlessly.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:4131:+    58	This is not a product feature you opt into later; it is a subscription policy built on explicit coverage metadata. See §7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:4340:+    20	| Kind 5 (delete) | After verification, scan referenced `e` and `a` tags and remove matching events authored by the deleter. Persisted as tombstone so later re-insertion is suppressed. Tombstone timestamp is the maximum delete timestamp observed for that target. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:4394:+    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:4628:+    42	**Statement.** A signature-verified kind:5 event from author X referencing event ids `[e1, e2, ...]` and/or replaceable coordinates `[a1, a2, ...]` removes any matching events the store holds that are *authored by X*; the deletions persist as tombstones so the same events cannot be re-inserted later.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:4640:+    54	3. Re-inserts `e1` (simulating a later relay redelivery); asserts the store rejects it and the timeline payload does not re-emit.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:4703:+    10	**Statement.** Every display-bearing field of every view payload is **non-`Option`** and carries either an authoritative value or a defined placeholder. When the authoritative value later arrives — a kind:0 for an author, kind:9735 zap receipts for a note, the decrypted body for a DM — the same payload re-emits with the field updated in place. The platform's reactive primitive (`@Observable` / `Flow` / signals) sees the change and only the affected cell re-renders. **No spinner ever gates an already-rendered cell, and no view module ever exposes a `loading: bool` to the platform.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5213:+docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:52:**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]**. M2 lands the `PublishPlanner` trait + `Nip65PublishPlanner` + the `PublishWithOverride` action (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` §7.1, §7.2, §7.4). M6 lands `SendNoteAction` as the first concrete consumer. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M2 planner + M6 first consumer"]`. Sub-paths 3 and 4 of the test exercise the planner in isolation (M2-completable); 1, 2, and 5 require M6's action consumer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5241:+docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:46:**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M4]**. M4 is the NIP-77 milestone (per `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` v1 ladder). The watermark *schema* lands earlier in M3 (`docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md`); M4 lands the engine that reads/writes them and the capability negotiation. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M4 sync engine"]`. Sub-path 2 (authoritative-miss given a populated watermark) is the structural assertion that does not require NIP-77 — it could be flipped to non-ignored as soon as M3 lands the schema and a stub engine.

Persisted as tombstone so later re-insertion is suppressed. Tombstone timestamp is the maximum delete timestamp observed for that target. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5625:+   138	- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5628:+   141	This composition pattern is the actual deliverable for NMP. Reproducing the kind:3 special case alone misses the point — what NMP needs is the **mixin + share() + claim() + switchMap-into-models** substrate that allows users to write `MailboxesModel`, `OutboxModel`, and ten future models without each one wiring up its own insert/remove/dedup/share machinery.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5816:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3262:docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md:138:- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5817:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3263:docs/research/ndk/outbox.md:98:Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5818:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3264:docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:36:Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5819:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3265:docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:66:`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5820:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3266:docs/research/ndk/missing-features-for-nmp.md:70:`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5821:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3267:docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md:84:The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5839:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5557:   138	- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5843:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5652:- TODO/FIXME: no real code TODO/FIXME/unimplemented found in changed code. The problem markers are the stale `TBD-from-research(...)` entries; TODO mentions in research are quoted upstream facts.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5927:    43	Apps built with this framework **never withhold cached data and never block on fetches**. Every view payload field carries a value, not a "loading" status. Missing display names default to a shortened npub; missing pictures default to a deterministic identicon URI; missing timestamps default to "now". When a more authoritative value (e.g., the author's kind:0) arrives later, the view payload updates in place and the affected cell re-renders. The UI never sees a spinner gating already-renderable content.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5942:    58	This is not a product feature you opt into later; it is a subscription policy built on explicit coverage metadata. See §7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:5989:    40	- **Empirical iOS proof before podcast rebuild (M10.5 gate).** No "we'll wire it up later" — the FFI surface ships rock-solid (stress harness + simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI fleet + Instruments leaks audit + iPhone-12 baseline) before any line of M11 code is written.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:6095:docs/aim.md:177:The repository is a Cargo workspace plus per-platform shells. The layout below is the long-term workspace shape. v1 publishes only the kernel subset described in [`docs/plan.md`](plan.md); product crates remain placeholders or later milestones until the kernel proves its invariants.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:6110:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:153:Atomicity invariant: an action's local event-store commit, side-effect intent, and ledger transition happen as actor messages with one parent action id. The action future runs on the tokio runtime, but all state mutation happens in `handle_message`. There is no public API that lets a developer publish, upload, sign, or issue an NWC request without a renderable action-ledger row.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:6114:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:295:`nmp-wallet` is staged. NIP-47 Wallet Connect lands first because it is a bounded client protocol over the action ledger and secret capabilities. Cashu/NIP-60 and nutzaps/NIP-61 come later because they require a durable wallet state machine, mint metadata cache, proof/token indexes, redemption state, and repair flows.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7494:docs/plan/m10.5-ffi-hardening.md:7:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:1958:++**Recommendation:** Add inline safety comment for documentation and future maintainability:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2044:++**Issue:** The `#[allow(unreachable_patterns)]` suppresses a warning for the final catch-all arm. This can occur if `tungstenite::MaybeTlsStream` enum variants change between versions. The intent is clear (defense against future enum variants), but the rationale is not documented in code.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2050:++// Stream type may have additional TLS variants in future tungstenite versions
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2257:++| 3 unsafe blocks in ffi.rs (F1, F2, F3) | Documentation gap | Standard FFI pattern; pointers validated by caller contract. Safety comments recommended for future audits but code is correct. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2270:++These are **not bugs**; the code is correct. Adding safety comments improves auditability and prevents future misclassification:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2275:++   - Impact: Documents FFI contract; improves future audits
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2285:++   - Recommended text: `// Stream type may have additional TLS variants in future tungstenite versions`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2304:++| Blocking debt markers (TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK/panic!/unimplemented!/todo!) | 0 | ✓ Clean |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2322:++**Recommendation:** The 3 safety comments in ffi.rs are optional but recommended for auditability and preventing future misclassification.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2359:++> **Each milestone is gated.** Every milestone ends with: a runnable artifact, automated tests in `nmp-testing`, a measured-numbers report in `docs/perf/m<N>/`, and an explicit ADR if a design decision was revised in flight. **No silent endings.** **No "for later" carve-outs** — if a slice is in the milestone scope, it ships in that milestone, or the milestone is not done.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2386:++**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2404:++- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

This can occur if `tungstenite::MaybeTlsStream` enum variants change between versions. The intent is clear (defense against future enum variants), but the rationale is not documented in code.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2629:+docs/perf/m10.5/debt-inventory.md:424:**Recommendation:** The 3 safety comments in ffi.rs are optional but recommended for auditability and preventing future misclassification.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2630:+docs/plan.md:7:> **Each milestone is gated.** Every milestone ends with: a runnable artifact, automated tests in `nmp-testing`, a measured-numbers report in `docs/perf/m<N>/`, and an explicit ADR if a design decision was revised in flight. **No silent endings.** **No "for later" carve-outs** — if a slice is in the milestone scope, it ships in that milestone, or the milestone is not done.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2631:+docs/plan.md:21:- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2632:+docs/plan.md:64:3. **Capability layering is strict.** Each milestone adds exactly one new architectural ingredient on top of the previous demo. No "we'll wire it up later" — wiring is the milestone.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2633:+docs/plan.md:77:**Demo product:** `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture` — a TODO list app driven by the kernel substrate with no Nostr concepts in it.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2635:+docs/plan.md:345:**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2637:+docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:2643:+The first pass found two concrete review axes already: a new docs file over the 300-line soft budget, and the debt inventory treating known documentation gaps as optional despite the new “no debt / no for-later carve-outs” language. I’m checking the exact sections now so the final review can be line-specific.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3162:+- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3176:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance, (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking in, (3) test coverage where tests should exist, (4) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?), (5) docs internal consistency (M10.5 gate, M11 pod-NIP language, etc), (6) hidden shortcuts. Be terse. If fine, say so. If concern, name file:line + fix.

This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3481:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:852:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Each pre-existing one is either resolved or has an ADR justifying the deferral with a tracking issue.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3520:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1159:13:+> **Each milestone is gated.** Every milestone ends with: a runnable artifact, automated tests in `nmp-testing`, a measured-numbers report in `docs/perf/m<N>/`, and an explicit ADR if a design decision was revised in flight. **No silent endings.** **No "for later" carve-outs** — if a slice is in the milestone scope, it ships in that milestone, or the milestone is not done.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3521:docs/perf/codex-reviews/2026-05-18-session-1.md:1160:40:+**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:3990:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:74:Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4003:     7	> **Each milestone is gated.** Every milestone ends with: a runnable artifact, automated tests in `nmp-testing`, a measured-numbers report in `docs/perf/m<N>/`, and an explicit ADR if a design decision was revised in flight. **No silent endings.** **No "for later" carve-outs** — if a slice is in the milestone scope, it ships in that milestone, or the milestone is not done.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4066:    43	Apps built with this framework **never withhold cached data and never block on fetches**. Every view payload field carries a value, not a "loading" status. Missing display names default to a shortened npub; missing pictures default to a deterministic identicon URI; missing timestamps default to "now". When a more authoritative value (e.g., the author's kind:0) arrives later, the view payload updates in place and the affected cell re-renders. The UI never sees a spinner gating already-renderable content.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/fcf8b43.md:4081:    58	This is not a product feature you opt into later; it is a subscription policy built on explicit coverage metadata. See §7.8.
