docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:4601:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0a10e42.md:341:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:29:| 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:6257:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2271: | 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:7190:docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:1119:The partition split has exactly the requested six files and all are under 300 LOC. I also found one `TODO` in the touched S4 stress file, so I’m checking whether it predates this wave before treating it as a finding.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:7207:docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:1269:crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:221:    // docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4 TODO).  Gate omitted this phase.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:7217:docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:1296:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:29:| 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:7235:docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:1362:crates/nmp-testing/bin/ffi-stress/s4_reconciler_backpressure.rs:221:    // docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md §G-S4 TODO).  Gate omitted this phase.

Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/f47a44d.md:7587:docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:744:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:9:| 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:112:+- **Empirical iOS proof before podcast rebuild (M10.5 gate).** No "we'll wire it up later" — the FFI surface ships rock-solid (stress harness + simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI fleet + Instruments leaks audit + iPhone-12 baseline) before any line of M11 code is written.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:168:+| `apps/` | Generated per-app crates (`apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`, future `apps/twitter/nmp-app-twitter`, `apps/podcast/nmp-app-podcast`, `apps/hl/nmp-app-hl`). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:207:+- [Sync & provenance — watermarks, NIP-77 backfill, redelivery merge](framework-magic/sync.md) (C9, C10)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:225:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:228:+| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:299:+**Statement.** Every display-bearing field of every view payload is **non-`Option`** and carries either an authoritative value or a defined placeholder. When the authoritative value later arrives — a kind:0 for an author, kind:9735 zap receipts for a note, the decrypted body for a DM — the same payload re-emits with the field updated in place. The platform's reactive primitive (`@Observable` / `Flow` / signals) sees the change and only the affected cell re-renders. **No spinner ever gates an already-rendered cell, and no view module ever exposes a `loading: bool` to the platform.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:393:+| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:531:+- **Mute-list changes (kind:10000).** The mute list is structurally analogous, but the user's scope-adjustments doc explicitly names kind:3. Mute-list auto-tracking would be a C5-shaped sibling bullet (potential C14 future addition); not in the v1 contract surface.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:592:+**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]**. M2 lands the `PublishPlanner` trait + `Nip65PublishPlanner` + the `PublishWithOverride` action (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` §7.1, §7.2, §7.4). M6 lands `SendNoteAction` as the first concrete consumer. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M2 planner + M6 first consumer"]`. Sub-paths 3 and 4 of the test exercise the planner in isolation (M2-completable); 1, 2, and 5 require M6's action consumer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:608:+- NIP-77 sync routing — that's C10 in `sync.md`. Sync and live REQ should share relay policy (`ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §6 last paragraph), but the symmetric assertion lives with C10.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:657:+**Statement.** A signature-verified kind:5 event from author X referencing event ids `[e1, e2, ...]` and/or replaceable coordinates `[a1, a2, ...]` removes any matching events the store holds that are *authored by X*; the deletions persist as tombstones so the same events cannot be re-inserted later.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:669:+3. Re-inserts `e1` (simulating a later relay redelivery); asserts the store rejects it and the timeline payload does not re-emit.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:909:+- **NIP-77 sync vs live REQ split.** C10 in `sync.md` covers the sync side; C8 covers the live tail only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:919:+# Framework Magic §C9–§C10 — Sync, Provenance, Watermarks
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:946:+## C10. Watermarks gate backfill; cache miss becomes authoritative; NIP-77 is the default
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:964:+**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M4]**. M4 is the NIP-77 milestone (per `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` v1 ladder). The watermark *schema* lands earlier in M3 (`docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md`); M4 lands the engine that reads/writes them and the capability negotiation. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M4 sync engine"]`. Sub-path 2 (authoritative-miss given a populated watermark) is the structural assertion that does not require NIP-77 — it could be flipped to non-ignored as soon as M3 lands the schema and a stub engine.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:968:+C9 is **what** the store remembers about each event's provenance. C10 is **what** the store remembers about each `(filter, relay)` pair's coverage. Together they answer the question `ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §9.8 raises: *"Having an event in the local store does not prove that a view is complete."* C9 is "we have this event"; C10 is "we have everything matching this filter from this relay up to this timestamp." The framework needs both to render correctly without fetching needlessly.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1058:+    reconciler_log:   Vec<AppUpdate>,             // every AppUpdate emitted across the FFI seam
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1100:+`WireFrameAuditLog` is a new `nmp-testing` primitive that captures every outbound frame the relay-worker emits. The M2 design has an audit log on the planner side; this harness has it on the wire side — both must agree, and a separate harness invariant could later assert that agreement.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1176:+The M2 `PlannerHarness` (`subscription-compilation/tests.md` §9.3) is deliberately scoped to the planner subsystem: it tests the compile function in isolation, with no actor, no FFI seam, no storage backend. Several framework-magic bullets (C7 publish flow, C11 onboarding, C12 account switch, C13 in-place refinement) require the full actor and the full FFI emission path. The `ContractHarness` is therefore a strict superset.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1186:+| M4 | C10 (full sync engine) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1209: - `grep -RnE '// MARK: NMP-WIRE — TODO' ios/NmpPodcast/Views/ | wc -l` is the work-remaining counter for Step 7.

Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1405:+- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1408:+This composition pattern is the actual deliverable for NMP. Reproducing the kind:3 special case alone misses the point — what NMP needs is the **mixin + share() + claim() + switchMap-into-models** substrate that allows users to write `MailboxesModel`, `OutboxModel`, and ten future models without each one wiring up its own insert/remove/dedup/share machinery.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1476:+**Reading `signers/password-signer.ts:39-74`** (no specific bugfix commit, design observation). `requestUnlock()` creates a deferred and stores it, but `unlock()` doesn't resolve it. Instead, the next call to a method that called `requestUnlock()` re-checks `if (this.key) return`, which now returns immediately. This works because all signing methods `await this.requestUnlock()` *then* re-read `this.key`, but it's a subtle invariant — if a future refactor caches `unlockPromise` and awaits it without re-checking `this.key`, the queued operations will hang forever.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1708:+1. **B1 (outbox-by-default on publish)** — it's the spec's strongest differentiator from Applesauce and not free to bolt on later because every publish call site would need rewriting.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1800:+The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2065:+Without a cache: sub A saw event X; sub B opened later requesting X; relay sent X; was dropped as "already seen" globally. Fix: removed global early-return; per-sub `eventFirstSeen` is the dedup. **Implication**: cache is no longer required for sub-overlap correctness.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2416:+Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2446:+`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2450:+`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2696:+Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2792:+The `ndk.signer = ...` setter (in `core/src/ndk/index.ts`) has an **async side effect** that queues a promise to resolve `activeUser`. This was the root of the logout race (`7d25cd76`): subscription handlers re-setting `ndk.signer = undefined` correctly, but a pending promise from a stale set would resolve later and re-set `activeUser`. Fixed by clearing NDK state *before* triggering subscriptions during `removeSession`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2877:+- `at-most` — send no later than N ms; collect what's there.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3096:5. TODO/FIXME/unimplemented sneaking into prose or code?
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3231:/bin/zsh -lc 'git diff --name-only edc17b0~..51120cb | xargs rg -n "TODO|FIXME|unimplemented|TBD-from-research|pending agent landing|proposed|M_n|NIP-XX"' in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3722:    43	Apps built with this framework **never withhold cached data and never block on fetches**. Every view payload field carries a value, not a "loading" status. Missing display names default to a shortened npub; missing pictures default to a deterministic identicon URI; missing timestamps default to "now". When a more authoritative value (e.g., the author's kind:0) arrives later, the view payload updates in place and the affected cell re-renders. The UI never sees a spinner gating already-renderable content.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3737:    58	This is not a product feature you opt into later; it is a subscription policy built on explicit coverage metadata. See §7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3805:    52	**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]**. M2 lands the `PublishPlanner` trait + `Nip65PublishPlanner` + the `PublishWithOverride` action (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` §7.1, §7.2, §7.4). M6 lands `SendNoteAction` as the first concrete consumer. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M2 planner + M6 first consumer"]`. Sub-paths 3 and 4 of the test exercise the planner in isolation (M2-completable); 1, 2, and 5 require M6's action consumer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3821:    68	- NIP-77 sync routing — that's C10 in `sync.md`. Sync and live REQ should share relay policy (`ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §6 last paragraph), but the symmetric assertion lives with C10.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3871:    45	- **NIP-77 sync vs live REQ split.** C10 in `sync.md` covers the sync side; C8 covers the live tail only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3879:     1	# Framework Magic §C9–§C10 — Sync, Provenance, Watermarks
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3906:    28	## C10. Watermarks gate backfill; cache miss becomes authoritative; NIP-77 is the default
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3924:    46	**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M4]**. M4 is the NIP-77 milestone (per `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` v1 ladder). The watermark *schema* lands earlier in M3 (`docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md`); M4 lands the engine that reads/writes them and the capability negotiation. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M4 sync engine"]`. Sub-path 2 (authoritative-miss given a populated watermark) is the structural assertion that does not require NIP-77 — it could be flipped to non-ignored as soon as M3 lands the schema and a stub engine.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3928:    50	C9 is **what** the store remembers about each event's provenance. C10 is **what** the store remembers about each `(filter, relay)` pair's coverage. Together they answer the question `ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §9.8 raises: *"Having an event in the local store does not prove that a view is complete."* C9 is "we have this event"; C10 is "we have everything matching this filter from this relay up to this timestamp." The framework needs both to render correctly without fetching needlessly.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4063:    58	This is not a product feature you opt into later; it is a subscription policy built on explicit coverage metadata. See §7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4272:    20	| Kind 5 (delete) | After verification, scan referenced `e` and `a` tags and remove matching events authored by the deleter. Persisted as tombstone so later re-insertion is suppressed. Tombstone timestamp is the maximum delete timestamp observed for that target. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4326:    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4560:    42	**Statement.** A signature-verified kind:5 event from author X referencing event ids `[e1, e2, ...]` and/or replaceable coordinates `[a1, a2, ...]` removes any matching events the store holds that are *authored by X*; the deletions persist as tombstones so the same events cannot be re-inserted later.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4572:    54	3. Re-inserts `e1` (simulating a later relay redelivery); asserts the store rejects it and the timeline payload does not re-emit.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4635:    10	**Statement.** Every display-bearing field of every view payload is **non-`Option`** and carries either an authoritative value or a defined placeholder. When the authoritative value later arrives — a kind:0 for an author, kind:9735 zap receipts for a note, the decrypted body for a DM — the same payload re-emits with the field updated in place. The platform's reactive primitive (`@Observable` / `Flow` / signals) sees the change and only the affected cell re-renders. **No spinner ever gates an already-rendered cell, and no view module ever exposes a `loading: bool` to the platform.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5145:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:52:**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]**. M2 lands the `PublishPlanner` trait + `Nip65PublishPlanner` + the `PublishWithOverride` action (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md` §7.1, §7.2, §7.4). M6 lands `SendNoteAction` as the first concrete consumer. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M2 planner + M6 first consumer"]`. Sub-paths 3 and 4 of the test exercise the planner in isolation (M2-completable); 1, 2, and 5 require M6's action consumer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5173:docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:46:**Milestone owner:** **[PENDING M4]**. M4 is the NIP-77 milestone (per `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` v1 ladder). The watermark *schema* lands earlier in M3 (`docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md`); M4 lands the engine that reads/writes them and the capability negotiation. Test checked in as `#[ignore = "pending M4 sync engine"]`. Sub-path 2 (authoritative-miss given a populated watermark) is the structural assertion that does not require NIP-77 — it could be flipped to non-ignored as soon as M3 lands the schema and a stub engine.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5247:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:20:| Kind 5 (delete) | After verification, scan referenced `e` and `a` tags and remove matching events authored by the deleter. Persisted as tombstone so later re-insertion is suppressed. Tombstone timestamp is the maximum delete timestamp observed for that target. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5254:docs/design/framework-magic/replaceable.md:42:**Statement.** A signature-verified kind:5 event from author X referencing event ids `[e1, e2, ...]` and/or replaceable coordinates `[a1, a2, ...]` removes any matching events the store holds that are *authored by X*; the deletions persist as tombstones so the same events cannot be re-inserted later.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5308:    42	**Statement.** A signature-verified kind:5 event from author X referencing event ids `[e1, e2, ...]` and/or replaceable coordinates `[a1, a2, ...]` removes any matching events the store holds that are *authored by X*; the deletions persist as tombstones so the same events cannot be re-inserted later.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5320:    54	3. Re-inserts `e1` (simulating a later relay redelivery); asserts the store rejects it and the timeline payload does not re-emit.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5557:   138	- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:5560:   141	This composition pattern is the actual deliverable for NMP. Reproducing the kind:3 special case alone misses the point — what NMP needs is the **mixin + share() + claim() + switchMap-into-models** substrate that allows users to write `MailboxesModel`, `OutboxModel`, and ten future models without each one wiring up its own insert/remove/dedup/share machinery.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:1296:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:29:| 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/942034d.md:2421:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8660961.md:2271: | 2026-05-18 07:22 | 19 | Heartbeat #19 (autonomous). DOUBLE LANDING: T29 m3-followup-1 (7d16b3f) + T30 m2-followup-1 (bc84cfe) both pushed CLEAN via HEAD:master refspec (7th+8th agents on protocol). T29: mem.rs split (1067→357 LOC max), types.rs split, D4 ingest wired (with M1 firehose-bench live PASS evidence), VerifiedEvent + nostr crate added, GC ceilings (per-view 1000, global 20000), kind:5 max-merge. T30: Rule 1 wildcard correctness bug FIXED + test, plan-id hashes only referenced pubkeys + scope + version inputs (3 stability tests), RoutingSource::Indexer removed (→ UserConfigured/Indexer), direction table extended for #p/active-account-read/IndexerProbe, audit assertion 300→1000, mod.rs minimal exports, lattice.rs split per-rule, TODO removed. Codex on both flagged 8 round-2 issues (5 T29 + 3 T30): T29 P1 ingest pre-insert-return drops provenance, P1 only kinds 1|6 go through store (kinds 0/3/10002 still bypass), P2 GC intra-call BTreeSet missing, P2 release() leaves claim_budgets, P3 ID-prefix panic; T30 P1 partition.rs Case A returns before Case C (loses #p inbox routing), P2 inbox uses only read_relays not ∪ both_relays, P2 #p indexer fallback breaks structural ban → T32 + T33 filed. T31 m105-followup-1 dispatched now that T29 brought VerifiedEvent (S3/S4/S5 event-pressure injection unblocked). 3 agents in flight: T31, T32 + T33 pending. Disk 96%. |

 succeeded in 0ms:
   748	- Symptom: cache query results look plausible but are semantically too broad. Cause: trusting native NostrDB filtering without reapplying the canonical Swift matcher. Fix: treat native queries as candidate generation and run `NDKFilter.matches(event:)` before returning results, including the in-memory fallback path [Task 2]
   749	- Symptom: small boundary bugs hide behind a clean build. Cause: off-by-one or `limit <= 0` behavior was never exercised directly. Fix: add explicit regression tests for zero-limit and first-result counting semantics instead of assuming search wrappers get limits right [Task 3]
   750	- Symptom: a relay subscription intermittently misses fast early events/EOSE even though connectivity looks fine. Cause: `applyRelayStrategy()` started the relay work before the event callbacks were installed. Fix: wire `setOnEvent` / `setOnEOSE` / `setOnRelayAdded` first, then apply the relay strategy, and validate with a real suite like `EOSETrackerTests` rather than a source-file-name filter [Task 4]

   777	## User preferences
   778	
   779	- when the user asked how to implement "nip-77, outbox, subscription compilation (like NDK does), etc" and then followed with "on what crate?" -> answer from the actual repo docs and current workspace boundary, not from a generic protocol overview [Task 1]
   780	- when the user asked to "review NDK and applesauce codebases via agents and then explain how we should implement outbox support in NMP" -> do codebase-grounded synthesis and preserve it in a durable note, not an ephemeral architectural riff [Task 2]
   781	- when the user later said "extend your investigation and document with how we should consider the design of nip-77, subscription compilation, etc" -> expand the same note instead of scattering the answer across separate one-off explanations [Task 2]
   782	
   783	## Reusable knowledge
   784	
   785	- the current workspace only contains `crates/nmp-core` and `crates/nmp-testing`; `nmp-sync`, `nmp-views`, and `nmp-actions` are still target-architecture names from `docs/product-spec.md`, not present crates in this checkout [Task 1]
   786	- the immediate implementation answer is to keep planner, outbox, sync, canonical matching, relay pool, and diagnostics as internal modules under `nmp-core`, with `nmp-testing` holding regression scenarios and mock-relay coverage [Task 1]
   787	- the repo docs already define the intended split: `docs/product-spec.md` says the planner owns `ViewSpec -> filters -> relay URLs -> on-the-wire REQ`, outbox is NIP-65 relay routing, and NIP-77 is the preferred historical backfill mechanism with `(filter, relay)` watermarks [Task 1]
   788	- NDK and Applesauce converge on the same durable pattern for NMP: live subscription first, background negentropy reconciliation second, fallback fetch when capability support is absent, and relay grouping/filter compilation as planner policy rather than socket-level behavior [Task 2]
   789	- outbox support should live in the actor/planner layer as durable domain policy; relay metadata, seen-relay provenance, and coverage should be stored as state, while the safe public API stays intent-based (`open a timeline`, `publish a reply`, `update relay list`) [Task 2]
   790	- `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` is the committed note that now captures the durable synthesis, including view-window loading/pagination guidance and the distinction between cache coverage and cache presence [Task 2]
   791	
   792	## Failures and how to do differently
   793	
   794	- Symptom: an architecture answer starts talking about crates that do not exist in the checkout. Cause: treating long-term spec tables as present-day workspace reality. Fix: answer in two layers, "what exists now" and "what can be extracted later" [Task 1]
   795	- Symptom: NDK/Applesauce review turns noisy and low-signal. Cause: broad searches across the whole external repo swamp the relevant relay/sync/planner surfaces. Fix: narrow early to NDK `sync/`, `core/src/subscription/`, `core/src/relay/`, and Applesauce `packages/relay/` plus timeline-loader/filter helpers [Task 2]
   796	- Symptom: current `cargo check -p nmp-core` failures tempt overclaiming or derail the design review. Cause: unrelated prototype compile errors are not evidence about the design note. Fix: treat the note as read-only synthesis unless the task is explicitly to implement/fix NMP code [Task 2]

 succeeded in 0ms:
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:4:> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1 (provenance + watermarks), §7.8 (sync engine); `docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md` (storage); `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §6 (NIP-77 lessons), §9.8 (coverage ≠ cache presence).
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:10:**Framework does:** the dedup-with-provenance-merge rule at `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1 row "Duplicate id". Storage of provenance sidecars at `docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md` (the 32-distinct-relay-per-event bound is set there). The "primary relay" selection is a deterministic function of the first observer, used for cache locality and diagnostics.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:24:7. Run 33 more relay deliveries of `e1` from distinct relay URLs; assert the provenance set caps at 32 entries per the `docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md` bound, with the **primary** entry preserved as the anchor.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:28:## C10. Watermarks gate backfill; cache miss becomes authoritative; NIP-77 is the default
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:30:**Statement.** Every `(filter, relay)` pair the framework reads from has a durable **sync watermark** recording how far back coverage has been reconciled. Before issuing any historical REQ, the planner consults the watermark: a fully-synced pair serves cache-misses as **authoritative** ("this event does not exist on that relay"); an unsynced or partially-synced pair triggers a backfill that prefers **NIP-77 negentropy** when the relay supports it, falling back to bounded REQ scan otherwise.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:32:**Framework does:** the watermark schema at `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1 (the watermarks table); the consult-before-REQ behavior at `subsystems.md` §7.2 line 62 ("Coverage-aware backfill"); the three sync triggers (foreground, view open, reconnect) at `subsystems.md` §7.8 lines 261–263; per-relay NIP-77 capability negotiation at `subsystems.md` §7.8 line 277. The watermark is durable across restart (`subsystems.md` §7.1 line 44). The authoritative-miss rule lives at `subsystems.md` §7.1 line 46: *"A cache-miss query against a fully-synced (filter, relay) pair is authoritative."*
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:36:**Failure mode prevented:** the cache-miss-disguised-as-empty bug (`product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 **bug #6**: "Cache miss returning empty without triggering a fallback fetch") and its inverse, the over-fetch bug — issuing the same historical REQ on every view open because the framework can't tell the cache is complete. Plus the bandwidth waste `ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §6 highlights: re-fetching a 10k-event historical window via REQ scan when the relay supports NIP-77 reconciliation.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:38:**Test:** `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss`. The test uses a mock relay with declared NIP-77 capability and a `SimulatedClock`:
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:40:1. **Unsynced pair → fetch.** Open `TimelineView { authors: [A], kinds: [1], since: T-1d, until: T }` against a fresh store (no watermark for this `(filter, relay)` pair). Assert the planner schedules a backfill — NIP-77 reconciliation against the mock relay (because capability negotiation succeeded). Mock relay returns a 50-event set; assert all 50 land in the store; assert the watermark for `(filter_sig, "wss://mock")` updates to `synced_up_to = T`.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:41:2. **Fully-synced pair → authoritative miss.** Close the view, re-open with the same filter, query for an event known not to exist in the response set. Assert the planner **does not issue a wire frame** (no REQ, no NIP-77); the cache-miss returns empty as authoritative; the watermark is unchanged.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:42:3. **Capability fallback.** Switch to a second mock relay that **does not** support NIP-77 (capability negotiation reports unsupported). Open the same filter against the new relay; assert the planner falls back to bounded REQ scan for that relay only; assert the first relay's plan is untouched (the fallback is per-relay).
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:43:4. **Reconnect gap-fill.** Simulate disconnect/reconnect on the mock relay after T+30s of being away; assert on reconnect, the planner re-establishes the live REQ tail (per C8 / `subsystems.md` §7.2 line 71) and schedules a NIP-77 gap fill for the disconnect window; assert the watermark updates after the gap closes.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:44:5. **`bytes_saved_vs_req` instrumentation.** Assert the cumulative counter for the synced relay is non-zero after step 1, per `subsystems.md` §7.1 watermarks-table column and §7.8 line 279.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:46:**Milestone owner:** **[LANDED M4]** (T53 follow-up). M4 shipped the NIP-77 engine and the `apply_coverage_filter` planner-side gate; the T53 follow-up wired it into `SubscriptionLifecycle::set_coverage_hook` (the `nmp-core` side of the seam) and flipped `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` to active in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`. Sub-paths 1, 2 (authoritative miss), 3 (NIP-77-capable → NegThenReq), and 4 (capability fallback → ReqSince + canonical-filter-hash refresh) are covered by the active C10 test. The reconnect gap-fill (sub-path 4 from the original spec) and the `bytes_saved_vs_req` instrumentation (sub-path 5) are exercised by the dedicated single-purpose tests in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/nip77_reconnect_resumes_from_watermark.rs` and `crates/nmp-testing/tests/nip77_cold_open_via_neg.rs`.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:50:C9 is **what** the store remembers about each event's provenance. C10 is **what** the store remembers about each `(filter, relay)` pair's coverage. Together they answer the question `ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §9.8 raises: *"Having an event in the local store does not prove that a view is complete."* C9 is "we have this event"; C10 is "we have everything matching this filter from this relay up to this timestamp." The framework needs both to render correctly without fetching needlessly.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:52:The two are paired in one chapter rather than split because their tests share the mock-relay-with-capability harness and because their failure modes intersect (a redelivered event from a new relay updates both the provenance set and the watermark, per `subsystems.md` §7.1 line 101 "Watermarks intersect with outbox").
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:56:- `docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md` for the storage schema and the 32-distinct-relay cap.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:57:- `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` for the planner's watermark consultation in the compile pipeline (the stage that consults coverage before issuing historical REQ; the compiler file names the stage).
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:58:- The "shared relay policy between sync and live REQ" lesson from `ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §6 last paragraph is implicit in the per-relay watermark — both engines key by the same `(filter_sig, relay_url)` pair, so they cannot disagree on the relay universe.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:60:**Applesauce coverage/watermark equivalent** (`docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md`): Applesauce's coverage model is implicit rather than explicit. It relies on the `eventLoader` fallback at `event-store.ts:102-104`: if a pointer is not in memory and the loader returns nothing, the miss is treated as authoritative for that event id. For timelines, `TimelineModel` does not track per-relay coverage — it trusts the store's contents. NMP's explicit `(filter_sig, relay_url)` watermark (`subsystems.md` §7.8`) is strictly more expressive: it distinguishes "relay synced up to T" from "relay has unknown coverage." The app-visible `WatermarksSummary` surface (`subsystems.md` §7.8 line 287) has no Applesauce analog — NMP adds this for diagnostic and sync-engine needs that Applesauce leaves to the app.

docs/perf/m4/negentropy.md:58:| Mixed-capability relay set: both populate the same store | `nip77_capability_negotiation.rs` | ✅ |

docs/design/framework-magic.md:36:| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:37:| C8 | Subscription planner deduplicates overlapping interests into one wire REQ per relay, auto-closes on EOSE / last-consumer-drop, and buffers ingress to ≤60Hz per view | subs.md | `c8_subscriptions_coalesce_autoclose_and_buffer` | **[PENDING M2]** | spec §7.2; §3.3 bug #2, bug #8 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:38:| C9 | Provenance preserved: same event id arriving from N relays merges into one stored event with N-entry provenance set; original `id` and signature untouched | sync.md | `c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries` | **[PENDING M3]** | aim §6 doctrine 10; spec §7.1 row "Provenance"; §3.3 bug #10 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[LANDED M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:41:| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:42:| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:46:**Test names** (13 behavior tests + 1 coverage meta-test = 14 total, all in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` — see [test-scaffolding.md](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md) for the harness):
docs/design/framework-magic.md:57:10. `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss`
docs/design/framework-magic.md:83:- `sync.md` §4 — Applesauce's watermark equivalent is the `claimLatest` / `claimEvents` refcount pair (`observable/claim-latest.ts`, `claim-events.ts`) plus the `EventMemory` LRU touch on claim (`event-memory.ts:188`). Coverage-awareness is implicit in the `eventLoader` fallback (`event-store.ts:102-104`): if the event is not in memory and the loader returns nothing, the miss is treated as authoritative for that pointer. NMP's explicit `(filter_sig, relay)` watermark is a more precise analog.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:1:# Cardinal Doctrines D0–D8
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:7:**Two kinds of doctrine.** D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines — they govern user-facing semantics (what the framework promises, what it forbids). D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* — they govern how the runtime is allowed to be implemented (what crosses FFI, how state changes propagate, what the hot path can do). Both kinds are equally binding; their distinction is the kind of review they enforce. Policy review flags "this API choice violates a user-facing principle"; substrate review flags "this implementation choice will leak across FFI / hide policy on the native side / degrade reactivity."
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:9:## D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:17:- Closed FFI enums that prevent modules from contributing typed views, actions, updates, capabilities, or identity scopes.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:21:## D1. Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:25:The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:34:## D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:36:NIP-77 negentropy reconciliation is the default backfill mechanism. Every `(filter, relay)` pair the app touches is treated as a tracked sync target with a watermark. Live REQ remains the tailing path, but historical gaps consult coverage first and prefer sync over REQ scans when relays support it.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:38:This is not a product feature you opt into later; it is a subscription policy built on explicit coverage metadata. See §7.8.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:40:## D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:50:- Silent reads against a default relay set that miss an author's actual relays; unknown relay lists surface as coverage/diagnostic state and use a bounded fallback policy.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:53:## D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:55:The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:57:## D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:59:What crosses FFI is the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store. `AppState` carries small screen-shaped data plus a map of `ViewId → ViewPayload` for views currently in use. Closing a view evicts its payload from the snapshot. The event store itself never crosses FFI. See §6.2 and the FFI architecture appendix (§A1).
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:61:## D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:63:Operational failures surface as `toast: Option<String>` state fields, never as exceptions or `Result<T, E>` across the FFI boundary. Long-running operations expose `busy` flags that clear on completion regardless of outcome. Native `dispatch` calls never need `try` / `catch`; native reconciler callbacks never receive a Rust error type.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:68:- Per-operation error-type proliferation across UniFFI.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:69:- Silent failure: every error has at least one observable state field carrying its consequences (a toast, a `busy` flag clearing, a diagnostic record per ADR-0007).
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:73:## D7. Capabilities report; never decide policy
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:75:Native bridges (Keychain, NIP-46 bunker, BGTask scheduler, `AVPlayer`, NIP-07 web extension, FilePicker, Blossom upload, etc.) execute platform APIs and report raw events back into the kernel. They never decide *whether to retry*, *whether an error is recoverable*, *which relay to publish to*, *which encryption scheme to negotiate*, *what state should become*, or *whether a duplicate request is a no-op*. Policy is Rust's; capabilities are reports.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:85:## D8. Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:87:The substrate that delivers D1, D2, D4, D5 in practice. Every inserted event participates in a composite reverse index that names the views interested in it; view recompute is bounded to ≤60 deltas per second per view; the working set is hot-resident with claim-pinned overlays. Allocations after warmup are linear in active-view count, never in cached-event count. False-wakeup rate ≤ 0.10 candidates per delta.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:89:The idle-tick emit path is gated on `kernel.changed_since_emit()` — idle ticks that produce no state change must not emit snapshots (D8 regression guard).
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:96:- A view re-rendering at greater than 60 Hz regardless of upstream burst.
docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:98:Per ADRs 0001 (composite dependency keys), 0002 (per-view delta budget), 0003 (working-set memory), and 0004 (allocation measurement), all in `docs/decisions/`. Validated continuously by `reactivity-bench` (`crates/nmp-testing/bin/reactivity-bench/`).
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:17:A Cargo workspace shipping a Nostr-native **app kernel** (`nmp-core`), reusable **Nostr protocol modules** (`nmp-nip01`, `nmp-nip17`, `nmp-nip65`, etc.), app-owned extension modules, a codegen tool (`nmp gen modules`) that produces per-app concrete FFI enums/wrappers, FFI bindings for Swift/Kotlin/TypeScript, a wasm target, a scaffolding CLI, and reference platform shells.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:19:The kernel composes the `rust-nostr` crate family plus OS capability crates into a substrate. It owns actor runtime, verified event store, subscription planner, relay routing pipeline, signer/session plumbing, durable action ledger, domain-store substrate, typed view registry, capability bridge, platform shadow/codegen machinery, diagnostics, and test harnesses.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:23:The framework treats common Nostr-correctness failures (stale replaceable events, lost subscriptions, mis-routed publishes, double-publication, multi-account desync, leaked secrets across FFI, naive cache invalidation, withheld cached data, blocking-on-fetch UI patterns) as **product defects in the framework** rather than as developer mistakes. The public API is designed so that the wrong thing is hard to type.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:27:## 1.5 Cardinal doctrines D0–D8
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:29:See [`docs/product-spec/doctrine.md`](./doctrine.md) for the full text of all nine doctrines.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:Summary: D0–D5 are *policy* doctrines (user-facing semantics); D6–D8 are *substrate invariants* (runtime implementation constraints). Both are equally binding. Every API decision answers to at least one; conflicts resolve in the order D0 > D1 > … > D8.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:35:| D0 | No app nouns in `nmp-core`; test surface gated behind `test-support` feature | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:36:| D1 | Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:37:| D2 | Negentropy first, REQ second | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:38:| D3 | Outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:39:| D4 | Single writer per fact; caches derive | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:40:| D5 | Snapshots bounded by what's open | Policy |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:41:| D6 | Errors never cross FFI as exceptions | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:42:| D7 | Capabilities report; never decide policy | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:43:| D8 | Reactivity contract: composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded; idle-tick emit gated on `changed_since_emit()` | Substrate |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:142:| Crate | Role | FFI? |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:146:| `nmp-ffi` | UniFFI building blocks used by generated app crates | UniFFI |
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:168:Generated bindings are **checked into git** under `bindings/{swift,kotlin,typescript}/`. Developers consuming the workspace as a path dependency do not need a host build to regenerate. The CI lane regenerates and diffs on every PR touching FFI-exposed types; binding drift fails the build.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:193:- Background sync via NIP-77 negentropy on foreground.
docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:252:4. `just gen-bindings` after changing FFI-visible types.

     1	//! `CompiledPlan`, `RelayPlan`, `SubShape`, and `RoutingSource` — the output
     2	//! types produced by the subscription compiler.
     3	//!
     4	//! Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3.3–§3.4
     5	//! Doctrine: D6 (planner errors are internal Results, never cross FFI).
     6	
     7	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     8	use std::collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet};
     9	use std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher;
    10	use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};
    11	
    12	use super::interest::{InterestId, InterestShape, RelayUrl};
    13	
    14	// ─── UserConfiguredCategory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15	
    16	/// Sub-category for `RoutingSource::UserConfigured`.
    17	///
    18	/// Indexer fallback is NOT a fifth diagnostic lane — it is `UserConfigured`
    19	/// with sub-category `Indexer`. This preserves the four-lane discipline
    20	/// (`docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md` §5.0 + §5.1 Lane 4)
    21	/// so the diagnostic UI always sees exactly four columns regardless of whether
    22	/// an author is served via NIP-65, hints, provenance, or any user-configured
    23	/// sub-category.
    24	///
    25	/// `ByLaneCounts::indexer_fallback` in the coverage view exposes the indexer
    26	/// sub-count WITHOUT splitting lane 4 — it is a sub-count of `user_configured`,
    27	/// not an extra lane.
    28	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Ord, PartialOrd, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    29	pub enum UserConfiguredCategory {
    30	    /// User's own read relays (overrides NIP-65 read for the active account).
    31	    AccountRead,
    32	    /// User's own write relays.
    33	    AccountWrite,
    34	    /// Kernel-configured indexer relay (e.g. purplepag.es).
    35	    ///
    36	    /// This is the sub-category that represents indexer fallback routing in
    37	    /// diagnostics — it is lane 4 (User-configured), not a fifth lane. The
    38	    /// indexer set is an operator policy choice applied by the kernel when
    39	    /// NIP-65 mailboxes are unknown. Never used for writes (D3).
    40	    Indexer,
    41	    /// Operator-injected relay for debug/testing purposes.
    42	    Debug,
    43	}
    44	
    45	// ─── RoutingSource ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46	
    47	/// Why a relay was included in the plan.
    48	///
    49	/// A relay may appear for multiple reasons simultaneously (e.g., both NIP-65
    50	/// and user-configured). `RelayPlan::role_tags` is a `BTreeSet<RoutingSource>`
    51	/// preserving all reasons — the four-lane diagnostic discipline requires that
    52	/// lanes are never collapsed.
    53	///
    54	/// **Indexer fallback** is represented as `UserConfigured(UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer)`,
    55	/// NOT as a separate variant. There are exactly four lanes in the diagnostic model
    56	/// (NIP-65 / Hint / Provenance / User-configured); the indexer is a sub-category
    57	/// of lane 4. See `docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md` §5.0.
    58	///
    59	/// Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md` §5.2
    60	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Ord, PartialOrd, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    61	pub enum RoutingSource {
    62	    /// Resolved from the author's published kind:10002 relay list (lane 1).
    63	    Nip65,
    64	    /// Resolved from a routing hint embedded in an event tag (lane 2).
    65	    Hint,
    66	    /// Observed as the provenance relay for a prior event (lane 3).
    67	    Provenance,
    68	    /// Resolved from a user-configured or operator-policy relay set (lane 4).
    69	    ///
    70	    /// Includes indexer fallback as `UserConfigured(UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer)`.
    71	    /// The sub-category is carried here so that `RelayPlan::role_tags` remains
    72	    /// self-describing without consulting a separate fact stream.
    73	    UserConfigured(UserConfiguredCategory),
    74	}
    75	
    76	// ─── SubShape ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    77	
    78	/// A single merged filter that will be emitted as one wire REQ.
    79	///
    80	/// The wire-emitter renders each `SubShape` as exactly one `["REQ", sub_id, filter]`
    81	/// frame. The `canonical_filter_hash` provides stable identity for ADR-0007
    82	/// `WireSubscriptionStatus` records across re-emissions.
    83	///
    84	/// # Wire-emitter lifecycle field
    85	/// Add `lifecycle: InterestLifecycle` to this struct when the wire-emitter lands.
    86	/// The compiler already computes lifecycle during the Stage 3 greedy merge;
    87	/// lifecycle equality is enforced by Rule 6 before any two shapes are merged.
    88	/// The wire-emitter needs lifecycle to decide whether to send CLOSE on EOSE
    89	/// (OneShot / BoundedTime) or keep the subscription open (Tailing).
    90	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    91	pub struct SubShape {
    92	    /// The canonical, post-merge filter description.
    93	    pub shape: InterestShape,
    94	    /// All logical interests whose filters were merged into this sub-shape.
    95	    pub originating_interests: Vec<InterestId>,
    96	    /// Blake3 hash of the serialised `shape` for stable wire-subscription identity.
    97	    /// Placeholder: populated by the compiler stage 4. Format: 8 hex chars.
    98	    pub canonical_filter_hash: String,
    99	}
   100	
   101	impl SubShape {
   102	    /// Recompute [`Self::canonical_filter_hash`] from the current `shape`.
   103	    ///
   104	    /// Required by any post-compile pass that mutates the shape (the M4
   105	    /// coverage gate is the only current consumer — it bumps `since` after the
   106	    /// compiler runs). Without this call the wire-emitter's diff would treat
   107	    /// the mutated shape as identical to the pre-mutation one and skip the
   108	    /// REQ frame — leaving the relay on a stale `since`. See
   109	    /// `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3.3 and the M4
   110	    /// codex review at `docs/perf/codex-reviews/076173d.md` (P1 plan-identity
   111	    /// bug).
   112	    pub fn recompute_hash(&mut self) {
   113	        self.canonical_filter_hash = canonical_filter_hash(&self.shape);
   114	    }
   115	}
   116	
   117	/// Canonical filter hash — single source of truth for `(filter, relay)`
   118	/// identity across the planner, wire-emitter, and watermark store.
   119	///
   120	/// The current implementation is the stop-gap `DefaultHasher` digest produced
   121	/// by the compiler since M2 (see `compiler/mod.rs::simple_shape_hash`). It is
   122	/// stable across recompiles of an identical `InterestShape` because every
   123	/// collection field uses a sorted container (`BTreeSet` / `BTreeMap`) and the
   124	/// JSON serialisation is therefore deterministic.
   125	///
   126	/// Replacement target — once the BLAKE3-CBOR canonical encoding described in
   127	/// `docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md` §3 lands, this function swaps to the
   128	/// 32-byte BLAKE3 hex form; the eight-character window will widen accordingly.
   129	/// All callers (compiler, planner gate, wire-emitter, watermark store) read
   130	/// this single helper so the swap is one edit.
   131	pub fn canonical_filter_hash(shape: &InterestShape) -> String {
   132	    let mut h = DefaultHasher::new();
   133	    if let Ok(json) = serde_json::to_string(shape) {
   134	        json.hash(&mut h);
   135	    }
   136	    format!("{:08x}", h.finish() & 0xffff_ffff)
   137	}
   138	
   139	// ─── RelayPlan ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   140	
   141	/// The per-relay slice of a `CompiledPlan`: which sub-shapes (REQs) go to
   142	/// this relay and why.
   143	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   144	pub struct RelayPlan {
   145	    /// The relay this plan slice targets.
   146	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
   147	    /// Why this relay is in the plan (may contain multiple sources).
   148	    pub role_tags: BTreeSet<RoutingSource>,
   149	    /// Each sub-shape becomes one wire REQ on this relay.
   150	    pub sub_shapes: Vec<SubShape>,
   151	}
   152	
   153	// ─── CompiledPlan ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   154	
   155	/// The output of the subscription compiler: a per-relay mapping of what REQs
   156	/// to emit.
   157	///
   158	/// `plan_id` is the stable identity the platform observes for diagnostic
   159	/// continuity. It is content-addressed over the interest set, mailbox snapshot,
   160	/// and lattice version — so two compiles with no material change produce the
   161	/// same id (idempotency check).
   162	///
   163	/// Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3.4
   164	/// Doctrine: D6 (errors are internal Results), D8 (composite reverse index).
   165	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   166	pub struct CompiledPlan {
   167	    /// Stable, content-addressed plan identity.
   168	    ///
   169	    /// `plan_id = hash(sorted_interests, sorted_mailbox_snapshot, lattice_version)`
   170	    /// (see compiler.md §3.4 for the full hash definition).

     1	//! M8-subs — subscription lifecycle: the seam between M2's `CompiledPlan`
     2	//! and the wire.
     3	//!
     4	//! See `docs/plan/m8-subscription-lifecycle.md` for the scope discriminator
     5	//! against M4 (negentropy), M5 (NIP-42 auth), M7 (publishing). This module
     6	//! ships only the **seams**:
     7	//!
     8	//! - [`InterestRegistry`] — single-writer logical-interest store (D4).
     9	//! - [`CompileTrigger`] inbox — FIFO + per-tick coalescing (D8).
    10	//! - Wire-emitter — `CompiledPlan` → `Vec<WireFrame>` diff.
    11	//! - [`ConnectionPool`] — uniform send-path shared by M4/M5/M7 (D7).
    12	//!
    13	//! Doctrine:
    14	//! - **D3** routing is consumed verbatim from the planner; we never reroute.
    15	//! - **D4** the registry is the single writer of the active-interest set.
    16	//! - **D6** all error types here are internal `Result`s; no FFI exposure.
    17	//! - **D7** the pool reports; the actor decides. No reconnect policy here.
    18	//! - **D8** per-tick coalesce caps recompiles at 1 / view / tick.
    19	//!
    20	//! Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md` §4.
    21	
    22	pub(crate) mod auth_gate;
    23	pub(crate) mod inbox;
    24	pub(crate) mod lifecycle_gate;
    25	pub(crate) mod pool;
    26	pub(crate) mod registry;
    27	pub(crate) mod trigger;
    28	pub(crate) mod wire;
    29	
    30	use std::sync::Arc;
    31	
    32	use auth_gate::AuthGate;
    33	use lifecycle_gate::LifecycleGate;
    34	
    35	use crate::planner::{
    36	    CompiledPlan, InMemoryMailboxCache, InterestId, MailboxSnapshot, PlannerError, RelayUrl,
    37	    SubscriptionCompiler,
    38	};
    39	
    40	/// Post-compile plan-mutation hook (M4 negentropy coverage gate).
    41	///
    42	/// The lifecycle owns the *seam* into which `nmp-nip77`'s
    43	/// `apply_coverage_filter` is installed by the actor at startup. The hook runs
    44	/// between `compile()` and `plan_diff()` — i.e. after the M2 compiler
    45	/// produces the plan but before the wire-emitter diffs against the prior
    46	/// plan. The hook is free to drop sub-shapes, bump `since`, or otherwise
    47	/// rewrite the plan; any sub-shape whose `shape` is mutated MUST call
    48	/// [`crate::planner::SubShape::recompute_hash`] (see the M4 codex review's P1
    49	/// finding in `docs/perf/codex-reviews/076173d.md`).
    50	///
    51	/// Direction: `nmp-core` defines the seam, `nmp-nip77` installs the policy —
    52	/// keeping coverage-gate / NIP-77 vocabulary out of `nmp-core` per D0
    53	/// ("kernel never grows app nouns").
    54	pub type PlanCoverageHook = Arc<dyn Fn(&mut CompiledPlan) + Send + Sync>;
    55	
    56	pub use inbox::TriggerInbox;
    57	pub use pool::{ConnectionPool, InMemoryPool, PoolSendOutcome};
    58	pub use registry::InterestRegistry;
    59	pub use trigger::{AccountId, CompileTrigger, InvalidateReason, RelayAuthState, SignerId};
    60	pub use wire::{plan_diff, WireFrame};
    61	
    62	// ─── SubscriptionLifecycle ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
    63	
    64	/// The top-level subscription lifecycle controller.
    65	///
    66	/// Owns the registry, trigger inbox, last-compiled plan, the lifecycle gate
    67	/// (OneShot / BoundedTime CLOSE bookkeeping), and the auth gate (REQs to
    68	/// auth-paused relays held in a pending buffer). Drives recompiles when
    69	/// ticked; emits `WireFrame`s for the actor to push through the connection
    70	/// pool.
    71	///
    72	/// **Per-tick discipline (D8):** N triggers in the inbox between two
    73	/// `drain_tick()` calls produce at most one compile. An empty inbox tick
    74	/// produces zero compiles.
    75	pub struct SubscriptionLifecycle {
    76	    registry: InterestRegistry,
    77	    inbox: TriggerInbox,
    78	    mailboxes: InMemoryMailboxCache,
    79	    indexer_relays: Vec<RelayUrl>,
    80	    /// The plan currently believed-to-be-live on the wire.
    81	    current_plan: Option<CompiledPlan>,
    82	    /// Per-sub lifecycle bookkeeping (OneShot, BoundedTime).
    83	    lifecycle_gate: LifecycleGate,
    84	    /// Per-relay auth state + pending REQ buffer.
    85	    auth_gate: AuthGate,
    86	    /// Monotonic compile counter for test assertions.
    87	    compile_count: u64,
    88	    /// Optional post-compile plan-mutation hook (see [`PlanCoverageHook`]).
    89	    /// Set via [`Self::set_coverage_hook`]; absent by default so the kernel
    90	    /// links cleanly without any NIP-77 dependency.
    91	    coverage_hook: Option<PlanCoverageHook>,
    92	}
    93	
    94	impl Default for SubscriptionLifecycle {
    95	    fn default() -> Self {
    96	        Self::new()
    97	    }
    98	}
    99	
   100	impl SubscriptionLifecycle {
   101	    /// Construct an empty lifecycle with no mailboxes and a default indexer set.
   102	    pub fn new() -> Self {
   103	        Self {
   104	            registry: InterestRegistry::new(),
   105	            inbox: TriggerInbox::new(),
   106	            mailboxes: InMemoryMailboxCache::new(),
   107	            indexer_relays: vec!["wss://purplepag.es".to_string()],
   108	            current_plan: None,
   109	            lifecycle_gate: LifecycleGate::new(),
   110	            auth_gate: AuthGate::new(),
   111	            compile_count: 0,
   112	            coverage_hook: None,
   113	        }
   114	    }
   115	
   116	    /// Install (or replace) the post-compile [`PlanCoverageHook`].
   117	    ///
   118	    /// The actor calls this once at startup with
   119	    /// `Arc::new(|plan| { nmp_nip77::apply_coverage_filter(plan, …); })`
   120	    /// — `nmp-core` itself never knows the hook's identity. C10 is the
   121	    /// `nmp-testing` contract test that exercises this seam end-to-end.
   122	    pub fn set_coverage_hook(&mut self, hook: PlanCoverageHook) {
   123	        self.coverage_hook = Some(hook);
   124	    }
   125	
   126	    /// Mutable access to the registry — view modules push interests through
   127	    /// this in production; integration tests push directly.
   128	    pub fn registry_mut(&mut self) -> &mut InterestRegistry {
   129	        &mut self.registry
   130	    }
   131	
   132	    /// Register or replace the mailbox entry for `pubkey` (test helper /
   133	    /// M2-phase-2 path).
   134	    pub fn set_mailbox(&mut self, pubkey: String, write_relays: &[&str]) {
   135	        let snapshot = MailboxSnapshot {
   136	            write_relays: write_relays.iter().map(|r| r.to_string()).collect(),
   137	            read_relays: vec![],
   138	            both_relays: vec![],
   139	        };
   140	        self.mailboxes.put(pubkey, snapshot);
   141	    }
   142	
   143	    /// Compile counter (one increment per planner invocation).
   144	    pub fn compile_count(&self) -> u64 {
   145	        self.compile_count
   146	    }
   147	
   148	    /// Enqueue a trigger. Coalesced with siblings until the next `drain_tick`.
   149	    pub fn enqueue_trigger(&mut self, trigger: CompileTrigger) {
   150	        self.inbox.enqueue(trigger);
   151	    }
   152	
   153	    /// Recompile from current registry + mailbox state, diff against the
   154	    /// last-compiled plan, and return the WireFrame delta. Updates the
   155	    /// lifecycle gate; diverts REQs targeting auth-paused relays into the
   156	    /// pending-auth buffer.
   157	    pub fn recompile_and_diff(&mut self) -> Result<Vec<WireFrame>, PlannerError> {
   158	        let interests = self.registry.iter_active();
   159	        let compiler = SubscriptionCompiler::new(&self.mailboxes, &self.indexer_relays);
   160	        let mut plan = compiler.compile(&interests)?;
   161	        self.compile_count = self.compile_count.saturating_add(1);
   162	
   163	        // D2 negentropy-first: let the coverage-gate hook (M4) rewrite the
   164	        // plan before the wire-emitter sees it — skipping authoritative
   165	        // (filter, relay) pairs and bumping `since` on pairs we already have
   166	        // a watermark for. With no hook installed (the kernel-only path) the
   167	        // plan flows through unchanged.
   168	        if let Some(hook) = self.coverage_hook.as_ref() {
   169	            hook(&mut plan);
   170	        }
   171	
   172	        let prior = self.current_plan.as_ref();
   173	        let raw_frames = plan_diff(prior, Some(&plan), &interests);
   174	
   175	        // Update lifecycle bookkeeping BEFORE auth partition, so REQs held
   176	        // back for auth are still considered "known" once they fire after
   177	        // Authenticated drains the buffer.
   178	        self.lifecycle_gate.observe_diff(&raw_frames);
   179	        self.current_plan = Some(plan);
   180	
   181	        Ok(self.auth_gate.partition(raw_frames))
   182	    }
   183	
   184	    /// Drain the trigger inbox at a tick boundary. Per D8, all triggers
   185	    /// collapse into at most one compile pass; an empty inbox is a no-op.
   186	    pub fn drain_tick(&mut self) -> Vec<WireFrame> {
   187	        let triggers = self.inbox.drain_coalesced();
   188	        if triggers.is_empty() {
   189	            return Vec::new();
   190	        }
   191	        // Apply non-recompile-side-effecting triggers first (auth-state). We
   192	        // do not flush pending REQs here even on Authenticated; the
   193	        // subsequent recompile will re-walk them as part of the new diff.
   194	        for t in &triggers {
   195	            if let CompileTrigger::RelayAuthStateChanged { url, state } = t {
   196	                let _ = self.auth_gate.record_transition(url.clone(), state.clone());
   197	            }
   198	        }
   199	        self.recompile_and_diff().unwrap_or_default()
   200	    }
   201	
   202	    /// A5 — relay-reconnected. Per recompilation.md §4.2: replay current plan
   203	    /// to that relay WITHOUT invoking the planner. This is a pure replay, not
   204	    /// a recompile.
   205	    pub fn handle_reconnect(&mut self, relay_url: RelayUrl) -> Vec<WireFrame> {
   206	        let Some(plan) = self.current_plan.as_ref() else {
   207	            return Vec::new();
   208	        };
   209	        let Some(relay_plan) = plan.per_relay.get(&relay_url) else {
   210	            return Vec::new();
   211	        };
   212	        let interests = self.registry.iter_active();
   213	        let mut frames = Vec::with_capacity(relay_plan.sub_shapes.len());
   214	        for shape in &relay_plan.sub_shapes {
   215	            let sub_id = wire::sub_id_for(&plan.plan_id, shape);
   216	            let interest_id = shape
   217	                .originating_interests
   218	                .first()
   219	                .cloned()
   220	                .unwrap_or(InterestId(0));
   221	            let lifecycle = wire::lifecycle_for_shape(shape, &interests);
   222	            let filter_json = wire::filter_json_for(&shape.shape);
   223	            frames.push(WireFrame::Req {
   224	                relay_url: relay_url.clone(),
   225	                sub_id,
   226	                filter_json,
   227	                interest_id,
   228	                lifecycle,
   229	            });
   230	        }
