     1	//! Planner-side coverage gate.
     2	//!
     3	//! Bridges [`crate::coverage_gate::decide_strategy`] into the M2 subscription
     4	//! planner.  The planner produces a [`CompiledPlan`]; this gate rewrites it
     5	//! per `(filter, relay)` according to the current watermark + capability
     6	//! reading:
     7	//!
     8	//! | Strategy | Plan rewrite |
     9	//! |---|---|
    10	//! | `SkipReq` | Sub-shape dropped from the relay plan; relay's plan removed if empty. |
    11	//! | `ReqSince(s)` | Sub-shape's `since` is bumped to `s` (no-op if existing `since` is already ≥ `s`). |
    12	//! | `NegThenReq` | Sub-shape preserved; the planner emits the REQ unchanged.  The companion negentropy run happens on the parallel sync path. |
    13	//! | `Resume { next, .. }` | Same as `next`; the resume blob is consumed by the negentropy run, not the planner. |
    14	//!
    15	//! Returns a [`CoverageReport`] enumerating the decisions taken so callers
    16	//! (e.g. ADR-0007 diagnostics, the firehose bench) can audit the gate's
    17	//! behaviour without re-running it.
    18	//!
    19	//! ## Doctrine
    20	//!
    21	//! * **D2** — every relay plan first consults coverage before its REQ flies.
    22	//! * **D6** — the gate is infallible: every input maps to a deterministic
    23	//!   plan rewrite.  No `Result`.  No FFI surface to leak through.
    24	
    25	use std::collections::BTreeMap;
    26	
    27	use nmp_core::planner::CompiledPlan;
    28	use nmp_core::store::{EventStore, WatermarkKey};
    29	
    30	use crate::capability::CapabilityCache;
    31	use crate::coverage_gate::{decide_strategy, GateInputs, SyncStrategy};
    32	
    33	/// Per-`(filter, relay)` outcome of the gate.
    34	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
    35	pub enum GateDecision {
    36	    Skipped,
    37	    BumpedSince(u64),
    38	    Kept,
    39	}
    40	
    41	/// Aggregate audit trail.
    42	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default)]
    43	pub struct CoverageReport {
    44	    pub decisions: BTreeMap<(String, String), GateDecision>,
    45	}
    46	
    47	impl CoverageReport {
    48	    pub fn count_skipped(&self) -> usize {
    49	        self.decisions
    50	            .values()
    51	            .filter(|d| matches!(d, GateDecision::Skipped))
    52	            .count()
    53	    }
    54	
    55	    pub fn count_bumped(&self) -> usize {
    56	        self.decisions
    57	            .values()
    58	            .filter(|d| matches!(d, GateDecision::BumpedSince(_)))
    59	            .count()
    60	    }
    61	}
    62	
    63	/// Rewrite `plan` in place per the M4 coverage gate.
    64	///
    65	/// `canonicalise` maps a sub-shape's filter to the 32-byte canonical filter
    66	/// hash expected by [`WatermarkKey::filter_hash`].  The kernel will eventually
    67	/// expose `canonical_filter_hash(&Filter)` (see `docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md`
    68	/// §3); until it does, callers pass their own hash function — keeping
    69	/// `nmp-nip77` decoupled from the (still-design-stage) canonical encoder.
    70	pub fn apply_coverage_filter<F>(
    71	    plan: &mut CompiledPlan,
    72	    store: &dyn EventStore,
    73	    caps: &dyn CapabilityCache,
    74	    canonicalise: F,
    75	) -> CoverageReport
    76	where
    77	    F: Fn(&nmp_core::planner::SubShape) -> [u8; 32],
    78	{
    79	    let mut report = CoverageReport::default();
    80	    let mut empty_relays: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
    81	
    82	    for (relay_url, relay_plan) in plan.per_relay.iter_mut() {
    83	        relay_plan.sub_shapes.retain_mut(|sub| {
    84	            let filter_hash = canonicalise(sub);
    85	            let key = WatermarkKey {
    86	                filter_hash,
    87	                relay_url: relay_url.clone(),
    88	            };
    89	            let coverage = store.coverage(&key).unwrap_or(nmp_core::store::Coverage::Unknown);
    90	            let watermark = store.read_watermark(&key).ok().flatten();
    91	            let capabilities = caps.get(relay_url);
    92	            let strategy = decide_strategy(
    93	                &key,
    94	                GateInputs {
    95	                    coverage,
    96	                    capabilities,
    97	                    watermark,
    98	                },
    99	            );
   100	            let (keep, decision) = apply_strategy_to_sub(sub, &strategy);
   101	            report.decisions.insert(
   102	                (relay_url.clone(), hex32(&filter_hash)),
   103	                decision,
   104	            );
   105	            keep
   106	        });
   107	        if relay_plan.sub_shapes.is_empty() {
   108	            empty_relays.push(relay_url.clone());
   109	        }
   110	    }
   111	    for relay_url in empty_relays {
   112	        plan.per_relay.remove(&relay_url);
   113	    }
   114	    report
   115	}
   116	
   117	fn apply_strategy_to_sub(
   118	    sub: &mut nmp_core::planner::SubShape,
   119	    strategy: &SyncStrategy,
   120	) -> (bool, GateDecision) {
   121	    match strategy.inner() {
   122	        SyncStrategy::SkipReq => (false, GateDecision::Skipped),
   123	        SyncStrategy::ReqSince(since) => {
   124	            if sub.shape.since.map(|s| s < *since).unwrap_or(true) {
   125	                sub.shape.since = Some(*since);
   126	                // Mutating `shape.since` changes wire identity. The
   127	                // wire-emitter keys sub-ids by `canonical_filter_hash`
   128	                // (`subs::wire::sub_id_for`), so we must recompute it here —
   129	                // otherwise the diff against the prior plan would see no
   130	                // change and the new `since` would never reach the relay.
   131	                // See the M4 codex review at
   132	                // `docs/perf/codex-reviews/076173d.md` (P1 plan-identity).
   133	                sub.recompute_hash();
   134	                (true, GateDecision::BumpedSince(*since))
   135	            } else {
   136	                (true, GateDecision::Kept)
   137	            }
   138	        }
   139	        SyncStrategy::NegThenReq => (true, GateDecision::Kept),
   140	        // `Resume` is collapsed by `.inner()`, so it's unreachable here.
   141	        SyncStrategy::Resume { .. } => (true, GateDecision::Kept),
   142	    }
   143	}
   144	
   145	fn hex32(bytes: &[u8; 32]) -> String {
   146	    static HEX: &[u8; 16] = b"0123456789abcdef";
   147	    let mut out = String::with_capacity(64);
   148	    for b in bytes {
   149	        out.push(HEX[(b >> 4) as usize] as char);
   150	        out.push(HEX[(b & 0x0F) as usize] as char);
   151	    }
   152	    out
   153	}
   154	
   155	// Unit tests live in `planner_gate_tests.rs` (sibling file) so this module
   156	// stays under the 300 LOC soft cap.  Registered from `lib.rs`.
   157

     1	//! Actor main loop — message routing, command dispatch, relay event handling.
     2	//!
     3	//! Idle-tick timing helpers are in `tick.rs`.
     4	//! Relay lifecycle helpers are in `relay_mgmt.rs`.
     5	
     6	mod relay_mgmt;
     7	mod tick;
     8	
     9	use relay_mgmt::{
    10	    all_relays_connected, bridge_commands, bridge_relays, close_relays, maybe_send_startup,
    11	    send_all_outbound, spawn_missing_relays,
    12	};
    13	use tick::{emit_now, flush_due, next_actor_msg};
    14	
    15	use crate::kernel::Kernel;
    16	use crate::relay::{OutboundMessage, RelayRole, DEFAULT_EMIT_HZ, DEFAULT_VISIBLE_LIMIT};
    17	use crate::relay_worker::{RelayCommand, RelayEvent};
    18	use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
    19	use std::sync::mpsc::{self, Receiver, Sender};
    20	use std::time::{Duration, Instant};
    21	
    22	/// Actor command variants.  The `actor` module is private (`mod actor`, not
    23	/// `pub mod actor`), so this `pub` is only reachable from outside the crate
    24	/// through the `testing` re-export gate.  In normal (non-test-support) builds
    25	/// nothing re-exports these items, so they remain effectively crate-private.
    26	#[derive(Debug)]
    27	pub enum ActorCommand {
    28	    Start { visible_limit: usize, emit_hz: u32 },
    29	    Configure { visible_limit: usize, emit_hz: u32 },
    30	    OpenAuthor { pubkey: String },
    31	    OpenThread { event_id: String },
    32	    OpenFirehoseTag { tag: String },
    33	    ClaimProfile { pubkey: String, consumer_id: String },
    34	    ReleaseProfile { pubkey: String, consumer_id: String },
    35	    CloseAuthor { pubkey: String },
    36	    CloseThread { event_id: String },
    37	    Stop,
    38	    Reset,
    39	    Shutdown,
    40	    /// Ingest pre-verified timeline events through the test-support kernel path.
    41	    ///
    42	    /// The caller is responsible for constructing `VerifiedEvent` values; this
    43	    /// command routes each through `kernel::ingest_pre_verified_event` under the
    44	    /// `"diag-firehose-stress"` sub-id. It inserts through the `EventStore`, then
    45	    /// updates the lightweight read-cache directly. No signature re-verification
    46	    /// is performed — the `VerifiedEvent` type is the gate.
    47	    ///
    48	    /// Test-support only (D0: not part of production FFI surface).
    49	    #[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-support"))]
    50	    IngestPreVerifiedEvents(Vec<crate::store::VerifiedEvent>),
    51	}
    52	
    53	pub(super) enum ActorMsg {
    54	    Command(ActorCommand),
    55	    Relay(RelayEvent),
    56	}
    57	
    58	pub(super) struct RelayControl {
    59	    pub(super) generation: u64,
    60	    pub(super) tx: Sender<RelayCommand>,
    61	}
    62	
    63	pub fn run_actor(command_rx: Receiver<ActorCommand>, update_tx: Sender<String>) {
    64	    let (actor_tx, actor_rx) = mpsc::channel();
    65	    bridge_commands(command_rx, actor_tx.clone());
    66	    let (relay_tx, relay_rx) = mpsc::channel();
    67	    bridge_relays(relay_rx, actor_tx.clone());
    68	
    69	    let mut kernel = Kernel::new(DEFAULT_VISIBLE_LIMIT);
    70	    let mut relay_controls: HashMap<RelayRole, RelayControl> = HashMap::new();
    71	    let mut connected_relays = HashSet::new();
    72	    let mut next_relay_generation = 1;
    73	    let mut running = false;
    74	    let mut emit_hz = DEFAULT_EMIT_HZ;
    75	    let mut last_emit = Instant::now() - Duration::from_secs(1);
    76	    let mut startup_sent = false;
    77	
    78	    loop {
    79	        let message = match next_actor_msg(&actor_rx, &kernel, running, last_emit, emit_hz) {
    80	            Ok(Some(message)) => message,
    81	            Ok(None) => {
    82	                // Flush any time-gated view requests (e.g. contacts_deadline).
    83	                let pending = kernel.pending_view_requests();
    84	                if !pending.is_empty() {
    85	                    send_all_outbound(&relay_controls, &mut kernel, pending);
    86	                }
    87	                // Only emit when state actually changed; do not emit on every
    88	                // idle tick (D8: zero false-wakeup allocations after warmup).
    89	                if kernel.changed_since_emit() {
    90	                    emit_now(&mut kernel, running, &update_tx, &mut last_emit);
    91	                }
    92	                continue;
    93	            }
    94	            Err(()) => {
    95	                close_relays(&mut relay_controls, &mut connected_relays, &mut kernel);
    96	                return;
    97	            }
    98	        };
    99	
   100	        match message {
   101	            ActorMsg::Command(command) => {
   102	                let relays_ready = all_relays_connected(&connected_relays);
   103	                let outbound = dispatch_command(
   104	                    command,
   105	                    &mut kernel,
   106	                    &mut relay_controls,
   107	                    &relay_tx,
   108	                    &mut connected_relays,
   109	                    &update_tx,
   110	                    &mut last_emit,
   111	                    &mut next_relay_generation,
   112	                    &mut running,
   113	                    &mut emit_hz,
   114	                    &mut startup_sent,
   115	                    relays_ready,
   116	                );
   117	                let Some(outbound) = outbound else {
   118	                    return; // Shutdown
   119	                };
   120	                if running {
   121	                    send_all_outbound(&relay_controls, &mut kernel, outbound);
   122	                    if maybe_send_startup(
   123	                        running,
   124	                        &mut startup_sent,
   125	                        &connected_relays,
   126	                        &relay_controls,
   127	                        &mut kernel,
   128	                    ) {
   129	                        emit_now(&mut kernel, running, &update_tx, &mut last_emit);
   130	                    }
   131	                }
   132	            }
   133	            ActorMsg::Relay(event) => {
   134	                let role = event.role();
   135	                let generation = event.generation();
   136	                if relay_controls
   137	                    .get(&role)
   138	                    .is_none_or(|control| control.generation != generation)
   139	                {
   140	                    continue;
   141	                }
   142	                handle_relay_event(
   143	                    event,
   144	                    &mut kernel,
   145	                    &mut relay_controls,
   146	                    &mut connected_relays,
   147	                    &update_tx,
   148	                    &mut last_emit,
   149	                    &mut startup_sent,
   150	                    running,
   151	                );
   152	            }
   153	        }
   154	
   155	        if flush_due(&kernel, running, last_emit, emit_hz) {
   156	            emit_now(&mut kernel, running, &update_tx, &mut last_emit);
   157	        }
   158	    }
   159	}
   160	
   161	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
   162	fn dispatch_command(
   163	    command: ActorCommand,
   164	    kernel: &mut Kernel,
   165	    relay_controls: &mut HashMap<RelayRole, RelayControl>,
   166	    relay_tx: &Sender<RelayEvent>,
   167	    connected_relays: &mut HashSet<RelayRole>,
   168	    update_tx: &Sender<String>,
   169	    last_emit: &mut Instant,
   170	    next_relay_generation: &mut u64,
   171	    running: &mut bool,
   172	    emit_hz: &mut u32,
   173	    startup_sent: &mut bool,
   174	    relays_ready: bool,
   175	) -> Option<Vec<OutboundMessage>> {
   176	    match command {
   177	        ActorCommand::Start {
   178	            visible_limit,
   179	            emit_hz: hz,
   180	        } => {
   181	            *running = true;
   182	            *emit_hz = hz;
   183	            *startup_sent = false;
   184	            kernel.set_visible_limit(visible_limit);
   185	            kernel.start();
   186	            spawn_missing_relays(relay_controls, relay_tx, kernel, next_relay_generation);
   187	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   188	            Some(Vec::new())
   189	        }
   190	        ActorCommand::Configure {
   191	            visible_limit,
   192	            emit_hz: hz,
   193	        } => {
   194	            *emit_hz = hz;
   195	            kernel.set_visible_limit(visible_limit);
   196	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   197	            Some(Vec::new())
   198	        }
   199	        ActorCommand::OpenAuthor { pubkey } => {
   200	            let outbound = kernel.open_author(pubkey, relays_ready);
   201	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   202	            Some(outbound)
   203	        }
   204	        ActorCommand::OpenThread { event_id } => {
   205	            let outbound = kernel.open_thread(event_id, relays_ready);
   206	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   207	            Some(outbound)
   208	        }
   209	        ActorCommand::OpenFirehoseTag { tag } => {
   210	            let outbound = kernel.open_firehose_tag(tag, relays_ready);
   211	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   212	            Some(outbound)
   213	        }
   214	        ActorCommand::ClaimProfile {
   215	            pubkey,
   216	            consumer_id,
   217	        } => {
   218	            let outbound = kernel.claim_profile(pubkey, consumer_id, relays_ready);
   219	            emit_now(kernel, *running, update_tx, last_emit);
   220	            Some(outbound)

     1	//! Framework Magic §C10 — Sync watermarks gate backfill; cache-miss authoritative.
     2	//!
     3	//! Standalone test binary so the C10 wiring proof can grow without breaking
     4	//! the 500 LOC ceiling on `framework_magic_contract.rs` (per AGENTS.md). The
     5	//! contract-table meta-test (`framework_magic_contract::contract_surface_complete`)
     6	//! key off the doc-table column for `c10_…`, not on this file's path —
     7	//! splitting it out is invisible at the contract layer.
     8	//!
     9	//! Design: `docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md` §C10.
    10	//! Codex review: `docs/perf/codex-reviews/076173d.md` (T53 follow-up — wires
    11	//! `apply_coverage_filter` into the actor plan-emit path, flips C10 active).
    12	
    13	use std::sync::Arc;
    14	
    15	use nmp_core::planner::{
    16	    canonical_filter_hash,
    17	    InterestId,
    18	    InterestLifecycle,
    19	    InterestScope,
    20	    InterestShape,
    21	    LogicalInterest,
    22	};
    23	use nmp_core::store::{EventStore, MemEventStore, SyncMethod, WatermarkKey, WatermarkRow};
    24	use nmp_core::subs::{SubscriptionLifecycle, WireFrame};
    25	use nmp_nip77::capability::{CapabilityCache, InMemoryCapabilityCache, RelayCapabilities};
    26	use nmp_nip77::planner_gate::apply_coverage_filter;
    27	
    28	// ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    29	
    30	fn pubkey(seed: &str) -> String {
    31	    format!("{seed:0>64}")
    32	        .chars()
    33	        .take(64)
    34	        .collect::<String>()
    35	        .to_lowercase()
    36	}
    37	
    38	fn now_unix() -> u64 {
    39	    std::time::SystemTime::now()
    40	        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
    41	        .unwrap()
    42	        .as_secs()
    43	}
    44	
    45	/// Hash-shape -> 32-byte watermark key.
    46	///
    47	/// The planner gate keys watermarks by `[u8; 32]` (see
    48	/// `WatermarkKey::filter_hash`) but the canonical hash today is the 8-hex
    49	/// `String` `canonical_filter_hash` returns. Expand it deterministically so
    50	/// the gate and the test agree on identity; the real BLAKE3-CBOR encoder
    51	/// (`docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md` §3) will replace both call-sites in one
    52	/// edit.
    53	fn canon(sub: &nmp_core::planner::SubShape) -> [u8; 32] {
    54	    let hex = canonical_filter_hash(&sub.shape);
    55	    let bytes = hex.as_bytes();
    56	    let mut out = [0u8; 32];
    57	    for (i, slot) in out.iter_mut().enumerate() {
    58	        *slot = bytes[i % bytes.len()];
    59	    }
    60	    out
    61	}
    62	
    63	fn canon_shape(shape: &InterestShape) -> [u8; 32] {
    64	    let hex = canonical_filter_hash(shape);
    65	    let bytes = hex.as_bytes();
    66	    let mut out = [0u8; 32];
    67	    for (i, slot) in out.iter_mut().enumerate() {
    68	        *slot = bytes[i % bytes.len()];
    69	    }
    70	    out
    71	}
    72	
    73	// ── C10 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    74	
    75	/// C10: Watermarks gate backfill; cache miss becomes authoritative; NIP-77 default.
    76	///
    77	/// Wires the lifecycle's `set_coverage_hook` seam (M4) to
    78	/// `nmp_nip77::apply_coverage_filter` and asserts the four observable
    79	/// properties from `docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md` §C10:
    80	///
    81	/// 1. **Unsynced pair → fetch.** No watermark + no capability data → the
    82	///    planner emits a REQ (the hook keeps the sub-shape).
    83	/// 2. **Fully-synced pair → authoritative miss.** Once a fresh `CompleteAsOf`
    84	///    watermark exists for the `(filter, relay)`, the hook drops the
    85	///    sub-shape and the plan diff CLOSEs the live REQ.
    86	/// 3. **NIP-77 capability is the default backfill.** A stale `PartialUpTo`
    87	///    watermark on a NIP-77-capable relay keeps the sub-shape intact
    88	///    (`SyncStrategy::NegThenReq`) — the actor's NIP-77 sync path handles
    89	///    backfill, the planner does not duplicate it with a since-bumped REQ.
    90	/// 4. **Capability fallback.** A stale watermark on a relay that does NOT
    91	///    support NIP-77 bumps `since` to `synced_up_to + 1`
    92	///    (`SyncStrategy::ReqSince`) AND recomputes the sub-shape's
    93	///    `canonical_filter_hash` so the wire-emitter actually routes the new
    94	///    REQ (P1 plan-identity guarantee from
    95	///    `docs/perf/codex-reviews/076173d.md`).
    96	#[test]
    97	fn c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss() {
    98	    // Single author routed to a single relay so the compiler emits exactly
    99	    // one sub-shape we can inspect.
   100	    let author = pubkey("alice-c10");
   101	    let relay_url = "wss://relay.c10.test/".to_string();
   102	    let interest_shape = InterestShape {
   103	        authors: [author.clone()].into_iter().collect(),
   104	        kinds: [1u32].into_iter().collect(),
   105	        ..Default::default()
   106	    };
   107	    let interest = LogicalInterest {
   108	        id: InterestId(910),
   109	        scope: InterestScope::Global,
   110	        shape: interest_shape.clone(),
   111	        hints: Vec::new(),
   112	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::Tailing,
   113	    };
   114	    // Since the compiler is deterministic and only one interest+mailbox
   115	    // pair exists, the live sub-shape's canonical hash matches the hash
   116	    // we compute directly from the interest's shape.
   117	    let filter_hash_active = canon_shape(&interest_shape);
   118	
   119	    let store = Arc::new(MemEventStore::new());
   120	    let caps = Arc::new(InMemoryCapabilityCache::new());
   121	
   122	    let mut lifecycle = SubscriptionLifecycle::new();
   123	    lifecycle.set_mailbox(author.clone(), &[relay_url.as_str()]);
   124	    let store_for_hook = Arc::clone(&store);
   125	    let caps_for_hook = Arc::clone(&caps);
   126	    lifecycle.set_coverage_hook(Arc::new(move |plan| {
   127	        let _ = apply_coverage_filter(plan, &*store_for_hook, &*caps_for_hook, canon);
   128	    }));
   129	    lifecycle.registry_mut().push(interest);
   130	
   131	    // ── Step 1: unsynced + no capability → REQ flies ────────────────────────
   132	    let frames = lifecycle.recompile_and_diff().expect("compile #1");
   133	    let reqs_step1 = frames
   134	        .iter()
   135	        .filter(|f| matches!(f, WireFrame::Req { .. }))
   136	        .count();
   137	    assert_eq!(
   138	        reqs_step1, 1,
   139	        "unsynced cold open must emit exactly one REQ; got {frames:?}"
   140	    );
   141	
   142	    // ── Step 2: fresh CompleteAsOf watermark → SkipReq → CLOSE ──────────────
   143	    let now_s = now_unix();
   144	    store
   145	        .write_watermark(WatermarkRow {
   146	            key: WatermarkKey {
   147	                filter_hash: filter_hash_active,
   148	                relay_url: relay_url.clone(),
   149	            },
   150	            synced_up_to: now_s,
   151	            last_sync_method: SyncMethod::Negentropy,
   152	            last_negentropy_state: None,
   153	            bytes_saved_vs_req: 0,
   154	            updated_at: now_s,
   155	        })
   156	        .unwrap();
   157	
   158	    let frames = lifecycle.recompile_and_diff().expect("compile #2");
   159	    let closes_step2 = frames
   160	        .iter()
   161	        .filter(|f| matches!(f, WireFrame::Close { .. }))
   162	        .count();
   163	    assert_eq!(
   164	        closes_step2, 1,
   165	        "authoritative coverage must CLOSE the live REQ; got {frames:?}"
   166	    );
   167	
   168	    // ── Step 3: stale watermark + NIP-77 capability → NegThenReq → REQ kept ──
   169	    //
   170	    // `MemEventStore::coverage` uses `now() - updated_at > 300 s` as the
   171	    // staleness threshold, so we backdate `updated_at` an hour into the past
   172	    // to land in `Coverage::PartialUpTo`.
   173	    let stale_synced_up_to = now_s.saturating_sub(3_600);
   174	    let stale_updated_at = stale_synced_up_to;
   175	    store
   176	        .write_watermark(WatermarkRow {
   177	            key: WatermarkKey {
   178	                filter_hash: filter_hash_active,
   179	                relay_url: relay_url.clone(),
   180	            },
   181	            synced_up_to: stale_synced_up_to,
   182	            last_sync_method: SyncMethod::Negentropy,
   183	            last_negentropy_state: None,
   184	            bytes_saved_vs_req: 0,
   185	            updated_at: stale_updated_at,
   186	        })
   187	        .unwrap();
   188	    caps.set(
   189	        relay_url.as_str(),
   190	        RelayCapabilities { supports_nip77: true },
   191	    );
   192	
   193	    let frames = lifecycle.recompile_and_diff().expect("compile #3");
   194	    let req_jsons_step3: Vec<_> = frames
   195	        .iter()
   196	        .filter_map(|f| match f {
   197	            WireFrame::Req { filter_json, .. } => Some(filter_json.clone()),
   198	            _ => None,
   199	        })
   200	        .collect();
   201	    assert_eq!(
   202	        req_jsons_step3.len(),
   203	        1,
   204	        "NegThenReq must re-emit the live REQ unchanged; got {frames:?}"
   205	    );
   206	    assert!(
   207	        !req_jsons_step3[0].contains("\"since\":"),
   208	        "NegThenReq must NOT bump since on the REQ; saw {}",
   209	        req_jsons_step3[0]
   210	    );
   211	
   212	    // ── Step 4: stale watermark + NO NIP-77 capability → ReqSince → bump ────
   213	    caps.set(
   214	        relay_url.as_str(),
   215	        RelayCapabilities { supports_nip77: false },
   216	    );
   217	    let frames = lifecycle.recompile_and_diff().expect("compile #4");
   218	    let req_jsons_step4: Vec<_> = frames
   219	        .iter()
   220	        .filter_map(|f| match f {
   221	            WireFrame::Req { filter_json, .. } => Some(filter_json.clone()),
   222	            _ => None,
   223	        })
   224	        .collect();
   225	    let closes_step4 = frames
   226	        .iter()
   227	        .filter(|f| matches!(f, WireFrame::Close { .. }))
   228	        .count();
   229	    assert_eq!(
   230	        req_jsons_step4.len(),
   231	        1,
   232	        "capability fallback must emit a since-bumped REQ; got {frames:?}"
   233	    );
   234	    assert_eq!(
   235	        closes_step4, 1,
   236	        "capability fallback must CLOSE the prior (un-bumped) REQ because \
   237	         the sub-id changed — proves P1 hash-recompute reaches the wire"
   238	    );
   239	    let expected_since = stale_synced_up_to + 1;
   240	    assert!(
   241	        req_jsons_step4[0].contains(&format!("\"since\":{expected_since}")),
   242	        "ReqSince must bump since to synced_up_to + 1 ({expected_since}); \
   243	         saw {}",
   244	        req_jsons_step4[0]
   245	    );
   246	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! Coverage gate — translates a [`Coverage`] reading + capability cache into
     2	//! the [`SyncStrategy`] the planner should execute.
     3	//!
     4	//! This is the load-bearing decision point for **D2 — negentropy first, REQ
     5	//! second**.  The planner calls [`decide_strategy`] before issuing any REQ;
     6	//! the returned strategy answers three questions in one shot:
     7	//!
     8	//! 1. Is the local cache authoritative (`SkipReq`)?
     9	//! 2. Should we sync first and fall back to REQ on gap (`NegThenReq`)?
    10	//! 3. Are we forced to REQ from a known watermark (`ReqSince`)?
    11	//!
    12	//! ## Threshold
    13	//!
    14	//! The task description fixes the cutover at **95 %** coverage and *recent*
    15	//! freshness.  Freshness comes from the store's `Coverage::CompleteAsOf`
    16	//! signal, which already encodes the 300-second staleness window from
    17	//! `docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md` §1.1.  We never re-implement that check
    18	//! here.
    19	//!
    20	//! ## D6
    21	//!
    22	//! [`decide_strategy`] is infallible — every input maps to a strategy.  No
    23	//! error type, no `Result`.  The planner never has a reason to surface a
    24	//! "coverage decision failed" toast.
    25	
    26	use nmp_core::store::{Coverage, WatermarkKey, WatermarkRow};
    27	
    28	use crate::capability::RelayCapabilities;
    29	
    30	/// Coverage proportion threshold above which a `CompleteAsOf` row earns
    31	/// `SyncStrategy::SkipReq`.  Documented at 95 % in the M4 task spec; kept
    32	/// public so callers and tests don't hard-code the constant in their own
    33	/// asserts.
    34	pub const COVERAGE_THRESHOLD_PCT: u8 = 95;
    35	
    36	/// Inputs the gate consumes.
    37	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
    38	pub struct GateInputs {
    39	    pub coverage: Coverage,
    40	    /// `None` when the capability probe has not run; the gate is conservative
    41	    /// and treats unknown capability as "do not negentropy yet".
    42	    pub capabilities: Option<RelayCapabilities>,
    43	    /// The previously-persisted watermark, if any.  Used to seed `ReqSince`.
    44	    pub watermark: Option<WatermarkRow>,
    45	}
    46	
    47	/// What the planner should do next for a given `(filter, relay)` pair.
    48	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
    49	pub enum SyncStrategy {
    50	    /// Cache is authoritative; emit no REQ.
    51	    SkipReq,
    52	    /// Run a negentropy reconciliation; if anything comes back in `need`, the
    53	    /// planner converts that into an `ids`-scoped REQ for the missing events.
    54	    NegThenReq,
    55	    /// Negentropy not available — REQ from `since` (the watermark's
    56	    /// `synced_up_to + 1`, or `0` if no watermark).  Per the M4 doc-typo
    57	    /// noted in the task description (`last_seen` ≡ `synced_up_to`), the
    58	    /// `+1` keeps us from re-fetching the boundary event.
    59	    ReqSince(u64),
    60	    /// Optional resume blob the planner can hand back to
    61	    /// [`crate::Reconciler::resume_client`] when starting the next sync.
    62	    /// Carried alongside `NegThenReq` and `ReqSince` so the planner doesn't
    63	    /// have to re-read the watermark separately.
    64	    Resume {
    65	        next: Box<SyncStrategy>,
    66	        state: Vec<u8>,
    67	    },
    68	}
    69	
    70	impl SyncStrategy {
    71	    /// Convenience: discard any `Resume` wrapper.
    72	    pub fn inner(&self) -> &SyncStrategy {
    73	        match self {
    74	            SyncStrategy::Resume { next, .. } => next.inner(),
    75	            other => other,
    76	        }
    77	    }
    78	
    79	    /// `true` iff the strategy results in any wire traffic.
    80	    pub fn issues_wire_traffic(&self) -> bool {
    81	        !matches!(self.inner(), SyncStrategy::SkipReq)
    82	    }
    83	}
    84	
    85	/// Apply the gate.
    86	pub fn decide_strategy(_key: &WatermarkKey, inputs: GateInputs) -> SyncStrategy {
    87	    let GateInputs {
    88	        coverage,
    89	        capabilities,
    90	        watermark,
    91	    } = inputs;
    92	
    93	    // Authoritative cache miss — D2 explicitly forbids us from REQ-ing here.
    94	    if matches!(coverage, Coverage::CompleteAsOf(_)) {
    95	        return SyncStrategy::SkipReq;
    96	    }
    97	
    98	    let prefer_neg = capabilities
    99	        .map(|c| c.supports_nip77)
   100	        .unwrap_or(false);
   101	
   102	    let base = if prefer_neg {
   103	        SyncStrategy::NegThenReq
   104	    } else {
   105	        let since = watermark
   106	            .as_ref()
   107	            .map(|w| w.synced_up_to.saturating_add(1))
   108	            .unwrap_or(0);
   109	        SyncStrategy::ReqSince(since)
   110	    };
   111	
   112	    match watermark.and_then(|w| w.last_negentropy_state) {
   113	        Some(state) if prefer_neg => SyncStrategy::Resume {
   114	            next: Box::new(base),
   115	            state,
   116	        },
   117	        _ => base,
   118	    }
   119	}
   120	
   121	/// Recency of a `(filter, relay)` watermark, normalised to `[0.0, 1.0]`.
   122	///
   123	/// This is **not** an event-count coverage ratio (we never claimed to know how
   124	/// many events the relay actually holds). It is a *recency* signal — the
   125	/// fraction of wall-clock time, between the unix epoch and `now_s`, that the
   126	/// watermark covers. `CompleteAsOf` collapses to `1.0` because that variant
   127	/// already encodes the "fresh enough to be authoritative" decision made by
   128	/// the store's staleness window.
   129	///
   130	/// | Coverage variant     | Returned ratio                          |
   131	/// |----------------------|-----------------------------------------|
   132	/// | `CompleteAsOf(_)`    | `1.0` (cache is authoritative)          |
   133	/// | `PartialUpTo(ts)`    | `ts / now_s`, clamped to `[0.0, 1.0]`   |
   134	/// | `Unknown`            | `0.0` (no signal)                       |
   135	///
   136	/// **Use cases.** Diagnostics surfaces (ADR-0007 wire view), firehose-bench
   137	/// instrumentation, and tests that assert a watermark crossed a freshness
   138	/// threshold. The planner gate itself never calls this — it consumes the
   139	/// stronger `Coverage::CompleteAsOf` signal directly via `decide_strategy`.
   140	///
   141	/// The previous `coverage_pct` name and `u8` percentage return type
   142	/// misleadingly suggested this number measured cache completeness; it never
   143	/// did. Renamed in the T53 follow-up per the M4 codex review at
   144	/// `docs/perf/codex-reviews/076173d.md` (P3 misleading public helper).
   145	pub fn freshness_ratio(coverage: Coverage, now_s: u64) -> f32 {
   146	    match coverage {
   147	        Coverage::CompleteAsOf(_) => 1.0,
   148	        Coverage::Unknown => 0.0,
   149	        Coverage::PartialUpTo(ts) => {
   150	            if now_s == 0 || ts == 0 {
   151	                return 0.0;
   152	            }
   153	            ((ts as f64 / now_s as f64).clamp(0.0, 1.0)) as f32
   154	        }
   155	    }
   156	}
   157	
   158	#[cfg(test)]
   159	mod tests {
   160	    use super::*;
   161	    use nmp_core::store::SyncMethod;
   162	
   163	    fn key() -> WatermarkKey {
   164	        WatermarkKey {
   165	            filter_hash: [0; 32],
   166	            relay_url: "wss://r/".into(),
   167	        }
   168	    }
   169	
   170	    fn watermark(synced_up_to: u64, state: Option<Vec<u8>>) -> WatermarkRow {
   171	        WatermarkRow {
   172	            key: key(),
   173	            synced_up_to,
   174	            last_sync_method: SyncMethod::Negentropy,
   175	            last_negentropy_state: state,
   176	            bytes_saved_vs_req: 0,
   177	            updated_at: synced_up_to,
   178	        }
   179	    }
   180	
   181	    #[test]
   182	    fn complete_coverage_skips_req() {
   183	        let s = decide_strategy(
   184	            &key(),
   185	            GateInputs {
   186	                coverage: Coverage::CompleteAsOf(100),
   187	                capabilities: Some(RelayCapabilities {
   188	                    supports_nip77: true,
   189	                }),
   190	                watermark: Some(watermark(100, None)),
   191	            },
   192	        );
   193	        assert_eq!(s, SyncStrategy::SkipReq);
   194	        assert!(!s.issues_wire_traffic());
   195	    }
   196	
   197	    #[test]
   198	    fn partial_coverage_with_nip77_runs_neg() {
   199	        let s = decide_strategy(
   200	            &key(),
   201	            GateInputs {
   202	                coverage: Coverage::PartialUpTo(50),
   203	                capabilities: Some(RelayCapabilities {
   204	                    supports_nip77: true,
   205	                }),
   206	                watermark: Some(watermark(50, None)),
   207	            },
   208	        );
   209	        assert_eq!(s.inner(), &SyncStrategy::NegThenReq);
   210	    }
   211	
   212	    #[test]
   213	    fn partial_coverage_without_nip77_falls_back_to_req_since() {
   214	        let s = decide_strategy(
   215	            &key(),
   216	            GateInputs {
   217	                coverage: Coverage::PartialUpTo(50),
   218	                capabilities: Some(RelayCapabilities {
   219	                    supports_nip77: false,
   220	                }),
   221	                watermark: Some(watermark(50, None)),
   222	            },
   223	        );
   224	        assert_eq!(s, SyncStrategy::ReqSince(51));
   225	    }
   226	
   227	    #[test]
   228	    fn unknown_coverage_with_no_watermark_req_from_zero() {
   229	        let s = decide_strategy(
   230	            &key(),
   231	            GateInputs {
   232	                coverage: Coverage::Unknown,
   233	                capabilities: None,
   234	                watermark: None,
   235	            },
   236	        );
   237	        assert_eq!(s, SyncStrategy::ReqSince(0));
   238	    }
   239	
   240	    #[test]
   241	    fn resume_blob_propagates_when_neg_preferred() {
   242	        let blob = vec![1u8, 2, 3];
   243	        let s = decide_strategy(
   244	            &key(),
   245	            GateInputs {
   246	                coverage: Coverage::PartialUpTo(50),
   247	                capabilities: Some(RelayCapabilities {
   248	                    supports_nip77: true,
   249	                }),
   250	                watermark: Some(watermark(50, Some(blob.clone()))),
   251	            },
   252	        );
   253	        match s {
   254	            SyncStrategy::Resume { next, state } => {
   255	                assert_eq!(*next, SyncStrategy::NegThenReq);
   256	                assert_eq!(state, blob);
   257	            }
   258	            other => panic!("expected Resume, got {other:?}"),
   259	        }
   260	    }
   261	
   262	    #[test]
   263	    fn freshness_ratio_collapses_complete_to_one() {
   264	        assert_eq!(freshness_ratio(Coverage::CompleteAsOf(1), 100), 1.0);
   265	        assert_eq!(freshness_ratio(Coverage::Unknown, 100), 0.0);
   266	        // PartialUpTo: 96 of "now=100" seconds → 0.96 recency.
   267	        let r = freshness_ratio(Coverage::PartialUpTo(96), 100);
   268	        assert!((r - 0.96).abs() < 1e-5, "expected ~0.96, got {r}");
   269	    }
   270	
   271	    #[test]
   272	    fn freshness_ratio_zero_guard_on_zero_now_or_zero_ts() {
   273	        // P3 corrected semantics — every degenerate input maps to 0.0, never
   274	        // panics or returns a >1.0 ratio.
   275	        assert_eq!(freshness_ratio(Coverage::PartialUpTo(100), 0), 0.0);
   276	        assert_eq!(freshness_ratio(Coverage::PartialUpTo(0), 100), 0.0);
   277	    }
   278	
   279	    #[test]
   280	    fn freshness_ratio_clamps_overshoot() {
   281	        // P3 regression — a misconfigured watermark (`ts > now`) must not
   282	        // report freshness > 1.0; the `CompleteAsOf` variant is the only
   283	        // path that earns the `1.0` authoritative claim.
   284	        let r = freshness_ratio(Coverage::PartialUpTo(200), 100);
   285	        assert_eq!(r, 1.0, "overshoot must clamp to 1.0, not {r}");
   286	    }
   287	}

One important report-class issue is emerging: the new hook exists and the C10 test installs it manually, but I don’t see production actor/kernel code installing `apply_coverage_filter`. I’ll keep that as report-only unless it turns out to be a docs wording problem rather than behavior.

     1	//! Kernel — the actor-owned event-processing core.
     2	//!
     3	//! Sub-modules:
     4	//! - `types`        — pure data types shared across the kernel
     5	//! - `ingest`       — relay frame parsing, event dispatch, and kind-specific ingest
     6	//! - `requests`     — relay state transitions, startup/view REQs, req/defer primitives
     7	//! - `status`       — diagnostics, metrics, and update-payload assembly
     8	//! - `update`       — diff/emit logic for the FFI update loop
     9	//! - `nostr`        — NostrEvent deserialization + helper functions
    10	//! - `test_support` — signature-free injection helpers (test / test-support feature)
    11	//! - `tests`        — unit tests (cfg(test) only)
    12	
    13	mod ingest;
    14	mod nostr;
    15	mod requests;
    16	mod status;
    17	#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-support"))]
    18	mod test_support;
    19	#[cfg(test)]
    20	mod tests;
    21	mod types;
    22	mod update;
    23	
    24	use crate::relay::{
    25	    OutboundMessage, RelayRole, CONTENT_RELAY_URL, DEFAULT_EMIT_HZ, FIATJAF_PUBKEY,
    26	    INDEXER_RELAY_URL, JB55_PUBKEY, TEST_NPUB, TEST_PUBKEY, TIMELINE_AUTHOR_LIMIT,
    27	};
    28	use chrono::{DateTime, Local};
    29	use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
    30	use serde_json::{json, Value};
    31	use std::collections::{BTreeSet, HashMap, HashSet, VecDeque};
    32	use std::time::{Duration, Instant, SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};
    33	use tungstenite::Message;
    34	
    35	use nostr::*;
    36	pub(crate) use nostr::{is_hex_id, is_hex_pubkey};
    37	
    38	use crate::store::{EventStore, MemEventStore};
    39	use types::*;
    40	
    41	/// The kernel owns all Nostr protocol state for the active app session.
    42	///
    43	/// It is driven by the actor loop in `crate::relay` through a simple message-
    44	/// passing interface: relay frames arrive via `handle_message`, view intents
    45	/// arrive via `open_*` / `close_*`, and the actor reads snapshots via `emit`.
    46	///
    47	/// The `EventStore` (`self.store`) is the single authoritative writer for all
    48	/// persisted events (D4).  The lightweight `events` read-cache is a derived
    49	/// projection populated only after the store confirms insertion or replacement.
    50	pub(crate) struct Kernel {
    51	    /// Pluggable event store. D4: the single writer for all Nostr events.
    52	    ///
    53	    /// `MemEventStore` by default; replace with `LmdbEventStore` in M3 phase 2.
    54	    store: Box<dyn EventStore>,
    55	    rev: u64,
    56	    visible_limit: usize,
    57	    started_at: Option<Instant>,
    58	    last_event_at: Option<Instant>,
    59	    first_event_at: Option<Instant>,
    60	    target_profile_loaded_at: Option<Instant>,
    61	    timeline_opened_at: Option<Instant>,
    62	    timeline_first_item_at: Option<Instant>,
    63	    relays: HashMap<RelayRole, RelayHealth>,
    64	    profiles: HashMap<String, Profile>,
    65	    events: HashMap<String, StoredEvent>,
    66	    timeline: VecDeque<String>,
    67	    selected_author: Option<ViewInterest>,
    68	    author_request_pending: bool,
    69	    author_view_seq: u64,
    70	    selected_thread: Option<ViewInterest>,
    71	    thread_request_pending: bool,
    72	    thread_view_seq: u64,
    73	    diagnostic_firehose: Option<ViewInterest>,
    74	    diagnostic_firehose_seq: u64,
    75	    diagnostic_firehose_events: u64,
    76	    pending_thread_ids: BTreeSet<String>,
    77	    requested_thread_ids: HashSet<String>,
    78	    thread_ids_inflight: bool,
    79	    pending_thread_reply_targets: BTreeSet<String>,
    80	    requested_thread_reply_targets: HashSet<String>,
    81	    thread_replies_inflight: bool,
    82	    deferred_outbound: VecDeque<OutboundMessage>,
    83	    seed_contacts: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>,
    84	    author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>,
    85	    timeline_authors: BTreeSet<String>,
    86	    profile_claims: HashMap<String, BTreeSet<String>>,
    87	    requested_profiles: HashSet<String>,
    88	    pending_profiles: BTreeSet<String>,
    89	    profile_req_seq: u64,
    90	    timeline_requested: bool,
    91	    contacts_deadline: Option<Instant>,
    92	    wire_subs: HashMap<String, WireSub>,
    93	    last_emitted_items: Vec<TimelineItem>,
    94	    update_sequence: u64,
    95	    events_since_last_update: u64,
    96	    max_event_to_emit_ms: u128,
    97	    max_events_per_update: u64,
    98	    changed_since_emit: bool,
    99	    logs: VecDeque<String>,
   100	}
   101	
   102	impl Kernel {
   103	    pub(crate) fn new(visible_limit: usize) -> Self {
   104	        Self {
   105	            store: Box::new(MemEventStore::new()),
   106	            rev: 0,
   107	            visible_limit,
   108	            started_at: None,
   109	            last_event_at: None,
   110	            first_event_at: None,
   111	            target_profile_loaded_at: None,
   112	            timeline_opened_at: None,
   113	            timeline_first_item_at: None,
   114	            relays: RelayRole::all()
   115	                .into_iter()
   116	                .map(|role| (role, RelayHealth::default()))
   117	                .collect(),
   118	            profiles: HashMap::new(),
   119	            events: HashMap::new(),
   120	            timeline: VecDeque::new(),
   121	            selected_author: None,
   122	            author_request_pending: false,
   123	            author_view_seq: 0,
   124	            selected_thread: None,
   125	            thread_request_pending: false,
   126	            thread_view_seq: 0,
   127	            diagnostic_firehose: None,
   128	            diagnostic_firehose_seq: 0,
   129	            diagnostic_firehose_events: 0,
   130	            pending_thread_ids: BTreeSet::new(),
   131	            requested_thread_ids: HashSet::new(),
   132	            thread_ids_inflight: false,
   133	            pending_thread_reply_targets: BTreeSet::new(),
   134	            requested_thread_reply_targets: HashSet::new(),
   135	            thread_replies_inflight: false,
   136	            deferred_outbound: VecDeque::new(),
   137	            seed_contacts: HashMap::new(),
   138	            author_relay_lists: HashMap::new(),
   139	            timeline_authors: BTreeSet::new(),
   140	            profile_claims: HashMap::new(),
   141	            requested_profiles: HashSet::new(),
   142	            pending_profiles: BTreeSet::new(),
   143	            profile_req_seq: 0,
   144	            timeline_requested: false,
   145	            contacts_deadline: None,
   146	            wire_subs: HashMap::new(),
   147	            last_emitted_items: Vec::new(),
   148	            update_sequence: 0,
   149	            events_since_last_update: 0,
   150	            max_event_to_emit_ms: 0,
   151	            max_events_per_update: 0,
   152	            changed_since_emit: true,
   153	            logs: VecDeque::new(),
   154	        }
   155	    }
   156	
   157	    pub(crate) fn start(&mut self) {
   158	        if self.started_at.is_none() {
   159	            self.started_at = Some(Instant::now());
   160	        }
   161	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
   162	        self.log("starting role-aware nmp demo slice");
   163	    }
   164	
   165	    pub(crate) fn set_visible_limit(&mut self, limit: usize) {
   166	        if self.visible_limit != limit {
   167	            self.visible_limit = limit;
   168	            self.changed_since_emit = true;
   169	        }
   170	    }
   171	
   172	    pub(crate) fn visible_limit(&self) -> usize {
   173	        self.visible_limit
   174	    }
   175	
   176	    pub(crate) fn changed_since_emit(&self) -> bool {
   177	        self.changed_since_emit
   178	    }
   179	}

    "crates/nmp-nip29",
    "crates/nmp-nip42",
    "crates/nmp-nip77",
    "crates/nmp-signers",
    "crates/nmp-testing",
]
resolver = "2"

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
license = "MIT"
version = "0.1.0"
[package]
name = "nmp-core"
version.workspace = true
edition.workspace = true
license.workspace = true

[lib]
name = "nmp_core"
crate-type = ["staticlib", "rlib"]

[features]
# Expose actor internals for live-bench harnesses and integration test binaries.
# Never enable in production builds.
test-support = []
# Enable the LMDB-backed EventStore (requires heed crate — not yet wired in M3 phase 1).
lmdb-backend = []

[dependencies]
chrono = { version = "0.4", default-features = false, features = ["clock"] }
nostr = "0.44"
rustls = { version = "0.23", default-features = false, features = ["ring"] }
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1.0"
tungstenite = { version = "0.24", default-features = false, features = ["handshake", "rustls-tls-webpki-roots"] }
[package]
name = "nmp-nip77"
version.workspace = true
edition.workspace = true
license.workspace = true

[lib]
name = "nmp_nip77"

[dependencies]
nmp-core = { path = "../nmp-core" }
negentropy = { version = "0.5", default-features = false, features = ["std"] }
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1.0"

[dev-dependencies]
nmp-core = { path = "../nmp-core", features = ["test-support"] }
[package]
name = "nmp-testing"
version.workspace = true
edition.workspace = true
license.workspace = true

[features]
# Enable LMDB-backed store tests. Passes through to nmp-core's lmdb-backend feature.
lmdb-backend = ["nmp-core/lmdb-backend"]

[dependencies]
nmp-core = { path = "../nmp-core", features = ["test-support"] }
nmp-nip77 = { path = "../nmp-nip77" }
paste = "1.0"
serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1.0"

[dev-dependencies]
nmp-core = { path = "../nmp-core" }

[[bin]]
name = "reactivity-bench"
path = "bin/reactivity-bench/main.rs"

[[bin]]
name = "firehose-bench"
path = "bin/firehose-bench/main.rs"

# M10.5 FFI hardening harness — S1..S5 (phase 1)
# Links nmp_app_* FFI symbols via the Rust rlib dependency with test-support
# feature; exercises the identical code paths Swift uses via extern "C" ABI.
[[bin]]
name = "ffi-stress"
path = "bin/ffi-stress/main.rs"

# M2 subscription compiler audit gate — Assertions 1–5 (phase 1 subset).
# `cargo test -p nmp-testing --test m2_subscription_compilation_audit`
[[test]]
name = "m2_subscription_compilation_audit"
path = "tests/m2_subscription_compilation_audit.rs"

# M8-subs — subscription lifecycle integration tests (Task #46).
# Pins the seam between M2's CompiledPlan and the wire: registry, trigger
# inbox, wire-emitter, connection pool. Eight tests defined in
# `docs/plan/m8-subscription-lifecycle.md` §6.
# `cargo test -p nmp-testing --test m8_subscription_lifecycle`
[[test]]
name = "m8_subscription_lifecycle"
path = "tests/m8_subscription_lifecycle.rs"

# Framework magic contract — 13 behaviour tests (C1-C13) + coverage meta-test.
# Cross-cutting; intentionally not milestone-prefixed.
# `cargo test -p nmp-testing --test framework_magic_contract`
[[test]]
name = "framework_magic_contract"
path = "tests/framework_magic_contract.rs"

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs | sed -n '1,540p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a57091c921169bd11
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! Framework Magic Contract — cross-cutting test suite.
     2	//!
     3	//! 14 tests: C1–C13 behaviour tests + 1 coverage meta-test.
     4	//! Intentionally NOT milestone-prefixed; see
     5	//! `docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md` §1.
     6	//!
     7	//! Active on master: C1, C2, C3, C4, C6, C9, C10, meta-test (8 of 14).
     8	//! Ignored pending milestone implementation:
     9	//!   C5  `#[ignore = "pending M2 FollowListChanged trigger"]`
    10	//!   C7  `#[ignore = "pending M2 PublishPlanner + M6 SendNote consumer"]`
    11	//!   C8  `#[ignore = "pending M2 wire-emitter auto-close + buffer"]`
    12	//!   C11 `#[ignore = "pending M6 signers + KeyringCapability"]`
    13	//!   C12 `#[ignore = "pending M8 multi-account state machine"]`
    14	//!   C13 `#[ignore = "pending M2 projection cache + ViewModule surface"]`
    15	//!
    16	//! Invocation: `cargo test -p nmp-testing --test framework_magic_contract`
    17	
    18	use nmp_core::planner::{
    19	    InMemoryMailboxCache, InterestId, InterestLifecycle, InterestScope, InterestShape,
    20	    LogicalInterest, MailboxSnapshot, SubscriptionCompiler,
    21	};
    22	use nmp_core::store::{InsertOutcome, TombstoneOrigin};
    23	use nmp_testing::store_harness::{StoreHarness, ALICE_HEX, BOB_HEX};
    24	
    25	// ── Shared helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	
    27	fn pubkey(seed: &str) -> String {
    28	    format!("{seed:0>64}").chars().take(64).collect::<String>().to_lowercase()
    29	}
    30	
    31	fn relay(url: &str) -> String { url.to_string() }
    32	
    33	fn interest_id(n: u64) -> InterestId { InterestId(n) }
    34	
    35	fn mailbox_write(relays: &[&str]) -> MailboxSnapshot {
    36	    MailboxSnapshot {
    37	        write_relays: relays.iter().map(|s| relay(s)).collect(),
    38	        read_relays: vec![],
    39	        both_relays: vec![],
    40	    }
    41	}
    42	
    43	// ── C1 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    44	
    45	/// C1: Replaceable-event supersession (kind 0 / 3 / 10000-19999) on insert.
    46	/// Design: `docs/design/framework-magic/replaceable.md` §C1.
    47	#[test]
    48	fn c1_replaceable_supersedes_on_insert() {
    49	    let h = StoreHarness::mem();
    50	
    51	    let ev1 = h.make_event(ALICE_HEX, 0, 1_000);
    52	    let id1 = ev1.id_bytes();
    53	    assert!(matches!(h.insert_raw(ev1, "wss://r1/", 1_000_000), InsertOutcome::Inserted { .. }));
    54	    h.assert_present(&id1);
    55	
    56	    let ev2 = h.make_event(ALICE_HEX, 0, 2_000);
    57	    let id2 = ev2.id_bytes();
    58	    let o2 = h.insert_raw(ev2, "wss://r1/", 2_000_000);
    59	    assert!(matches!(o2, InsertOutcome::Replaced { .. }), "newer must replace: {o2:?}");
    60	    h.assert_absent(&id1);
    61	    h.assert_present(&id2);
    62	
    63	    let ev_stale = h.make_event(ALICE_HEX, 0, 500);
    64	    let id_stale = ev_stale.id_bytes();
    65	    let o_stale = h.insert_raw(ev_stale, "wss://r2/", 3_000_000);
    66	    assert!(matches!(o_stale, InsertOutcome::Superseded { .. }), "stale: {o_stale:?}");
    67	    h.assert_absent(&id_stale);
    68	    h.assert_present(&id2);
    69	
    70	    // Tie-break at same created_at: lexicographically smaller id wins.
    71	    // Use BOB_HEX so we don't conflict with Alice's kind:0 slot.
    72	    let id_large = "f".repeat(64);
    73	    let id_small = "0".repeat(64);
    74	    let ev_large = h.make_event_with_id(&id_large, BOB_HEX, 0, 5_000);
    75	    let large_id_bytes = ev_large.id_bytes();
    76	    h.insert_raw(ev_large, "wss://r1/", 5_000_000);
    77	    h.assert_present(&large_id_bytes);
    78	
    79	    let ev_small = h.make_event_with_id(&id_small, BOB_HEX, 0, 5_000);
    80	    let small_id_bytes = ev_small.id_bytes();
    81	    let o_small = h.insert_raw(ev_small, "wss://r1/", 5_000_001);
    82	    assert!(
    83	        matches!(o_small, InsertOutcome::Replaced { .. }),
    84	        "smaller id must replace larger at same timestamp: {o_small:?}"
    85	    );
    86	    h.assert_present(&small_id_bytes);
    87	    h.assert_absent(&large_id_bytes);
    88	}
    89	
    90	// ── C2 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    91	
    92	/// C2: Parameterized replaceable supersession (kind 30000-39999) by
    93	/// `(pubkey, kind, d-tag)`. Different d-tags coexist; same d-tag supersedes.
    94	/// Design: `docs/design/framework-magic/replaceable.md` §C2.
    95	#[test]
    96	fn c2_parameterized_replaceable_supersedes_by_dtag() {
    97	    use nmp_testing::store_harness::ALICE_PUBKEY;
    98	    let h = StoreHarness::mem();
    99	
   100	    let ev1 = h.make_event_with_tags(ALICE_HEX, 30_023, 1_000, vec![
   101	        vec!["d".to_string(), "foo".to_string()],
   102	    ]);
   103	    let id1 = ev1.id_bytes();
   104	    h.insert_raw(ev1, "wss://t/", 1_000_000);
   105	    let ev2 = h.make_event_with_tags(ALICE_HEX, 30_023, 2_000, vec![
   106	        vec!["d".to_string(), "foo".to_string()],
   107	    ]);
   108	    let id2 = ev2.id_bytes();
   109	    let o2 = h.insert_raw(ev2, "wss://t/", 2_000_000);
   110	    assert!(matches!(o2, InsertOutcome::Replaced { .. }), "newer d=foo must replace: {o2:?}");
   111	    h.assert_absent(&id1);
   112	    h.assert_present(&id2);
   113	
   114	    let ev_bar = h.make_event_with_tags(ALICE_HEX, 30_023, 1_000, vec![
   115	        vec!["d".to_string(), "bar".to_string()],
   116	    ]);
   117	    let id_bar = ev_bar.id_bytes();
   118	    h.insert_raw(ev_bar, "wss://t/", 1_000_000);
   119	    h.assert_present(&id2);
   120	    h.assert_present(&id_bar);
   121	    let foo = h.store.get_param_replaceable(&ALICE_PUBKEY, 30_023, b"foo").unwrap();
   122	    let bar = h.store.get_param_replaceable(&ALICE_PUBKEY, 30_023, b"bar").unwrap();
   123	    assert_eq!(foo.unwrap().raw.id_bytes(), id2, "foo slot must hold v2");
   124	    assert_eq!(bar.unwrap().raw.id_bytes(), id_bar, "bar slot must be independent");
   125	
   126	    let ev_24 = h.make_event_with_tags(ALICE_HEX, 30_024, 1_000, vec![
   127	        vec!["d".to_string(), "foo".to_string()],
   128	    ]);
   129	    let id_24 = ev_24.id_bytes();
   130	    h.insert_raw(ev_24, "wss://t/", 1_000_000);
   131	    h.assert_present(&id2);
   132	    h.assert_present(&id_24);
   133	    let r24 = h.store.get_param_replaceable(&ALICE_PUBKEY, 30_024, b"foo").unwrap();
   134	    assert_eq!(r24.unwrap().raw.id_bytes(), id_24, "kind:30024 slot is independent");
   135	}
   136	
   137	// ── C3 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   138	
   139	/// C3: Kind:5 delete — referenced events removed, tombstone persisted.
   140	/// Cross-author kind:5 has no effect.
   141	/// Design: `docs/design/framework-magic/replaceable.md` §C3.
   142	#[test]
   143	fn c3_kind5_delete_removes_referenced_and_tombstones() {
   144	    let h = StoreHarness::mem();
   145	
   146	    let kind1 = h.make_event(ALICE_HEX, 1, 1_000);
   147	    let kind1_id = kind1.id_bytes();
   148	    let kind1_id_hex = kind1.id.clone();
   149	    let kind1_clone = kind1.clone();
   150	    h.insert_raw(kind1, "wss://r1/", 1_000_000);
   151	    h.assert_present(&kind1_id);
   152	
   153	    let kind5 = h.make_event_with_tags(ALICE_HEX, 5, 2_000, vec![
   154	        vec!["e".to_string(), kind1_id_hex],
   155	    ]);
   156	    h.insert_raw(kind5, "wss://r1/", 2_000_000);
   157	    h.assert_absent(&kind1_id);
   158	    h.assert_tombstoned(&kind1_id);
   159	    assert_eq!(h.store.tombstones_for(&kind1_id).unwrap()[0].origin, TombstoneOrigin::Kind5);
   160	
   161	    let o_rein = h.insert_raw(kind1_clone, "wss://r2/", 3_000_000);
   162	    assert!(
   163	        matches!(o_rein, InsertOutcome::Tombstoned { origin: TombstoneOrigin::Kind5, .. }),
   164	        "reinsert must be Tombstoned: {o_rein:?}"
   165	    );
   166	    h.assert_absent(&kind1_id);
   167	
   168	    // Bob's kind:5 on Alice's event must be a no-op (cross-author delete forbidden).
   169	    let alice_ev2 = h.make_event(ALICE_HEX, 1, 3_000);
   170	    let alice_ev2_id = alice_ev2.id_bytes();
   171	    let alice_ev2_hex = alice_ev2.id.clone();
   172	    h.insert_raw(alice_ev2, "wss://r1/", 3_000_000);
   173	    h.insert_raw(
   174	        h.make_event_with_tags(BOB_HEX, 5, 4_000, vec![vec!["e".to_string(), alice_ev2_hex]]),
   175	        "wss://r1/",
   176	        4_000_000,
   177	    );
   178	    h.assert_present(&alice_ev2_id); // Bob cannot delete Alice's events
   179	}
   180	
   181	// ── C4 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   182	
   183	/// C4: NIP-40 expiration — expired-on-arrival rejected; GC reaps with tombstone;
   184	/// tombstone blocks re-insert.
   185	/// Design: `docs/design/framework-magic/replaceable.md` §C4.
   186	#[test]
   187	fn c4_nip40_expiration_removes_and_persists_schedule() {
   188	    use nmp_core::store::{GcBudget, RejectReason};
   189	    let h = StoreHarness::mem();
   190	
   191	    let ev_past = h.make_event_with_tags(ALICE_HEX, 1, 1_000, vec![
   192	        vec!["expiration".to_string(), "999".to_string()],
   193	    ]);
   194	    let past_id = ev_past.id_bytes();
   195	    let o = h.insert_raw(ev_past, "wss://t/", 1_700_000_000_000u64);
   196	    assert!(
   197	        matches!(o, InsertOutcome::Rejected { reason: RejectReason::ExpiredOnArrival, .. }),
   198	        "expired on arrival must be rejected: {o:?}"
   199	    );
   200	    h.assert_absent(&past_id);
   201	
   202	    // Insert with already-past expiration (unix second 2) and early received_at.
   203	    let ev = h.make_event_with_tags(ALICE_HEX, 1, 1u64, vec![
   204	        vec!["expiration".to_string(), "2".to_string()],
   205	    ]);
   206	    let ev_id = ev.id_bytes();
   207	    let ev_clone = ev.clone();
   208	    h.insert_raw(ev, "wss://t/", 1u64);
   209	    h.assert_present(&ev_id);
   210	
   211	    let expiring: Vec<_> = h.store
   212	        .scan_expiring_before(12, 100).unwrap()
   213	        .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>().unwrap();
   214	    assert!(expiring.iter().any(|e| e.raw.id_bytes() == ev_id));
   215	
   216	    let report = h.store.gc_step(GcBudget { max_events_per_step: 100, max_duration_ms: 1_000 }).unwrap();
   217	    assert!(report.expired_reaped >= 1, "gc_step must reap the expired event");
   218	    h.assert_absent(&ev_id);
   219	    let tombs = h.store.tombstones_for(&ev_id).unwrap();
   220	    assert!(!tombs.is_empty() && tombs[0].origin == TombstoneOrigin::NIP40Expiry);
   221	
   222	    let o_rein = h.insert_raw(ev_clone, "wss://r2/", 1u64);
   223	    assert!(
   224	        matches!(
   225	            o_rein,
   226	            InsertOutcome::Tombstoned { origin: TombstoneOrigin::NIP40Expiry, .. }
   227	                | InsertOutcome::Rejected { .. }
   228	        ),
   229	        "re-insert after NIP40 expiry must be blocked: {o_rein:?}"
   230	    );
   231	}
   232	
   233	// ── C5 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   234	
   235	/// C5: Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles
   236	/// every dependent subscription transparently.
   237	/// Design: `docs/design/framework-magic/kind3.md`
   238	#[test]
   239	#[ignore = "pending M2 FollowListChanged trigger + FollowingTimelineView compiler integration"]
   240	fn c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs() {
   241	    unimplemented!("M2: FollowListChanged trigger must fire and cause delta CLOSE/REQ");
   242	}
   243	
   244	// ── C6 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   245	
   246	/// C6: Outbox read routing — `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those
   247	/// authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated; plan-id stable under re-compile.
   248	/// Design: `docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md` §C6.
   249	#[test]
   250	fn c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays() {
   251	    let alice = pubkey("alice");
   252	    let bob = pubkey("bob");
   253	    let carol = pubkey("carol");
   254	    let mut cache = InMemoryMailboxCache::new();
   255	    cache.put(alice.clone(), mailbox_write(&["wss://r1/", "wss://r2/"]));
   256	    cache.put(bob.clone(), mailbox_write(&["wss://r2/", "wss://r3/"]));
   257	    cache.put(carol.clone(), mailbox_write(&["wss://r3/"]));
   258	    // Bind to a local so the slice reference outlives the compiler call.
   259	    let indexers = vec![relay("wss://purplepag.es")];
   260	    let compiler = SubscriptionCompiler::new(&cache, &indexers);
   261	
   262	    let mk = |n: u64, authors: Vec<String>| LogicalInterest {
   263	        id: interest_id(n),
   264	        scope: InterestScope::ActiveAccount,
   265	        shape: InterestShape {
   266	            authors: authors.into_iter().collect(),
   267	            kinds: [1u32, 6u32].into_iter().collect(),
   268	            ..Default::default()
   269	        },
   270	        hints: vec![],
   271	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::Tailing,
   272	    };
   273	
   274	    let plan = compiler
   275	        .compile(&[mk(1, vec![alice.clone(), bob.clone(), carol.clone()])])
   276	        .expect("compile");
   277	
   278	    let rs: std::collections::BTreeSet<_> = plan.per_relay.keys().cloned().collect();
   279	    assert!(rs.contains("wss://r1/") && rs.contains("wss://r2/") && rs.contains("wss://r3/"));
   280	    assert!(!rs.contains("wss://purplepag.es"), "indexer must not appear for known authors");
   281	    for (url, rp) in &plan.per_relay {
   282	        assert_eq!(rp.sub_shapes.len(), 1, "relay {url} must have one merged sub-shape");
   283	    }
   284	    let r1 = &plan.per_relay["wss://r1/"].sub_shapes[0].shape.authors;
   285	    assert!(r1.contains(&alice) && !r1.contains(&carol));
   286	    let r3 = &plan.per_relay["wss://r3/"].sub_shapes[0].shape.authors;
   287	    assert!(r3.contains(&bob) && r3.contains(&carol) && !r3.contains(&alice));
   288	
   289	    let plan2 = compiler
   290	        .compile(&[mk(1, vec![alice, bob, carol])])
   291	        .expect("compile #2");
   292	    assert_eq!(plan.plan_id, plan2.plan_id, "plan_id must be stable under identical inputs");
   293	}
   294	
   295	// ── C7 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   296	
   297	/// C7: Write fan-out — publish routes to outbox + recipient-inbox; private events
   298	/// fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown.
   299	/// Design: `docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md` §C7.
   300	#[test]
   301	#[ignore = "pending M2 PublishPlanner trait + M6 SendNote consumer"]
   302	fn c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed() {
   303	    unimplemented!("M6: PublishPlanner must route public/notification/private events correctly");
   304	}
   305	
   306	// ── C8 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   307	
   308	/// C8: Subscriptions auto-dedup, auto-coalesce, auto-close, and auto-buffer.
   309	/// Design: `docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md`
   310	#[test]
   311	#[ignore = "pending M2 wire-emitter auto-close + actor-tick buffer infrastructure"]
   312	fn c8_subscriptions_coalesce_autoclose_and_buffer() {
   313	    unimplemented!("M2: compiler + wire-emitter + scheduler all required for full C8");
   314	}
   315	
   316	// ── C9 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   317	
   318	/// C9: Provenance preserved — same event id from N relays merges into one record
   319	/// with N-entry provenance set; signature and id byte-stable.
   320	/// Design: `docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md` §C9.
   321	#[test]
   322	fn c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries() {
   323	    let h = StoreHarness::mem();
   324	    let ev = h.make_event(ALICE_HEX, 1, 1_000_000);
   325	    let id = ev.id_bytes();
   326	    let ev2 = ev.clone();
   327	    let ev3 = ev.clone();
   328	
   329	    assert!(matches!(h.insert_raw(ev, "wss://r1/", 1_000), InsertOutcome::Inserted { .. }));
   330	    let prov1 = h.store.provenance_for(&id).unwrap();
   331	    assert_eq!(prov1.len(), 1);
   332	    assert_eq!(prov1.iter().find(|e| e.primary).unwrap().relay_url, "wss://r1/");
   333	
   334	    let o2 = h.insert_raw(ev2, "wss://r2/", 5_000);
   335	    assert!(
   336	        matches!(o2, InsertOutcome::Duplicate { sources_after: 2, .. }),
   337	        "second relay must produce Duplicate: {o2:?}"
   338	    );
   339	    let prov2 = h.store.provenance_for(&id).unwrap();
   340	    assert_eq!(prov2.len(), 2);
   341	    assert_eq!(prov2.iter().find(|e| e.primary).unwrap().relay_url, "wss://r1/");
   342	
   343	    let o3 = h.insert_raw(ev3, "wss://r1/", 60_000);
   344	    assert!(
   345	        matches!(o3, InsertOutcome::Duplicate { sources_after: 2, .. }),
   346	        "same-relay re-delivery must not add a third provenance entry: {o3:?}"
   347	    );
   348	    let prov3 = h.store.provenance_for(&id).unwrap();
   349	    assert_eq!(prov3.len(), 2);
   350	    let r1 = prov3.iter().find(|e| e.relay_url == "wss://r1/").unwrap();
   351	    assert_eq!(r1.first_seen_ms, 1_000);
   352	    assert_eq!(r1.last_seen_ms, 60_000);
   353	}
   354	
   355	// ── C10 ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   356	//
   357	// C10's body lives in the sibling test binary `framework_magic_c10.rs` so the
   358	// wired SubscriptionLifecycle proof can grow without breaking AGENTS.md's
   359	// 500 LOC ceiling on this file. The contract meta-test below keys off the
   360	// `framework-magic.md` doc table, not file location — splitting is invisible
   361	// at the contract layer.
   362	//
   363	// Test binary: `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_c10.rs`
   364	// Test name:   `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss`

   389	/// uses defined placeholders; authoritative data refines in place.

   396	
   397	// ── Coverage meta-test ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   398	
   399	/// `contract_surface_complete` — asserts structural correspondence between this
   400	/// file and `docs/design/framework-magic.md`'s contract table.
   401	///
   402	/// Drift classes caught:
   403	/// 1. Doc table gains a row but this file does not grow a `#[test] fn`.
   404	/// 2. This file gains a `#[test] fn` but the doc table does not list it.
   405	/// 3. A renamed test breaks the doc-test correspondence.
   406	///
   407	/// Design: `docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md` §4.
   408	#[test]
   409	fn contract_surface_complete() {
   410	    const EXPECTED_TESTS: &[&str] = &[
   411	        "c1_replaceable_supersedes_on_insert",
   412	        "c2_parameterized_replaceable_supersedes_by_dtag",
   413	        "c3_kind5_delete_removes_referenced_and_tombstones",
   414	        "c4_nip40_expiration_removes_and_persists_schedule",
   415	        "c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs",
   416	        "c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays",
   417	        "c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed",
   418	        "c8_subscriptions_coalesce_autoclose_and_buffer",
   419	        "c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries",
   420	        "c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss",
   421	        "c11_bunker_url_and_nsec_creation_complete_via_actions",
   422	        "c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance",
   423	        "c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place",
   424	    ];
   425	
   426	    // Parse test names from the contract table in framework-magic.md.
   427	    // Table row format: `| # | Behavior | Sub-file | Test name | Milestone | Doctrine |`
   428	    let doc = include_str!("../../../docs/design/framework-magic.md");
   429	    let doc_test_names: Vec<String> = doc
   430	        .lines()
   431	        .filter(|l| l.starts_with("| C") || l.starts_with("| c"))
   432	        .filter_map(|l| {
   433	            let cols: Vec<&str> = l.split('|').collect();
   434	            if cols.len() >= 5 {
   435	                let name = cols[4].trim();
   436	                if name.starts_with('`') && name.ends_with('`') {
   437	                    Some(name[1..name.len() - 1].to_string())
   438	                } else {
   439	                    None
   440	                }
   441	            } else {
   442	                None
   443	            }
   444	        })
   445	        .collect();
   446	
   447	    for doc_name in &doc_test_names {
   448	        assert!(
   449	            EXPECTED_TESTS.contains(&doc_name.as_str()),
   450	            "contract doc lists '{}' not in EXPECTED_TESTS",
   451	            doc_name
   452	        );
   453	    }
   454	    for expected in EXPECTED_TESTS {
   455	        assert!(
   456	            doc_test_names.iter().any(|n| n == expected),
   457	            "EXPECTED_TESTS lists '{}' not in the contract doc table",
   458	            expected
   459	        );
   460	    }
   461	    assert_eq!(
   462	        doc_test_names.len(),
   463	        EXPECTED_TESTS.len(),
   464	        "doc table has {} names, EXPECTED_TESTS has {} — must agree",
   465	        doc_test_names.len(),
   466	        EXPECTED_TESTS.len()
   467	    );
   468	}
