 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! Wire-emitter — `CompiledPlan` → `Vec<WireFrame>` diff.
     2	//!
     3	//! Given a prior plan and a next plan, computes the minimum set of `REQ` and
     4	//! `CLOSE` frames that transitions the wire from the prior to the next. Per
     5	//! recompilation.md §4.3 idempotence contract: `plan_diff(P, P)` returns an
     6	//! empty vector.
     7	//!
     8	//! ## Sub-id stability
     9	//!
    10	//! `sub_id_for` derives a stable wire sub-id from `(plan_id, shape's
    11	//! canonical_filter_hash)`. The same shape appearing in two consecutive plans
    12	//! gets the same sub-id and is therefore a no-op in the diff; a shape that
    13	//! drops out produces a CLOSE; a shape that appears produces a REQ.
    14	//!
    15	//! ## D8 cost shape
    16	//!
    17	//! `plan_diff` is O(N_prior + N_next) where N is the number of `SubShape`s.
    18	//! No per-event allocation; only one `BTreeSet` and one `Vec::with_capacity`.
    19	
    20	use std::collections::BTreeSet;
    21	
    22	use crate::planner::{
    23	    CompiledPlan, InterestId, InterestLifecycle, InterestShape, LogicalInterest, RelayUrl,
    24	    SubShape,
    25	};
    26	
    27	/// A frame to push onto the wire.
    28	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
    29	pub enum WireFrame {
    30	    /// `["REQ", sub_id, filter]` for the given relay.
    31	    Req {
    32	        relay_url: RelayUrl,
    33	        sub_id: String,
    34	        filter_json: String,
    35	        interest_id: InterestId,
    36	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle,
    37	    },
    38	    /// `["CLOSE", sub_id]` for the given relay.
    39	    Close {
    40	        relay_url: RelayUrl,
    41	        sub_id: String,
    42	    },
    43	}
    44	
    45	/// Compute the wire-frame delta between `prior` and `next` plans.
    46	///
    47	/// Both arguments are `Option<&CompiledPlan>` so the same function handles
    48	/// the initial-compile case (prior = None → all REQs) and the teardown case
    49	/// (next = None → all CLOSEs). `next_interests` is consulted to determine
    50	/// lifecycle metadata for the REQ frames.
    51	pub fn plan_diff(
    52	    prior: Option<&CompiledPlan>,
    53	    next: Option<&CompiledPlan>,
    54	    next_interests: &[LogicalInterest],
    55	) -> Vec<WireFrame> {
    56	    let prior_sub_ids = collect_sub_ids(prior);
    57	    let next_sub_ids = collect_sub_ids(next);
    58	
    59	    let mut frames = Vec::new();
    60	
    61	    // CLOSE for sub_ids in prior but not in next.
    62	    if let Some(plan) = prior {
    63	        for (relay_url, relay_plan) in &plan.per_relay {
    64	            for shape in &relay_plan.sub_shapes {
    65	                let sub_id = sub_id_for(&plan.plan_id, shape);
    66	                if !next_sub_ids.contains(&sub_id) {
    67	                    frames.push(WireFrame::Close {
    68	                        relay_url: relay_url.clone(),
    69	                        sub_id,
    70	                    });
    71	                }
    72	            }
    73	        }
    74	    }
    75	
    76	    // REQ for sub_ids in next but not in prior.
    77	    if let Some(plan) = next {
    78	        for (relay_url, relay_plan) in &plan.per_relay {
    79	            for shape in &relay_plan.sub_shapes {
    80	                let sub_id = sub_id_for(&plan.plan_id, shape);
    81	                if !prior_sub_ids.contains(&sub_id) {
    82	                    frames.push(emit_req(relay_url.clone(), shape, next_interests, sub_id));
    83	                }
    84	            }
    85	        }
    86	    }
    87	
    88	    frames
    89	}
    90	
    91	fn collect_sub_ids(plan: Option<&CompiledPlan>) -> BTreeSet<String> {
    92	    let mut out = BTreeSet::new();
    93	    if let Some(plan) = plan {
    94	        for relay_plan in plan.per_relay.values() {
    95	            for shape in &relay_plan.sub_shapes {
    96	                out.insert(sub_id_for(&plan.plan_id, shape));
    97	            }
    98	        }
    99	    }
   100	    out
   101	}
   102	
   103	fn emit_req(
   104	    relay_url: RelayUrl,
   105	    shape: &SubShape,
   106	    interests: &[LogicalInterest],
   107	    sub_id: String,
   108	) -> WireFrame {
   109	    let interest_id = shape
   110	        .originating_interests
   111	        .first()
   112	        .cloned()
   113	        .unwrap_or(InterestId(0));
   114	    let lifecycle = lifecycle_for_shape(shape, interests);
   115	    let filter_json = filter_json_for(&shape.shape);
   116	    WireFrame::Req {
   117	        relay_url,
   118	        sub_id,
   119	        filter_json,
   120	        interest_id,
   121	        lifecycle,
   122	    }
   123	}
   124	
   125	/// Derive a stable wire sub-id for `(plan_id, shape)`. Two identical shapes
   126	/// in consecutive plans get the same sub-id — the diff treats them as a
   127	/// no-op. A shape merging differently across recompiles gets a new sub-id
   128	/// because the underlying `canonical_filter_hash` would change.
   129	///
   130	/// We deliberately do NOT include the plan_id in the sub-id — that would
   131	/// force every shape to be CLOSE+REQ'd on every plan-id change, defeating
   132	/// the diff. Instead, the sub-id is derived purely from the shape's hash.
   133	pub fn sub_id_for(_plan_id: &str, shape: &SubShape) -> String {
   134	    format!("sub-{}", shape.canonical_filter_hash)
   135	}
   136	
   137	/// Determine the lifecycle to apply to a merged sub-shape.
   138	///
   139	/// Rule 6 of the lattice (`lattice::rules::rule6_lifecycle_equality`) refuses
   140	/// to merge shapes with different lifecycles, so all originating interests
   141	/// share one lifecycle. We pick the first originating interest's lifecycle;
   142	/// fallback to `Tailing` if the originating set is empty (defensive).
   143	pub fn lifecycle_for_shape(
   144	    shape: &SubShape,
   145	    interests: &[LogicalInterest],
   146	) -> InterestLifecycle {
   147	    for origin in &shape.originating_interests {
   148	        if let Some(i) = interests.iter().find(|i| &i.id == origin) {
   149	            return i.lifecycle.clone();
   150	        }
   151	    }
   152	    InterestLifecycle::Tailing
   153	}
   154	
   155	/// Serialise an `InterestShape` into the Nostr filter JSON object form.
   156	///
   157	/// This is a minimal serializer — only the fields a relay accepts. Internal
   158	/// fields like `pin_to` and `addresses` are translated to wire-equivalent
   159	/// forms (`#a` tags for addresses) or omitted (pin_to is client-side only).
   160	pub fn filter_json_for(shape: &InterestShape) -> String {
   161	    let mut parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   162	    if !shape.authors.is_empty() {
   163	        let arr: Vec<String> = shape.authors.iter().map(|a| format!("\"{a}\"")).collect();
   164	        parts.push(format!("\"authors\":[{}]", arr.join(",")));
   165	    }
   166	    if !shape.kinds.is_empty() {
   167	        let arr: Vec<String> = shape.kinds.iter().map(|k| k.to_string()).collect();
   168	        parts.push(format!("\"kinds\":[{}]", arr.join(",")));
   169	    }
   170	    if !shape.event_ids.is_empty() {
   171	        let arr: Vec<String> = shape.event_ids.iter().map(|e| format!("\"{e}\"")).collect();
   172	        parts.push(format!("\"ids\":[{}]", arr.join(",")));
   173	    }
   174	    for (tag_key, values) in &shape.tags {
   175	        let arr: Vec<String> = values.iter().map(|v| format!("\"{v}\"")).collect();
   176	        parts.push(format!("\"#{tag_key}\":[{}]", arr.join(",")));
   177	    }
   178	    if !shape.addresses.is_empty() {
   179	        let arr: Vec<String> = shape
   180	            .addresses
   181	            .iter()
   182	            .map(|a| format!("\"{}:{}:{}\"", a.kind, a.pubkey, a.d_tag))
   183	            .collect();
   184	        parts.push(format!("\"#a\":[{}]", arr.join(",")));
   185	    }
   186	    if let Some(since) = shape.since {
   187	        parts.push(format!("\"since\":{since}"));
   188	    }
   189	    if let Some(until) = shape.until {
   190	        parts.push(format!("\"until\":{until}"));
   191	    }
   192	    if let Some(limit) = shape.limit {
   193	        parts.push(format!("\"limit\":{limit}"));
   194	    }
   195	    format!("{{{}}}", parts.join(","))
   196	}
   197	
   198	#[cfg(test)]
   199	mod tests {
   200	    use super::*;
   201	    use crate::planner::{
   202	        InMemoryMailboxCache, InterestId, InterestScope, MailboxSnapshot, SubscriptionCompiler,
   203	    };
   204	
   205	    fn pubkey(s: &str) -> String {
   206	        format!("{s:0>64}").chars().take(64).collect()
   207	    }
   208	
   209	    fn ti(id: u64, authors: &[&str], lc: InterestLifecycle) -> LogicalInterest {
   210	        LogicalInterest {
   211	            id: InterestId(id),
   212	            scope: InterestScope::Global,
   213	            shape: InterestShape {
   214	                authors: authors.iter().map(|a| pubkey(a)).collect(),
   215	                kinds: [1u32].into_iter().collect(),
   216	                ..Default::default()
   217	            },
   218	            hints: Vec::new(),
   219	            lifecycle: lc,
   220	        }
   221	    }
   222	
   223	    #[test]
   224	    fn diff_against_empty_emits_all_reqs() {
   225	        let mut cache = InMemoryMailboxCache::new();
   226	        cache.put(
   227	            pubkey("a"),
   228	            MailboxSnapshot {
   229	                write_relays: vec!["wss://r1".to_string()],
   230	                read_relays: vec![],
   231	                both_relays: vec![],
   232	            },
   233	        );
   234	        let indexer = vec!["wss://ix".to_string()];
   235	        let compiler = SubscriptionCompiler::new(&cache, &indexer);
   236	        let interests = vec![ti(1, &["a"], InterestLifecycle::Tailing)];
   237	        let plan = compiler.compile(&interests).expect("compile");
   238	
   239	        let frames = plan_diff(None, Some(&plan), &interests);
   240	        let reqs = frames
   241	            .iter()
   242	            .filter(|f| matches!(f, WireFrame::Req { .. }))
   243	            .count();
   244	        let closes = frames
   245	            .iter()
   246	            .filter(|f| matches!(f, WireFrame::Close { .. }))
   247	            .count();
   248	        assert!(reqs >= 1);
   249	        assert_eq!(closes, 0);
   250	    }
   251	
   252	    #[test]
   253	    fn diff_identical_is_empty() {
   254	        let mut cache = InMemoryMailboxCache::new();
   255	        cache.put(
   256	            pubkey("a"),
   257	            MailboxSnapshot {
   258	                write_relays: vec!["wss://r1".to_string()],
   259	                read_relays: vec![],
   260	                both_relays: vec![],
   261	            },
   262	        );
   263	        let indexer = vec!["wss://ix".to_string()];
   264	        let compiler = SubscriptionCompiler::new(&cache, &indexer);
   265	        let interests = vec![ti(1, &["a"], InterestLifecycle::Tailing)];
   266	        let plan = compiler.compile(&interests).expect("compile");
   267	        let frames = plan_diff(Some(&plan), Some(&plan), &interests);
   268	        assert!(frames.is_empty(), "identical plans → empty diff");
   269	    }
   270	}

     1	//! Unit tests for [`crate::planner_gate`].  Sibling file so the production
     2	//! module stays under the 300 LOC soft cap (AGENTS.md).
     3	
     4	use crate::capability::{CapabilityCache, InMemoryCapabilityCache, RelayCapabilities};
     5	use crate::planner_gate::apply_coverage_filter;
     6	use nmp_core::planner::{
     7	    CompiledPlan, InterestId, InterestShape, RelayPlan, RoutingSource, SubShape,
     8	};
     9	use nmp_core::store::{
    10	    EventStore, MemEventStore, SyncMethod, WatermarkKey, WatermarkRow,
    11	};
    12	use std::collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet};
    13	
    14	fn make_plan(relay: &str, since: Option<u64>) -> CompiledPlan {
    15	    let shape = InterestShape {
    16	        authors: BTreeSet::new(),
    17	        kinds: BTreeSet::from([1]),
    18	        tags: BTreeMap::new(),
    19	        since,
    20	        until: None,
    21	        limit: None,
    22	        event_ids: BTreeSet::new(),
    23	        addresses: BTreeSet::new(),
    24	        pin_to: None,
    25	    };
    26	    let sub = SubShape {
    27	        shape,
    28	        originating_interests: vec![InterestId(7)],
    29	        canonical_filter_hash: "00000001".into(),
    30	    };
    31	    let mut relays = BTreeMap::new();
    32	    relays.insert(
    33	        relay.to_string(),
    34	        RelayPlan {
    35	            relay_url: relay.to_string(),
    36	            role_tags: BTreeSet::from([RoutingSource::Nip65]),
    37	            sub_shapes: vec![sub],
    38	        },
    39	    );
    40	    CompiledPlan {
    41	        plan_id: "test".into(),
    42	        per_relay: relays,
    43	    }
    44	}
    45	
    46	fn canon_static(_s: &SubShape) -> [u8; 32] {
    47	    [0xAA; 32]
    48	}
    49	
    50	fn now() -> u64 {
    51	    std::time::SystemTime::now()
    52	        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
    53	        .unwrap()
    54	        .as_secs()
    55	}
    56	
    57	#[test]
    58	fn skip_req_removes_subshape_and_drops_empty_relay() {
    59	    let store = MemEventStore::new();
    60	    let caps = InMemoryCapabilityCache::new();
    61	    let now_s = now();
    62	    store
    63	        .write_watermark(WatermarkRow {
    64	            key: WatermarkKey {
    65	                filter_hash: [0xAA; 32],
    66	                relay_url: "wss://r/".into(),
    67	            },
    68	            synced_up_to: now_s,
    69	            last_sync_method: SyncMethod::Negentropy,
    70	            last_negentropy_state: None,
    71	            bytes_saved_vs_req: 0,
    72	            updated_at: now_s,
    73	        })
    74	        .unwrap();
    75	    caps.set(
    76	        "wss://r/",
    77	        RelayCapabilities {
    78	            supports_nip77: true,
    79	        },
    80	    );
    81	    let mut plan = make_plan("wss://r/", None);
    82	    let report = apply_coverage_filter(&mut plan, &store, &caps, canon_static);
    83	
    84	    assert_eq!(report.count_skipped(), 1);
    85	    assert!(plan.per_relay.is_empty(), "empty relays must be dropped");
    86	}
    87	
    88	#[test]
    89	fn req_since_bumps_subshape_when_no_capability() {
    90	    let store = MemEventStore::new();
    91	    let caps = InMemoryCapabilityCache::new();
    92	    store
    93	        .write_watermark(WatermarkRow {
    94	            key: WatermarkKey {
    95	                filter_hash: [0xAA; 32],
    96	                relay_url: "wss://legacy/".into(),
    97	            },
    98	            synced_up_to: 1_000,
    99	            last_sync_method: SyncMethod::ReqScan,
   100	            last_negentropy_state: None,
   101	            bytes_saved_vs_req: 0,
   102	            updated_at: 1_000, // stale ⇒ PartialUpTo
   103	        })
   104	        .unwrap();
   105	    caps.set(
   106	        "wss://legacy/",
   107	        RelayCapabilities {
   108	            supports_nip77: false,
   109	        },
   110	    );
   111	    let mut plan = make_plan("wss://legacy/", None);
   112	    let report = apply_coverage_filter(&mut plan, &store, &caps, canon_static);
   113	
   114	    assert_eq!(report.count_bumped(), 1);
   115	    let sub = &plan.per_relay["wss://legacy/"].sub_shapes[0];
   116	    assert_eq!(sub.shape.since, Some(1_001));
   117	}
   118	
   119	#[test]
   120	fn req_since_no_op_when_existing_since_already_newer() {
   121	    let store = MemEventStore::new();
   122	    let caps = InMemoryCapabilityCache::new();
   123	    store
   124	        .write_watermark(WatermarkRow {
   125	            key: WatermarkKey {
   126	                filter_hash: [0xAA; 32],
   127	                relay_url: "wss://legacy/".into(),
   128	            },
   129	            synced_up_to: 500,
   130	            last_sync_method: SyncMethod::ReqScan,
   131	            last_negentropy_state: None,
   132	            bytes_saved_vs_req: 0,
   133	            updated_at: 500,
   134	        })
   135	        .unwrap();
   136	    caps.set(
   137	        "wss://legacy/",
   138	        RelayCapabilities {
   139	            supports_nip77: false,
   140	        },
   141	    );
   142	    let mut plan = make_plan("wss://legacy/", Some(2_000));
   143	    let _ = apply_coverage_filter(&mut plan, &store, &caps, canon_static);
   144	    assert_eq!(
   145	        plan.per_relay["wss://legacy/"].sub_shapes[0].shape.since,
   146	        Some(2_000)
   147	    );
   148	}
   149	
   150	#[test]
   151	fn bumping_since_recomputes_canonical_filter_hash() {
   152	    // P1 regression — the M4 codex review (076173d.md) flagged that
   153	    // `apply_coverage_filter` mutated `shape.since` without refreshing the
   154	    // sub-shape's `canonical_filter_hash`. Because the wire-emitter keys
   155	    // sub-ids by that hash, the bumped REQ never reached the relay — the
   156	    // diff treated old + bumped as identical.
   157	    let store = MemEventStore::new();
   158	    let caps = InMemoryCapabilityCache::new();
   159	    store
   160	        .write_watermark(WatermarkRow {
   161	            key: WatermarkKey {
   162	                filter_hash: [0xAA; 32],
   163	                relay_url: "wss://legacy/".into(),
   164	            },
   165	            synced_up_to: 1_000,
   166	            last_sync_method: SyncMethod::ReqScan,
   167	            last_negentropy_state: None,
   168	            bytes_saved_vs_req: 0,
   169	            updated_at: 1_000,
   170	        })
   171	        .unwrap();
   172	    caps.set(
   173	        "wss://legacy/",
   174	        RelayCapabilities {
   175	            supports_nip77: false,
   176	        },
   177	    );
   178	
   179	    // Seed both `canonical_filter_hash` and the compiler-equivalent hash for
   180	    // the unmutated shape. Coverage gate must produce a different hash after
   181	    // bumping `since`.
   182	    let mut plan = make_plan("wss://legacy/", None);
   183	    let hash_before = plan.per_relay["wss://legacy/"].sub_shapes[0]
   184	        .canonical_filter_hash
   185	        .clone();
   186	
   187	    let report = apply_coverage_filter(&mut plan, &store, &caps, canon_static);
   188	    assert_eq!(report.count_bumped(), 1, "expected ReqSince bump");
   189	
   190	    let bumped_sub = &plan.per_relay["wss://legacy/"].sub_shapes[0];
   191	    assert_eq!(
   192	        bumped_sub.shape.since,
   193	        Some(1_001),
   194	        "since should advance past synced_up_to"
   195	    );
   196	    assert_ne!(
   197	        bumped_sub.canonical_filter_hash, hash_before,
   198	        "mutating `since` must invalidate the canonical filter hash so the \
   199	         wire-emitter routes a new REQ frame to the relay"
   200	    );
   201	
   202	    // Cross-check: recomputing the hash from scratch matches the post-gate
   203	    // value (i.e. the gate uses the same algorithm the compiler does).
   204	    use nmp_core::planner::canonical_filter_hash as canon;
   205	    assert_eq!(
   206	        bumped_sub.canonical_filter_hash,
   207	        canon(&bumped_sub.shape),
   208	        "post-gate hash must equal the canonical hash of the mutated shape"
   209	    );
   210	}
   211	
   212	#[test]
   213	fn bumped_plan_diff_emits_close_and_req() {
   214	    // Integration-flavoured P1 regression — model the actor's plan-emit path
   215	    // (prior plan → coverage gate → next plan → plan_diff) and assert that the
   216	    // diff is non-empty when the gate bumps `since`. Without the
   217	    // `recompute_hash` fix this would return an empty diff and the relay
   218	    // would silently keep its stale REQ.
   219	    let store = MemEventStore::new();
   220	    let caps = InMemoryCapabilityCache::new();
   221	    store
   222	        .write_watermark(WatermarkRow {
   223	            key: WatermarkKey {
   224	                filter_hash: [0xAA; 32],
   225	                relay_url: "wss://legacy/".into(),
   226	            },
   227	            synced_up_to: 1_000,
   228	            last_sync_method: SyncMethod::ReqScan,
   229	            last_negentropy_state: None,
   230	            bytes_saved_vs_req: 0,
   231	            updated_at: 1_000,
   232	        })
   233	        .unwrap();
   234	    caps.set(
   235	        "wss://legacy/",
   236	        RelayCapabilities {
   237	            supports_nip77: false,
   238	        },
   239	    );
   240	
   241	    let prior = make_plan("wss://legacy/", None);
   242	    let mut next = prior.clone();
   243	    let report = apply_coverage_filter(&mut next, &store, &caps, canon_static);
   244	    assert_eq!(report.count_bumped(), 1);
   245	
   246	    let frames = nmp_core::subs::plan_diff(Some(&prior), Some(&next), &[]);
   247	    let closes = frames
   248	        .iter()
   249	        .filter(|f| matches!(f, nmp_core::subs::WireFrame::Close { .. }))
   250	        .count();
   251	    let reqs = frames
   252	        .iter()
   253	        .filter(|f| matches!(f, nmp_core::subs::WireFrame::Req { .. }))
   254	        .count();
   255	    assert_eq!(
   256	        (closes, reqs),
   257	        (1, 1),
   258	        "bumped `since` must produce one CLOSE (old sub-id) and one REQ \
   259	         (new sub-id); identical sub-ids would mean the relay keeps the stale \
   260	         REQ. Frames observed: {frames:?}"
   261	    );
   262	}
   263	
   264	#[test]
   265	fn neg_then_req_keeps_subshape_unchanged() {
   266	    let store = MemEventStore::new();
   267	    let caps = InMemoryCapabilityCache::new();
   268	    store
   269	        .write_watermark(WatermarkRow {
   270	            key: WatermarkKey {
   271	                filter_hash: [0xAA; 32],
   272	                relay_url: "wss://supports/".into(),
   273	            },
   274	            synced_up_to: 500,
   275	            last_sync_method: SyncMethod::Negentropy,
   276	            last_negentropy_state: None,
   277	            bytes_saved_vs_req: 0,
   278	            updated_at: 500,
   279	        })
   280	        .unwrap();
   281	    caps.set(
   282	        "wss://supports/",
   283	        RelayCapabilities {
   284	            supports_nip77: true,
   285	        },
   286	    );
   287	    let mut plan = make_plan("wss://supports/", None);
   288	    let _ = apply_coverage_filter(&mut plan, &store, &caps, canon_static);
   289	    let sub = &plan.per_relay["wss://supports/"].sub_shapes[0];
   290	    assert_eq!(sub.shape.since, None);
   291	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! The subscription compiler: 4-stage pipeline from `Vec<LogicalInterest>`
     2	//! to `CompiledPlan`.
     3	//!
     4	//! ## Pipeline stages
     5	//!
     6	//! 1. **Resolve authors → mailboxes** — consult `MailboxCache` (phase 1 stub:
     7	//!    `EmptyMailboxCache`; real impl in `nmp-nip65`).
     8	//! 2. **Indexer fallback** — authors with no known mailbox route to the
     9	//!    configured indexer set.
    10	//! 3. **Per-relay shape merge** — group by relay URL; merge compatible shapes
    11	//!    with `lattice::merge()` (Rules 1–8). Author sets are partitioned per
    12	//!    relay — only authors that declared a relay appear in its sub-shape.
    13	//! 4. **Plan-id binding** — deterministic hash → stable `plan_id`.
    14	//!
    15	//! ## Module structure
    16	//!
    17	//! - `mailbox`   — `MailboxCache` trait + `MailboxSnapshot` + phase-1 impls.
    18	//! - `plan_id`   — `CompileContext` + `compute_plan_id` (FNV-1a hash).
    19	//! - `partition` — `RelayEntry` + `partition_interest` (Stage 1+2).
    20	//!
    21	//! Design: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3
    22	//! Doctrine: D3 (outbox routing automatic), D6 (errors never cross FFI),
    23	//!           D8 (zero per-event allocs after warmup).
    24	
    25	mod mailbox;
    26	mod partition;
    27	mod plan_id;
    28	
    29	pub use mailbox::{EmptyMailboxCache, InMemoryMailboxCache, MailboxCache, MailboxSnapshot};
    30	pub use plan_id::CompileContext;
    31	
    32	use std::collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet};
    33	
    34	use crate::planner::{
    35	    interest::{InterestId, InterestLifecycle, InterestShape, LogicalInterest, RelayUrl},
    36	    lattice::{merge, MergeOutcome},
    37	    plan::{canonical_filter_hash, CompiledPlan, PlannerError, RelayPlan, RoutingSource, SubShape},
    38	};
    39	use partition::{partition_interest, RelayEntry};
    40	use plan_id::compute_plan_id;
    41	
    42	/// Version of the merge lattice — bump when Rule semantics change.
    43	const MERGE_LATTICE_VERSION: u8 = 1;
    44	
    45	// ─── SubscriptionCompiler ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46	
    47	/// The subscription compiler.
    48	///
    49	/// Holds a reference to the mailbox cache and indexer relay set. Both may be
    50	/// updated between compilations (the compiler always reads the current state).
    51	///
    52	/// ## Direction table (§3.1 / §3.2)
    53	///
    54	/// | Interest shape          | Direction | Relay source                                     |
    55	/// |-------------------------|-----------|--------------------------------------------------|
    56	/// | Has `authors`           | Outbox    | author's write relays via NIP-65 (or indexer)    |
    57	/// | Has `#p` tag values     | Inbox     | tagged pubkey's read relays (post-v1 DMs/notifs) |
    58	/// | Has `addresses`         | Outbox    | coord.pubkey's write relays                      |
    59	/// | No author/addr/p        | Read      | active-account read relays (hashtag firehose)    |
    60	pub struct SubscriptionCompiler<'a> {
    61	    mailbox_cache: &'a dyn MailboxCache,
    62	    indexer_relays: &'a [RelayUrl],
    63	    /// Active account read relays — for no-author/no-address interests.
    64	    /// Phase 1: empty → falls through to indexer set.
    65	    /// Phase 2: populated from active account's kind:10002 read-relays.
    66	    active_account_read_relays: &'a [RelayUrl],
    67	}
    68	
    69	impl<'a> SubscriptionCompiler<'a> {
    70	    /// Construct a compiler bound to a mailbox cache and indexer set.
    71	    pub fn new(mailbox_cache: &'a dyn MailboxCache, indexer_relays: &'a [RelayUrl]) -> Self {
    72	        Self { mailbox_cache, indexer_relays, active_account_read_relays: &[] }
    73	    }
    74	
    75	    /// Construct with explicit active-account read relays.
    76	    ///
    77	    /// When `active_account_read_relays` is non-empty, no-author interests
    78	    /// (hashtag firehose, global search) route to those relays instead of the
    79	    /// indexer set, using `RoutingSource::UserConfigured(AccountRead)`.
    80	    pub fn with_active_account_read_relays(
    81	        mailbox_cache: &'a dyn MailboxCache,
    82	        indexer_relays: &'a [RelayUrl],
    83	        active_account_read_relays: &'a [RelayUrl],
    84	    ) -> Self {
    85	        Self { mailbox_cache, indexer_relays, active_account_read_relays }
    86	    }
    87	
    88	    /// Compile a set of logical interests into a `CompiledPlan`.
    89	    ///
    90	    /// **Warning — use only in tests or when policy versions are immutable.**
    91	    ///
    92	    /// Delegates to `compile_with_context(..., &CompileContext::default())`,
    93	    /// which sets `indexer_set_version = 0` and `user_config_version = 0`.
    94	    /// If the indexer relay set or active-account config changes at runtime,
    95	    /// the resulting `plan_id` will NOT change — callers that rely on plan-id
    96	    /// stability for subscription diffing MUST use `compile_with_context` with
    97	    /// their own monotonic version counters.
    98	    ///
    99	    /// Production callers: use `compile_with_context`.
   100	    /// Test-only / static-config callers: `compile` is safe.
   101	    pub fn compile(
   102	        &self,
   103	        interests: &[LogicalInterest],
   104	    ) -> Result<CompiledPlan, PlannerError> {
   105	        self.compile_with_context(interests, &CompileContext::default())
   106	    }
   107	
   108	    /// Compile with explicit versioning context for plan-id stability.
   109	    ///
   110	    /// Callers that track `indexer_set_version` / `user_config_version` should
   111	    /// use this form so plan-ids invalidate correctly on policy changes.
   112	    pub fn compile_with_context(
   113	        &self,
   114	        interests: &[LogicalInterest],
   115	        ctx: &CompileContext,
   116	    ) -> Result<CompiledPlan, PlannerError> {
   117	        // ── Stages 1 + 2: author-partitioned relay entry collection ──────────
   118	        let mut relay_entries: BTreeMap<RelayUrl, Vec<RelayEntry>> = BTreeMap::new();
   119	        for interest in interests {
   120	            partition_interest(
   121	                interest,
   122	                self.mailbox_cache,
   123	                self.indexer_relays,
   124	                self.active_account_read_relays,
   125	                &mut relay_entries,
   126	            );
   127	        }
   128	
   129	        // ── Stage 3: Per-relay shape merge ──────────────────────────────────
   130	        //
   131	        // `role_tags` accumulates ALL RoutingSource lanes across all entries
   132	        // for a relay, preserving the four-lane discipline (§3.1).
   133	        let mut per_relay: BTreeMap<RelayUrl, RelayPlan> = BTreeMap::new();
   134	        for (relay_url, entries) in relay_entries {
   135	            let mut role_tags: BTreeSet<RoutingSource> = BTreeSet::new();
   136	            // Shape + lifecycle + all source lanes + originating interest id.
   137	            let shaped: Vec<(InterestShape, InterestLifecycle, BTreeSet<RoutingSource>, InterestId)> =
   138	                entries.into_iter().map(|entry| entry.into_shape()).collect();
   139	
   140	            let mut sub_shapes: Vec<(InterestShape, InterestLifecycle, Vec<InterestId>)> =
   141	                Vec::new();
   142	            for (shape, lifecycle, entry_sources, interest_id) in shaped {
   143	                // Merge all source lanes from this entry into role_tags.
   144	                for src in entry_sources {
   145	                    role_tags.insert(src);
   146	                }
   147	                let mut merged = false;
   148	                for (existing_shape, existing_lifecycle, existing_ids) in sub_shapes.iter_mut() {
   149	                    if let MergeOutcome::Merged(new_shape) =
   150	                        merge(&existing_shape.clone(), &shape, existing_lifecycle, &lifecycle)
   151	                    {
   152	                        *existing_shape = new_shape;
   153	                        // Dedupe: the same interest_id can land on a relay more
   154	                        // than once (e.g. when Case A's outbox push and the
   155	                        // "both populated" inbox push both target the same
   156	                        // relay because the author's write relay == a tagged
   157	                        // pubkey's read relay). `originating_interests` is a
   158	                        // set semantically, not a multiset.
   159	                        if !existing_ids.contains(&interest_id) {
   160	                            existing_ids.push(interest_id.clone());
   161	                        }
   162	                        merged = true;
   163	                        break;
   164	                    }
   165	                }
   166	                if !merged {
   167	                    sub_shapes.push((shape, lifecycle, vec![interest_id]));
   168	                }
   169	            }
   170	
   171	            let relay_sub_shapes: Vec<SubShape> = sub_shapes
   172	                .into_iter()
   173	                .map(|(shape, _lifecycle, ids)| {
   174	                    let hash = canonical_filter_hash(&shape);
   175	                    SubShape { shape, originating_interests: ids, canonical_filter_hash: hash }
   176	                })
   177	                .collect();
   178	
   179	            per_relay.insert(
   180	                relay_url.clone(),
   181	                RelayPlan { relay_url, role_tags, sub_shapes: relay_sub_shapes },
   182	            );
   183	        }
   184	
   185	        // ── Stage 4: Plan-id binding ──────────────────────────────────────────
   186	        let plan_id = compute_plan_id(interests, self.mailbox_cache, ctx, MERGE_LATTICE_VERSION);
   187	        Ok(CompiledPlan { plan_id, per_relay })
   188	    }
   189	}
   190	
   191	// ─── Canonical filter hash ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   192	//
   193	// The canonical hash function moved to `plan::canonical_filter_hash` so any
   194	// post-compile pass that mutates a `SubShape` can recompute its
   195	// `canonical_filter_hash` without having to import a compiler-private helper.
   196	// See `plan::canonical_filter_hash` for the BLAKE3-CBOR migration target.

docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[LANDED M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/design/framework-magic.md:46:**Test names** (13 behavior tests + 1 coverage meta-test = 14 total, all in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` — see [test-scaffolding.md](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md) for the harness):
docs/design/framework-magic.md:57:10. `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss`
docs/design/framework-magic.md:61:14. `contract_surface_complete` — **meta-test**; asserts every behavior listed in this index has a corresponding `#[test] fn` in `framework_magic_contract.rs` (ignored or not). Drift between this doc and the test file fails the build.
docs/design/framework-magic.md:70:2. Which `#[ignore = "pending M_n"]` lines were removed from `framework_magic_contract.rs`.
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:38:**Test:** `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss`. The test uses a mock relay with declared NIP-77 capability and a `SimulatedClock`:
docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:46:**Milestone owner:** **[LANDED M4]** (T53 follow-up). M4 shipped the NIP-77 engine and the `apply_coverage_filter` planner-side gate; the T53 follow-up wired it into `SubscriptionLifecycle::set_coverage_hook` (the `nmp-core` side of the seam) and flipped `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` to active in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`. Sub-paths 1, 2 (authoritative miss), 3 (NIP-77-capable → NegThenReq), and 4 (capability fallback → ReqSince + canonical-filter-hash refresh) are covered by the active C10 test. The reconnect gap-fill (sub-path 4 from the original spec) and the `bytes_saved_vs_req` instrumentation (sub-path 5) are exercised by the dedicated single-purpose tests in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/nip77_reconnect_resumes_from_watermark.rs` and `crates/nmp-testing/tests/nip77_cold_open_via_neg.rs`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:60:+- **Framework-magic contract** (kind:3 auto-tracking, `bunker://` URL onboarding, new-nsec creation, outbox-by-default-on-publish, etc.): designed ([docs/design/framework-magic.md](docs/design/framework-magic.md)) with 13 behaviors and 14 named tests in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:193:README.md:21:- **Framework-magic contract** (kind:3 auto-tracking, `bunker://` URL onboarding, new-nsec creation, outbox-by-default-on-publish, etc.): designed ([docs/design/framework-magic.md](docs/design/framework-magic.md)) with 13 behaviors and 14 named tests in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:216:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5730:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1312:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1261:+| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/b49f8f3.md:668:docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:9:crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs
docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:18:The next milestone owner must **not** rename the file to fit the milestone-prefix pattern. The framework-magic delta protocol in `intro.md` §4 covers this: a milestone landing that renames `framework_magic_contract.rs` requires an ADR.
docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:24:name = "framework_magic_contract"
docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:25:path = "tests/framework_magic_contract.rs"
docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:28:Invocation: `cargo test -p nmp-testing --test framework_magic_contract`.
docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:44:c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss
docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:134:        "c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss",
docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:161:            "EXPECTED_TESTS lists '{}' but no #[test] fn with that name exists in framework_magic_contract.rs",
docs/design/framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md:178:A test is `#[ignore = "pending M_n"]`'d when its owning milestone has not landed the implementation. The ignore reason **must** name the milestone (e.g., `"pending M3 tombstone persistence"`) so that a `grep -n "pending M" framework_magic_contract.rs` produces a checklist for each milestone's exit gate.

NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/de9e7b4.md:4904:    45	The deliverable: `docs/design/framework-magic.md` (the contract) + `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` (one test per bullet). The contract evolves alongside milestones; each milestone owner adds a "framework-magic delta" section to their exit-gate report.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/de9e7b4.md:12733:?? crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:90:+- Framework-magic contract codified (13 behaviors, 14 tests in framework_magic_contract.rs)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:163:++- **Framework-magic contract** (kind:3 auto-tracking, `bunker://` URL onboarding, new-nsec creation, outbox-by-default-on-publish, etc.): designed ([docs/design/framework-magic.md](docs/design/framework-magic.md)) with 13 behaviors and 14 named tests in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:263:++> **Companion test file:** `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` (one test per contract bullet plus a coverage meta-test; layout in [test-scaffolding.md](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md)).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:279:++- [Test scaffolding — the `framework_magic_contract.rs` harness, naming convention, coverage meta-test](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:283:++Each row binds a behavior to: the sub-file that specifies it, the test name in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`, the milestone that owns the implementation, and the doctrine clause it discharges.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:296:++| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:303:++**Test names** (13 behavior tests + 1 coverage meta-test = 14 total, all in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` — see [test-scaffolding.md](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md) for the harness):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:314:++10. `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:318:++14. `contract_surface_complete` — **meta-test**; asserts every behavior listed in this index has a corresponding `#[test] fn` in `framework_magic_contract.rs` (ignored or not). Drift between this doc and the test file fails the build.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:327:++2. Which `#[ignore = "pending M_n"]` lines were removed from `framework_magic_contract.rs`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:451:++4. A test in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` that verifies the guarantee.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:483:++**Test:** `c_n_<snake_case>` in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`. <one sentence on what the test asserts>
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:557:++**Test:** `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`. The test:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1024:++**Test:** `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss`. The test uses a mock relay with declared NIP-77 capability and a `SimulatedClock`:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1067:++crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1076:++The next milestone owner must **not** rename the file to fit the milestone-prefix pattern. The framework-magic delta protocol in `intro.md` §4 covers this: a milestone landing that renames `framework_magic_contract.rs` requires an ADR.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1082:++name = "framework_magic_contract"
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1083:++path = "tests/framework_magic_contract.rs"
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1086:++Invocation: `cargo test -p nmp-testing --test framework_magic_contract`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1102:++c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1192:++        "c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss",
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1219:++            "EXPECTED_TESTS lists '{}' but no #[test] fn with that name exists in framework_magic_contract.rs",
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1236:++A test is `#[ignore = "pending M_n"]`'d when its owning milestone has not landed the implementation. The ignore reason **must** name the milestone (e.g., `"pending M3 tombstone persistence"`) so that a `grep -n "pending M" framework_magic_contract.rs` produces a checklist for each milestone's exit gate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1329:++| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

The ignore reason **must** name the milestone (e.g., `"pending M3 tombstone persistence"`) so that a `grep -n "pending M" framework_magic_contract.rs` produces a checklist for each milestone's exit gate.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1312:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1261:+| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1008:+The next milestone owner must **not** rename the file to fit the milestone-prefix pattern. The framework-magic delta protocol in `intro.md` §4 covers this: a milestone landing that renames `framework_magic_contract.rs` requires an ADR.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1168:+A test is `#[ignore = "pending M_n"]`'d when its owning milestone has not landed the implementation. The ignore reason **must** name the milestone (e.g., `"pending M3 tombstone persistence"`) so that a `grep -n "pending M" framework_magic_contract.rs` produces a checklist for each milestone's exit gate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1261:+| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

The ignore reason **must** name the milestone (e.g., `"pending M3 tombstone persistence"`) so that a `grep -n "pending M" framework_magic_contract.rs` produces a checklist for each milestone's exit gate.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/5ff0ac5.md:747:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

665:+pub fn coverage_pct(coverage: Coverage, now_s: u64) -> u8 {
784:+    fn coverage_pct_collapses_complete_to_100() {
785:+        assert_eq!(coverage_pct(Coverage::CompleteAsOf(1), 100), 100);
786:+        assert_eq!(coverage_pct(Coverage::Unknown, 100), 0);
787:+        assert_eq!(coverage_pct(Coverage::PartialUpTo(96), 100), 96);
825:+//!   `Result` returns; mapping to `toast`/`busy` state happens at the actor /
857:+pub use planner_gate::{apply_coverage_filter, CoverageReport, GateDecision};
1109:+/// expose `canonical_filter_hash(&Filter)` (see `docs/design/lmdb/watermarks.md`
1112:+pub fn apply_coverage_filter<F>(

3426:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:237:**Atomicity** (doctrine guarantee): the kernel ensures `module.reduce(...)`, ledger transitions, and local store writes happen as one actor message. External effects such as relay publishes cannot be rolled back after a relay accepts them, so publish steps must be ledger-correlated and restart-recoverable: a "publish accepted but local insert failed" path becomes an explicit failed/recovery ledger state, not silent divergence.
3438:docs/design/framework-magic.md:39:| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
3460:docs/design/framework-magic/capabilities.md:45:**Milestone owner:** **[PARTIAL]** for placeholder shape — the M1 timeline slice ships `author_display` as a non-`Option` `String` with shortened-npub fallback (verified in `crates/nmp-core` timeline tests). However, `author_picture_url` is currently `Option<String>` in the M1 payload: when no kind:0 is present the field is `None` rather than a deterministic identicon URI, which violates D1's "every display field carries a value" guarantee. The D1-compliant fix is to make `author_picture_url: String` non-optional and populate it with a deterministic identicon URI derived from the pubkey hash when no picture URL is known — making the placeholder computable without any network call and without an `Option` that tempts the platform to branch on `None`. This change lands in M2 alongside the view-module surface refactor. **[PENDING M2/M3]** for the full in-place refinement guarantees: sub-paths 2 and 4 require the kernel's projection cache (`kernel-substrate.md` §3 line 148 `on_projection_changed`) which graduates in M2; sub-path 3 requires the per-tick re-format hook (`fn on_tick`, M2's `ViewModule` trait work).
3482:docs/product-spec/doctrine.md:55:The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
3521:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:114:| S1 | Mount/unmount churn | actor recv + refcount | D5 (snapshot bounded), bible #5 |
3540:docs/design/ffi-hardening.md:251:| **D4** one writer per fact | S1, S5 — refcount only mutated on actor thread; reentrancy under same single-writer rule |
3550:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D6, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
3602:docs/design/ffi-hardening/gates.md:240:- ✅ **Proof:** debt-inventory §3 D4 audit — single-threaded actor
3624:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:27:| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
3628:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:31:| **D6** errors never cross FFI as exceptions | C7 (publish fail → `PublishPlanError` state, not exception); C11 (signer errors → ledger state); implicitly all 13 via the actor's toast-surface design |
3659:docs/design/lmdb/trait.md:213:- `OverPinned` → the caller (actor) surfaces this as `Effect::ViewOverPinned { claimer }` and then calls `release(claimer)` to drop the offending claim, keeping the working set bounded per D8.
3670:docs/design/podcast/capabilities.md:185:iOS impl: CoreML inference against the bundled `bge-small-en-v1.5.mlpackage`. The bridge holds the model handle (loaded once on first request, kept warm until app backgrounded for > 60 s). Bounded state: one `MLModel` instance. Idempotent: identical inputs → identical outputs (deterministic per CoreML guarantee at FP16; documented + asserted in tests).
3674:docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:6:> **Why M11.5 exists:** The podcast app (M11) proves the kernel boundary for a non-Nostr-shaped product. M11.5 proves the boundary for a Nostr-shaped product that introduces a *new non-trivial protocol* (NIP-29) on top of the existing social stack, by re-architecting an app that already ships a working Rust core. The "Rust port" step in M11 is greenfield; in M11.5 it's *re-architecting Highlighter's existing `app/core/src/` onto NMP's substrate, deleting its hand-rolled actor + subscription pump + outbox in the process*. The deletion is the proof.
3675:docs/plan/m11.5-highlighter.md:46:The existing Highlighter Rust core's hand-rolled actor (`client.rs` 1,956 LOC), subscription pump (`subscriptions.rs` 2,155 LOC), runtime (`nostr_runtime.rs` 1,328 LOC), outbox (`outbox.rs` 374 LOC), cache shim (`cache.rs`), and delta events (`events.rs`) **all delete** — NMP's kernel substrate provides this once, framework-wide. **The deletion of ≈ 6,000 LOC of bespoke infrastructure is the primary doctrine validation of M11.5.**
3699:docs/design/lmdb/trait/types.md:182:No `StoreError` ever crosses FFI (D6). The actor maps every variant to a typed `Effect` or a graceful degraded state. See [`../trait.md`](../trait.md) §5 for the full mapping.
3710:docs/design/nip29-crate.md:23:Treating NIP-29 as "just another kind range" would force NMP's kernel actor or M2 compiler to grow group-aware special cases, violating ADR-0009 doctrine D0 ("the kernel never grows app nouns"). The crate is the boundary where the special cases live; the kernel sees a generic `RelayPinnedInterest` and `RelayPinnedPublish` it already knows how to handle (see `routing.md`).
3722:docs/design/nip29-crate.md:181:2. **Trust model for relay-signed metadata.** Resolved: M11.5 ships policy A (NIP-11 strict) when NIP-11 declares a relay pubkey, otherwise B (TOFU per `(host_relay_url, group_id)`). Policy C (accept-any-from-host) is explicitly rejected — codex review surfaced a P1 spoofing vector where any host relay also accepting parameterized writes lets a malicious user forge a 39001/39002 admitting themselves as admin. See `moderation.md` §4. The remaining ADR-level question is rotation UX (silent accept on first warning vs explicit prompt); deferred to post-M11.5.
3846:Internal test-facing surface (e.g., `spawn_actor`) is gated behind `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-support"))]` so production builds export no actor internals.
3882:The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.

4570:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:117:The current `ModuleRegistry` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mod.rs:41`) discards the concrete `M: DomainModule` type after `register_domain::<M>()` returns — only the `ModuleDescriptor` is retained. The store cannot get from a namespace string back to `M::SCHEMA_VERSION` or `M::migrations()` at runtime. M3 adds a `DomainFactories { schema_version: fn() -> u32, migrations: fn() -> Vec<DomainMigration>, indexes: fn() -> Vec<DomainIndex> }` struct attached per descriptor, populated by capturing the `M::*` consts and fns in `fn`-pointer closures at register time. This matches the existing `key_fn: fn(&[u8]) -> Option<Vec<u8>>` pattern in `DomainIndex` (`crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/domain.rs:18`) — no `Box<dyn DomainModule>` and no new trait object-safety constraints on `DomainModule`. The change is additive to the substrate module surface. See [`lmdb/watermarks.md`](lmdb/watermarks.md) §4.1 for the registry-side code shape.
4573:docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:227:6. **`ModuleRegistry::register_domain` API stability.** Adding `DomainFactories` to `ModuleDescriptor` is a non-breaking additive change to the public substrate API (existing callers using only the generic `register_domain::<M>()` continue to compile), but it commits us to keeping `DomainModule::SCHEMA_VERSION` and `DomainModule::migrations` as compile-time-resolvable items rather than object-safe methods. Confirm this with the substrate maintainer before M3 lands — if `DomainModule` is expected to support runtime composition (e.g., plugin loading), we need option (c): the actor passes the live `&[Box<dyn DomainModule>]` to `EventStore::open` instead. Recommended default: stay with `fn`-pointer factories; revisit if a plugin-loading use case appears.

5478:# Framework Magic §C9–§C10 — Sync, Provenance, Watermarks
5505:## C10. Watermarks gate backfill; cache miss becomes authoritative; NIP-77 is the default
5527:C9 is **what** the store remembers about each event's provenance. C10 is **what** the store remembers about each `(filter, relay)` pair's coverage. Together they answer the question `ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §9.8 raises: *"Having an event in the local store does not prove that a view is complete."* C9 is "we have this event"; C10 is "we have everything matching this filter from this relay up to this timestamp." The framework needs both to render correctly without fetching needlessly.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Framework Magic — Test Scaffolding
     2	
     3	> Parent: `docs/design/framework-magic.md`.
     4	> Read first: `docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md` §9.3 (the `PlannerHarness` this scaffolding extends); `docs/design/lmdb/tests.md` (storage-layer test patterns); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.13 (`nmp-testing` surface).
     5	
     6	## 1. File location and naming convention break
     7	
     8	```
     9	crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs
    10	```
    11	
    12	The existing convention is **milestone-prefixed** (`m2_subscription_compilation_audit.rs`, `m3_lmdb_invariants.rs`, etc.). This file is intentionally **cross-cutting** — it is the *only* test file in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/` that is not milestone-prefixed. The convention break is deliberate:
    13	
    14	- The contract spans M2 + M3 + M4 + M6 + M8 + reactivity-bench; no single milestone owns it.
    15	- Renaming the file under a single milestone (e.g., `m_cross_framework_magic.rs`) would suggest one milestone is responsible for the whole contract; the opposite is true — every milestone owner adds to it.
    16	- The file is the *index test* — the meta-test (§4 below) reads `docs/design/framework-magic.md`'s row table and asserts every row has a `#[test] fn` with the expected name. A renaming under a milestone prefix would obscure this role.
    17	
    18	The next milestone owner must **not** rename the file to fit the milestone-prefix pattern. The framework-magic delta protocol in `intro.md` §4 covers this: a milestone landing that renames `framework_magic_contract.rs` requires an ADR.
    19	
    20	`Cargo.toml` for `nmp-testing` adds the standard `[[test]]` block:
    21	
    22	```toml
    23	[[test]]
    24	name = "framework_magic_contract"
    25	path = "tests/framework_magic_contract.rs"
    26	```
    27	
    28	Invocation: `cargo test -p nmp-testing --test framework_magic_contract`.
    29	
    30	## 2. Test names — the canonical 14
    31	
    32	Thirteen behavior tests (C1–C13; the table in `framework-magic.md` shows the exact names) plus the coverage meta-test, total 14 `#[test] fn` declarations:
    33	
    34	```
    35	c1_replaceable_supersedes_on_insert
    36	c2_parameterized_replaceable_supersedes_by_dtag
    37	c3_kind5_delete_removes_referenced_and_tombstones
    38	c4_nip40_expiration_removes_and_persists_schedule
    39	c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs
    40	c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays
    41	c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed
    42	c8_subscriptions_coalesce_autoclose_and_buffer
    43	c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries
    44	c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss
    45	c11_bunker_url_and_nsec_creation_complete_via_actions
    46	c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance
    47	c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place
    48	contract_surface_complete                                  # meta-test
    49	```
    50	
    51	Test names are **stable identifiers**. Renaming any of them constitutes a contract revision per `intro.md` §4 and requires the deprecation marker (`#[test] fn old_name() { c_n_new_name() }` for at least one milestone cycle).
    52	
    53	## 3. The harness
    54	
    55	The harness is the union of three existing testing surfaces, exposed as one builder:
    56	
    57	```rust
    58	// crates/nmp-testing/src/framework_magic.rs (proposed)
    59	
    60	pub struct ContractHarness {
    61	    actor:            TestActor,                  // wraps the real actor with a recorded reconciler
    62	    planner:          PlannerHarness,             // from subscription-compilation/tests.md §9.3
    63	    clock:            SimulatedClock,             // from subsystems.md §7.13
    64	    network_chaos:    NetworkChaos,               // from subsystems.md §7.13
    65	    mock_relays:      Vec<MockRelay>,             // from nostr-relay-builder
    66	    keyring:          InMemoryKeyringCapability,  // for C11
    67	    audit:            WireFrameAuditLog,          // proposed; captures every CLOSE/REQ/EVENT frame
    68	    reconciler_log:   Vec<AppUpdate>,             // every AppUpdate emitted across the FFI seam
    69	}
    70	
    71	impl ContractHarness {
    72	    pub fn new() -> Self;
    73	    pub fn with_mock_relays(self, count: u8) -> Self;
    74	    pub fn with_nip77_capable_relays(self, capable: &[bool]) -> Self;
    75	    pub fn with_seeded_accounts(self, accounts: &[(Pubkey, SignerKind)]) -> Self;
    76	    pub fn with_active_account(self, pubkey: Pubkey) -> Self;
    77	    pub fn with_seeded_mailboxes(self, entries: &[(Pubkey, MailboxList)]) -> Self;
    78	    pub fn with_seeded_follows(self, account: Pubkey, follows: &[Pubkey]) -> Self;
    79	    pub fn build(self) -> Contract;
    80	}
    81	
    82	pub struct Contract {
    83	    // dispatch surface
    84	    pub fn dispatch(&mut self, action: AppAction);
    85	    pub fn open_view<V: ViewModule>(&mut self, spec: V::Spec) -> ViewHandle<V>;
    86	    pub fn close_view<V: ViewModule>(&mut self, handle: ViewHandle<V>);
    87	    pub fn ingest(&mut self, relay: usize, event: NostrEvent);
    88	    pub fn ingest_eose(&mut self, relay: usize, sub_id: &str);
    89	    pub fn disconnect_relay(&mut self, relay: usize);
    90	    pub fn reconnect_relay(&mut self, relay: usize);
    91	    pub fn advance_clock_ms(&mut self, ms: u64);
    92	    pub fn simulate_actor_restart(&mut self);
    93	
    94	    // assertion surface
    95	    pub fn wire_frames(&self, relay: usize) -> &[WireFrame];
    96	    pub fn reconciler_log(&self) -> &[AppUpdate];
    97	    pub fn event_store_get(&self, id: &EventId) -> Option<&StoredEvent>;
    98	    pub fn provenance_of(&self, id: &EventId) -> &Provenance;
    99	    pub fn watermark_of(&self, filter_sig: &FilterSig, relay: usize) -> Option<&Watermark>;
   100	    pub fn action_ledger(&self) -> &[ActionLedgerRow];
   101	    pub fn keyring_entries(&self) -> &[KeyringEntry];
   102	    pub fn session_state(&self) -> &SessionState;
   103	}
   104	```
   105	
   106	The harness extends `PlannerHarness` rather than wrapping it: every assertion the M2 audit gate makes against `PlannerHarness::compile_audit_log()` is accessible through the contract harness via `Contract::wire_frames(relay)`, but the contract harness also drives the full actor (so action ledger transitions, projection cache updates, and reconciler emissions are observable).
   107	
   108	`InMemoryKeyringCapability` is a new `nmp-testing` primitive for C11. It implements the `KeyringCapability` trait with a `HashMap<String, Vec<u8>>` backing store; the test inspects the stored bytes to verify NIP-49 encryption envelope shape.
   109	
   110	`WireFrameAuditLog` is a new `nmp-testing` primitive that captures every outbound frame the relay-worker emits. The M2 design has an audit log on the planner side; this harness has it on the wire side — both must agree, and a separate harness invariant could later assert that agreement.
   111	
   112	The harness does **not** include a real network — every relay is a `MockRelay`. Every contract test runs in deterministic time with no I/O. Total runtime budget for the full suite: <5 seconds.
   113	
   114	## 4. The coverage meta-test
   115	
   116	```rust
   117	#[test]
   118	fn contract_surface_complete() {
   119	    // 1. Read docs/design/framework-magic.md and parse the contract table.
   120	    let contract = parse_contract_table(include_str!("../../../docs/design/framework-magic.md"));
   121	
   122	    // 2. Enumerate the #[test] fns in this binary via inventory or a const list.
   123	    //    The const list is the canonical surface; inventory is the consistency check.
   124	    const EXPECTED_TESTS: &[&str] = &[
   125	        "c1_replaceable_supersedes_on_insert",
   126	        "c2_parameterized_replaceable_supersedes_by_dtag",
   127	        "c3_kind5_delete_removes_referenced_and_tombstones",
   128	        "c4_nip40_expiration_removes_and_persists_schedule",
   129	        "c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs",
   130	        "c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays",
   131	        "c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed",
   132	        "c8_subscriptions_coalesce_autoclose_and_buffer",
   133	        "c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries",
   134	        "c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss",
   135	        "c11_bunker_url_and_nsec_creation_complete_via_actions",
   136	        "c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance",
   137	        "c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place",
   138	    ];
   139	
   140	    // 3. Assert every row in the contract table has a matching expected test name.
   141	    for row in &contract.rows {
   142	        assert!(
   143	            EXPECTED_TESTS.contains(&row.test_name.as_str()),
   144	            "contract row {} has test name '{}' which is not in EXPECTED_TESTS — \
   145	             update either the doc table or EXPECTED_TESTS so they agree",
   146	            row.id, row.test_name,
   147	        );
   148	    }
   149	
   150	    // 4. Assert no expected test name is missing from the contract table.
   151	    for expected in EXPECTED_TESTS {
   152	        let found = contract.rows.iter().any(|r| r.test_name == *expected);
   153	        assert!(found, "EXPECTED_TESTS lists '{}' which is not in the contract doc table", expected);
   154	    }
   155	
   156	    // 5. Assert every EXPECTED_TESTS entry is actually a #[test] fn in this binary.
   157	    //    Compile-time check via inventory crate or a build script that scans the file.
   158	    for expected in EXPECTED_TESTS {
   159	        assert!(
   160	            test_exists_in_binary(expected),
   161	            "EXPECTED_TESTS lists '{}' but no #[test] fn with that name exists in framework_magic_contract.rs",
   162	            expected,
   163	        );
   164	    }
   165	}
   166	```
   167	
   168	The meta-test is **not** `#[ignore]`. It runs on every CI run. It catches three classes of drift:
   169	
   170	1. The doc table grows a row but the test file doesn't grow a `#[test] fn` — caught by step 4.
   171	2. The test file grows a `#[test] fn` but the doc table doesn't list it — caught by step 3.
   172	3. A renamed test breaks the doc-test correspondence — caught by either step 3 or 4 depending on which side renamed first.
   173	
   174	The meta-test does **not** check `#[ignore]` status. A test for a pending milestone is correctly `#[ignore]`'d; the meta-test's job is structural correspondence, not implementation readiness. The milestone delta protocol (`intro.md` §4) handles the un-ignore cadence.
   175	
   176	## 5. `#[ignore]` discipline
   177	
   178	A test is `#[ignore = "pending M_n"]`'d when its owning milestone has not landed the implementation. The ignore reason **must** name the milestone (e.g., `"pending M3 tombstone persistence"`) so that a `grep -n "pending M" framework_magic_contract.rs` produces a checklist for each milestone's exit gate.
   179	
   180	The framework-magic delta in a milestone's exit-gate report enumerates which `pending M_n` ignore lines were removed during the milestone. Removing an ignore line without the delta entry fails the post-merge codex review.
