   143	+++ b/crates/nmp-nip77/src/capability.rs
   144	@@ -0,0 +1,243 @@
   145	+//! Per-relay NIP-77 capability negotiation.
   146	+//!
   147	+//! A relay either supports the `NEG-OPEN` verb or it doesn't.  We discover
   148	+//! support by probing once on first connect; the result is cached so future
   149	+//! reconciliations skip the probe.  The cache is owned by the M4 substrate;
   150	+//! persistence is layered on top by [`crate::capability_domain::CapabilityDomain`].
   151	+//!
   152	+//! ## Probe state machine
   153	+//!
   154	+//! ```text
   155	+//!  ┌──────────┐  start_probe        ┌───────────┐
   156	+//!  │  Unknown │ ───────────────────▶│  Probing  │
   157	+//!  └──────────┘                     └──────┬────┘
   158	+//!                                          │
   159	+//!                          ┌── NEG-MSG ────┤── NEG-ERR ──┐
   160	+//!                          ▼                              ▼
   161	+//!                  ┌──────────────┐               ┌──────────────┐
   162	+//!                  │ Supported    │               │ Unsupported  │
   163	+//!                  └──────────────┘               └──────────────┘
   164	+//! ```
   165	+//!
   166	+//! `Supported` and `Unsupported` are terminal in normal operation.  A relay
   167	+//! that downgrades or revokes support requires an explicit reset (e.g. a
   168	+//! manual `RunSync` invocation) — same policy as `nostr-sdk`'s capability
   169	+//! tracking.
   170	+
   171	+use std::collections::HashMap;
   172	+use std::sync::Mutex;
   173	+
   174	+/// What we believe about a relay's NIP-77 support.
   175	+#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
   176	+pub struct RelayCapabilities {
   177	+    pub supports_nip77: bool,
   178	+}
   179	+
   180	+/// Probe lifecycle state for a single relay.
   181	+#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
   182	+pub enum ProbeState {
   183	+    Unknown,
   184	+    Probing,
   185	+    Supported,
   186	+    Unsupported,
   187	+}
   188	+
   189	+/// What a probe transition produces — used by callers to drive next steps.
   190	+#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
   191	+pub enum ProbeOutcome {
   192	+    /// Probe was already complete; no further work.
   193	+    AlreadySettled(RelayCapabilities),
   194	+    /// Probe transitioned to a terminal state on this call.
   195	+    Settled(RelayCapabilities),
   196	+    /// Probe is in flight; caller must wait for the next relay frame.
   197	+    Pending,
   198	+}
   199	+
   200	+/// In-memory cache of per-relay capabilities.
   201	+///
   202	+/// Implementations are free to back the cache with disk (see
   203	+/// [`crate::capability_domain::CapabilityDomain`]) — the trait is the
   204	+/// minimal substrate the M4 reconciler depends on.
   205	+pub trait CapabilityCache: Send + Sync {
   206	+    fn get(&self, relay_url: &str) -> Option<RelayCapabilities>;
   207	+    fn set(&self, relay_url: &str, caps: RelayCapabilities);
   208	+    fn state(&self, relay_url: &str) -> ProbeState;
   209	+    fn set_state(&self, relay_url: &str, state: ProbeState);
   210	+}
   211	+
   212	+/// Lock-protected `HashMap` implementation of [`CapabilityCache`].  Adequate
   213	+/// for app-lifetime usage; persistence is layered on top by the domain
   214	+/// module.
   215	+pub struct InMemoryCapabilityCache {
   216	+    inner: Mutex<Inner>,
   217	+}
   218	+
   219	+#[derive(Default)]
   220	+struct Inner {

     1	# Design: Framework Magic Contract — Things That Just Work
     2	
     3	> **Status:** Draft. Research citations folded in from `docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md` and `docs/research/ndk/kind3-auto-tracking.md`. Doctrine wording aligned with `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (D0–D8 canonical set).
     4	> **Date:** 2026-05-18.
     5	> **Source directives:** `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` "Framework magic contract" section; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (cardinal doctrines D0–D8) + §3.3 (bug-class extinction); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1–§7.8.
     6	> **Companion test file:** `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` (one test per contract bullet plus a coverage meta-test; layout in [test-scaffolding.md](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md)).
     7	> **Scope:** Enumerate every behavior the framework guarantees so the application does not have to author code for it. The user directive is explicit: *"apps shouldn't have to care or know about these operations happening in the background, things should just work."* This document is the contract; the test suite is the proof; the milestone implementations are the substrate.
     8	
     9	This document is split into focused sub-files to stay under the 300 LOC ceiling (`AGENTS.md`) and to let milestone owners revise one chapter at a time without merge contention.
    10	
    11	## Section map
    12	
    13	- [Intro — purpose, doctrine alignment, per-bullet template, how this contract evolves](framework-magic/intro.md)
    14	- [Kind:3 auto-tracking — follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions](framework-magic/kind3.md) (C5)
    15	- [Replaceable & delete invariants — supersession, parameterized supersession, kind:5, NIP-40](framework-magic/replaceable.md) (C1–C4)
    16	- [Outbox routing — read fan-out, write fan-out, private events fail closed](framework-magic/outbox.md) (C6, C7)
    17	- [Subscriptions — dedup, coalesce, auto-close, buffered batches](framework-magic/subs.md) (C8)
    18	- [Sync & provenance — watermarks, NIP-77 backfill, redelivery merge](framework-magic/sync.md) (C9, C10)
    19	- [Signers & onboarding — bunker://, nsec creation, Keychain persistence](framework-magic/signers.md) (C11)
    20	- [Sessions — account switch = state, view rebuild without imperative dance](framework-magic/sessions.md) (C12)
    21	- [Capabilities & rendering — best-effort placeholders, in-place refinement](framework-magic/capabilities.md) (C13)
    22	- [Test scaffolding — the `framework_magic_contract.rs` harness, naming convention, coverage meta-test](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md)
    23	
    24	## The 13 contract bullets
    25	
    26	Each row binds a behavior to: the sub-file that specifies it, the test name in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`, the milestone that owns the implementation, and the doctrine clause it discharges.

    28	| # | Behavior | Sub-file | Test name | Milestone | Doctrine / spec |
    29	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
    30	| C1 | Replaceable-event supersession (kind 0 / 3 / 10000–19999) on insert | replaceable.md | `c1_replaceable_supersedes_on_insert` | **[DONE]** kernel | spec §7.1 row "Replaceable kinds"; §3.3 bug #1 |
    31	| C2 | Parameterized replaceable supersession (30000–39999) by `(pubkey, kind, d-tag)` | replaceable.md | `c2_parameterized_replaceable_supersedes_by_dtag` | **[PENDING M3]** | spec §7.1 row "Parameterized replaceable"; §3.3 bug #1 |
    32	| C3 | Kind:5 delete propagation: referenced events removed, tombstone persisted | replaceable.md | `c3_kind5_delete_removes_referenced_and_tombstones` | **[PENDING M3]** | spec §7.1 row "Kind 5 (delete)" |
    33	| C4 | NIP-40 expiration auto-removes event at expiry; survives actor restart | replaceable.md | `c4_nip40_expiration_removes_and_persists_schedule` | **[PENDING M3]** | spec §7.1 row "NIP-40 expiration" |
    34	| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
    35	| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
    36	| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
    37	| C8 | Subscription planner deduplicates overlapping interests into one wire REQ per relay, auto-closes on EOSE / last-consumer-drop, and buffers ingress to ≤60Hz per view | subs.md | `c8_subscriptions_coalesce_autoclose_and_buffer` | **[PENDING M2]** | spec §7.2; §3.3 bug #2, bug #8 |
    38	| C9 | Provenance preserved: same event id arriving from N relays merges into one stored event with N-entry provenance set; original `id` and signature untouched | sync.md | `c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries` | **[PENDING M3]** | aim §6 doctrine 10; spec §7.1 row "Provenance"; §3.3 bug #10 |
    39	| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[LANDED M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |
    40	| C11 | Signer onboarding: pasted `bunker://` URL parses + connects via NIP-46; "create new nsec" generates, NIP-49-encrypts, and persists via KeyringCapability — both as kernel actions, no app code | signers.md | `c11_bunker_url_and_nsec_creation_complete_via_actions` | **[PENDING M6]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M6"; spec §7.4 |
    41	| C12 | Account switch is a state transition: dispatching the switch action re-resolves every `ActiveAccount`-scoped view without the app issuing CLOSE/REQ or rebuilding view handles | sessions.md | `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance` | **[PENDING M8]** | D4; spec §7.4; §3.3 bug #5; M2 §4 trigger A4 |
    42	| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
    43	
    44	**Bullet count:** 13 (eleven sourced verbatim from `scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md`; two — **C3** kind:5 delete propagation and **C4** NIP-40 expiration — derived from `product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1 because they are guaranteed invariants of the same insert path and the contract is incomplete without them).
    45	
    46	**Test names** (13 behavior tests + 1 coverage meta-test = 14 total, all in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs` — see [test-scaffolding.md](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md) for the harness):
    47	
    48	1. `c1_replaceable_supersedes_on_insert`
    49	2. `c2_parameterized_replaceable_supersedes_by_dtag`
    50	3. `c3_kind5_delete_removes_referenced_and_tombstones`
    51	4. `c4_nip40_expiration_removes_and_persists_schedule`
    52	5. `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs`
    53	6. `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays`
    54	7. `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed`
    55	8. `c8_subscriptions_coalesce_autoclose_and_buffer`
    56	9. `c9_provenance_merges_across_relay_redeliveries`
    57	10. `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss`
    58	11. `c11_bunker_url_and_nsec_creation_complete_via_actions`
    59	12. `c12_account_switch_rebinds_views_without_imperative_dance`
    60	13. `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place`
    61	14. `contract_surface_complete` — **meta-test**; asserts every behavior listed in this index has a corresponding `#[test] fn` in `framework_magic_contract.rs` (ignored or not). Drift between this doc and the test file fails the build.
    62	
    63	Tests for behaviors whose owning milestone has not landed are checked in with `#[ignore = "pending M_n"]`; the meta-test still counts them. This is the "doc says 13, code tests 11" regression the file-naming convention break (cross-cutting, not milestone-prefixed) is designed to support — see [test-scaffolding.md](framework-magic/test-scaffolding.md) §1.
    64	
    65	## How this contract evolves
    66	
    67	Every milestone owner adds a **"framework-magic delta"** subsection to their exit-gate report. The delta names:
    68	
    69	1. Which contract bullets transitioned from `[PENDING M_n]` to `[DONE]`.
    70	2. Which `#[ignore = "pending M_n"]` lines were removed from `framework_magic_contract.rs`.
    71	3. Whether the contract gained or lost a bullet during the milestone (rare; bullet count changes need an ADR).
    72	
    73	The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.
    74	
    75	## Research citations (resolved)
    76	
    77	The following items were pending research; citations are now concrete per `docs/research/ndk/kind3-auto-tracking.md` and `docs/research/applesauce/event-store-query-builders.md`:
    78	
    79	- `kind3.md` §3 — NDK's session layer opens a long-lived REQ for active-user events (including kind:3) at `sessions/src/store.ts:184-194`; kind:3 is processed in `handleContactListEvent` at `store.ts:492-512`, which updates `session.followSet`. Core NDK has **no** automatic open-subscription rewire on follow-list change; Svelte gets it via runes (`subscription.svelte.ts:164-177`), React requires explicit deps (`subscribe.ts:110`). NMP's kernel fills this gap with `Trigger::FollowListChanged` (C5).
    80	- `kind3.md` §4 — Applesauce's query-builder magic is `EventModels.model(Constructor, ...args)` at `event-models.ts:50-86`, backed by `share({resetOnRefCountZero: timer(60_000)})`. The `OutboxModel` composition at `models/outbox.ts:14-24` uses `switchMap` into per-contact `ReplaceableModel(kind:10002)` instances — when a kind:3 arrives, `ContactsModel` re-emits, `OutboxModel` switchMaps the new contact list, and every downstream relay-set consumer updates automatically. NMP's `ViewModule.dependencies()` + `Trigger::FollowListChanged` is the analog.
    81	- `outbox.md` §2 — NDK's relay auto-add on NIP-65 arrival is `refreshRelayConnections` at `core/src/ndk/index.ts:458-471` + `subscription/index.ts:787-812`. It **only adds** relays (never removes) and is triggered by NIP-65, not kind:3. NMP's wire-emitter diff (CLOSE + REQ delta) is strictly more correct.
    82	- `subs.md` §3 — Applesauce's logical-vs-wire split: `EventModels.model()` at `event-models.ts:50-86` is the logical layer (one shared pipeline per `(constructor, args)` hash); the underlying `EventStore.insert$` / `remove$` streams are the wire layer. NMP's `LogicalInterest` (`subscription-compilation/intro.md` §2.1) covers the same split.
    83	- `sync.md` §4 — Applesauce's watermark equivalent is the `claimLatest` / `claimEvents` refcount pair (`observable/claim-latest.ts`, `claim-events.ts`) plus the `EventMemory` LRU touch on claim (`event-memory.ts:188`). Coverage-awareness is implicit in the `eventLoader` fallback (`event-store.ts:102-104`): if the event is not in memory and the loader returns nothing, the miss is treated as authoritative for that pointer. NMP's explicit `(filter_sig, relay)` watermark is a more precise analog.
    84	
    85	## Non-goals
    86	
    87	- This document does **not** specify HOW the framework implements each behavior — that lives in the milestone design doc named in the table.
    88	- This document does **not** duplicate `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1 invariants — `replaceable.md` references the rows; it does not restate them.
    89	- This document does **not** introduce new types or traits — `PublishPlanner`, `ViewModule`, `LogicalInterest`, `SubscriptionCompiler`, `MailboxCache`, `KeyringCapability` are already defined in the cited design docs and product spec. The contract uses those names.
    90	- This document does **not** describe the proof app, the starter app, or the kernel-substrate trait families — those are the substrate the contract holds the framework to.

     1	//! Kernel request coordination — relay state transitions, startup REQs, view
     2	//! open/close dispatch, and the core `req` / `defer_outbound` / `record_tx`
     3	//! primitives.
     4	//!
     5	//! Per-view request builders are in sibling files:
     6	//! - `profile.rs` — profile/author open/close/claim/release
     7	//! - `thread.rs`  — thread open/close/hydration
     8	
     9	mod profile;
    10	mod thread;
    11	
    12	use super::*;
    13	
    14	impl Kernel {
    15	    pub(crate) fn relay_connecting(&mut self, role: RelayRole) {
    16	        let relay = self.relay_mut(role);
    17	        relay.connection = "connecting".to_string();
    18	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
    19	        self.log(format!("connecting {} relay {}", role.key(), role.url()));
    20	    }
    21	
    22	    pub(crate) fn relay_connected(&mut self, role: RelayRole) {
    23	        let relay = self.relay_mut(role);
    24	        relay.connection = "connected".to_string();
    25	        relay.connected_at = Some(Instant::now());
    26	        relay.last_error = None;
    27	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
    28	        self.log(format!("{} relay connected", role.key()));
    29	    }
    30	
    31	    pub(crate) fn relay_failed(&mut self, role: RelayRole, error: String) {
    32	        let relay = self.relay_mut(role);
    33	        relay.connection = "backing_off".to_string();
    34	        relay.last_error = Some(truncate(&error, 160));
    35	        relay.reconnect_count = relay.reconnect_count.saturating_add(1);
    36	        self.thread_ids_inflight = false;
    37	        self.thread_replies_inflight = false;
    38	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
    39	        self.log(format!(
    40	            "{} relay error: {}",
    41	            role.key(),
    42	            truncate(&error, 140)
    43	        ));
    44	        for sub in self.wire_subs.values_mut() {
    45	            if sub.role == role && sub.state != "closed" {
    46	                sub.state = "retrying".to_string();
    47	            }
    48	        }
    49	    }
    50	
    51	    pub(crate) fn relay_closed(&mut self, role: RelayRole) {
    52	        self.relay_mut(role).connection = "closed".to_string();
    53	        for sub in self.wire_subs.values_mut() {
    54	            if sub.role == role {
    55	                sub.state = "closed".to_string();
    56	            }
    57	        }
    58	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
    59	    }
    60	
    61	    pub(crate) fn startup_requests(&mut self) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
    62	        self.contacts_deadline = Some(Instant::now() + Duration::from_secs(3));
    63	        let seeds = seed_accounts();
    64	        let seed_pubkeys = seeds.iter().map(|seed| seed.pubkey).collect::<Vec<_>>();
    65	
    66	        for seed in &seeds {
    67	            self.timeline_authors.insert(seed.pubkey.to_string());
    68	            self.log(format!(
    69	                "seed account: {} {}",
    70	                seed.name,
    71	                short_hex(seed.pubkey)
    72	            ));
    73	        }
    74	
    75	        let mut requests = Vec::new();
    76	        requests.push(self.req(
    77	            RelayRole::Content,
    78	            "seed-bootstrap",
    79	            "seed author bootstrap timeline",
    80	            json!({"kinds":[1,6],"authors":seed_pubkeys.clone(),"limit":80}),
    81	        ));
    82	        requests.push(self.req(
    83	            RelayRole::Indexer,
    84	            "profile-target",
    85	            "target kind:0 profile via indexer",
    86	            json!({"kinds":[0],"authors":[TEST_PUBKEY],"limit":1}),
    87	        ));
    88	        requests.push(self.req(
    89	            RelayRole::Indexer,
    90	            "target-relays",
    91	            "target NIP-65 relay list",
    92	            json!({"kinds":[10002],"authors":[TEST_PUBKEY],"limit":1}),
    93	        ));
    94	        requests.push(self.req(
    95	            RelayRole::Indexer,
    96	            "seed-contacts",
    97	            "seed kind:3 contacts via indexer",
    98	            json!({"kinds":[3],"authors":seed_pubkeys.clone(),"limit":10}),
    99	        ));
   100	        requests.push(self.req(
   101	            RelayRole::Indexer,
   102	            "seed-profiles",
   103	            "seed kind:0 profiles via indexer",
   104	            json!({"kinds":[0],"authors":seed_pubkeys.clone(),"limit":20}),
   105	        ));
   106	        requests.push(self.req(
   107	            RelayRole::Indexer,
   108	            "seed-relays",
   109	            "seed NIP-65 relay lists",
   110	            json!({"kinds":[10002],"authors":seed_pubkeys,"limit":10}),
   111	        ));
   112	        self.requested_profiles.insert(TEST_PUBKEY.to_string());
   113	        for seed in seed_accounts() {
   114	            self.requested_profiles.insert(seed.pubkey.to_string());
   115	        }
   116	        requests
   117	    }
   118	
   119	    pub(crate) fn active_subscriptions(&self, role: RelayRole) -> Vec<String> {
   120	        self.wire_subs
   121	            .values()
   122	            .filter(|sub| {
   123	                sub.role == role && !matches!(sub.state.as_str(), "closed" | "closed_by_relay")
   124	            })
   125	            .map(|sub| sub.id.clone())
   126	            .collect()
   127	    }
   128	
   129	    pub(crate) fn pending_view_requests(&mut self) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
   130	        let mut requests = Vec::new();
   131	        while let Some(message) = self.deferred_outbound.pop_front() {
   132	            requests.push(message);
   133	        }
   134	        // Check time-gated timeline open (contacts_deadline may have elapsed).
   135	        requests.extend(self.maybe_open_timeline());
   136	        if self.author_request_pending {
   137	            requests.extend(self.author_requests());
   138	        }
   139	        if self.thread_request_pending {
   140	            requests.extend(self.prepare_thread_requests());
   141	        }
   142	        if self.diagnostic_firehose.is_some()
   143	            && !self
   144	                .wire_subs
   145	                .keys()
   146	                .any(|sub_id| sub_id.starts_with("diag-firehose-"))
   147	        {
   148	            requests.extend(self.firehose_requests());
   149	        }
   150	        requests.extend(self.pending_profile_claim_requests());
   151	        requests.extend(self.maybe_open_thread_hydration());
   152	        requests
   153	    }
   154	
   155	    pub(crate) fn close_subscriptions_with_prefixes(
   156	        &mut self,
   157	        prefixes: &[&str],
   158	    ) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
   159	        let mut closes = Vec::new();
   160	        for sub in self.wire_subs.values_mut() {
   161	            if prefixes.iter().any(|prefix| sub.id.starts_with(prefix))
   162	                && !matches!(sub.state.as_str(), "closed" | "closed_by_relay")
   163	            {
   164	                sub.state = "closed".to_string();
   165	                sub.close_reason = Some("view closed".to_string());
   166	                closes.push(OutboundMessage {
   167	                    role: sub.role,
   168	                    text: json!(["CLOSE", sub.id]).to_string(),
   169	                });
   170	            }
   171	        }
   172	        closes
   173	    }
   174	
   175	    pub(crate) fn req(
   176	        &mut self,
   177	        role: RelayRole,
   178	        sub_id: &str,
   179	        summary: &str,
   180	        filter: Value,
   181	    ) -> OutboundMessage {
   182	        self.log(format!("REQ {sub_id}@{}: {summary}", role.key()));
   183	        self.wire_subs.insert(
   184	            sub_id.to_string(),
   185	            WireSub {
   186	                id: sub_id.to_string(),
   187	                role,
   188	                filter_summary: summary.to_string(),
   189	                state: "opening".to_string(),
   190	                opened_at: Instant::now(),
   191	                last_event_at: None,
   192	                eose_at: None,
   193	                close_reason: None,
   194	            },
   195	        );
   196	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
   197	        OutboundMessage {
   198	            role,
   199	            text: json!(["REQ", sub_id, filter]).to_string(),
   200	        }
   201	    }
   202	
   203	    pub(crate) fn defer_outbound(&mut self, message: OutboundMessage) {
   204	        self.log(format!(
   205	            "defer {} outbound until relay reconnects",
   206	            message.role.key()
   207	        ));
   208	        self.deferred_outbound.push_back(message);
   209	        while self.deferred_outbound.len() > 64 {
   210	            self.deferred_outbound.pop_front();
   211	        }
   212	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
   213	    }
   214	
   215	    pub(crate) fn record_tx(&mut self, role: RelayRole, bytes: usize) {
   216	        let relay = self.relay_mut(role);
   217	        relay.counters.bytes_tx = relay.counters.bytes_tx.saturating_add(bytes as u64);
   218	    }
   219	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	//! Profile, author, and diagnostic-firehose request builders.
     2	//!
     3	//! # M2 migration plan (compiler.md §3.5)
     4	//! Per `docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md` §3.5, these request
     5	//! builders are scheduled for replacement by `SubscriptionCompiler`-driven
     6	//! interest registration once the wire-emitter, InterestRegistry, and
     7	//! trigger-based recompilation infrastructure land (M2 full migration):
     8	//!
     9	//! - `open_author`         → register three LogicalInterests; call compiler.recompile()
    10	//! - `claim_profile`       → register LogicalInterest { kinds:[0], limit:1 }; dedup via registry
    11	//! - `release_profile`     → unregister LogicalInterest by InterestId
    12	//! - `close_author`        → drop interests by InterestId; recompile(Trigger::ViewClose)
    13	//! - `author_requests`     → disappears (replaced by open_author interest registration)
    14	//! - `profile_claim_request` → disappears (compiler routes via Stage 1+2)
    15	//! - `pending_profile_claim_requests` → disappears (compiler handles deferred relay reconnect)
    16	//! - `open_firehose_tag`   → register LogicalInterest { kinds:[1], tags:{#t:[tag]} }
    17	//! - `firehose_requests`   → disappears (replaced by open_firehose_tag registration)
    18	//!
    19	//! The `req()` helper and `RelayRole`-based routing are replaced by the
    20	//! wire-emitter's `emit_req(relay_url, sub_id, filter)` call.
    21	
    22	use super::super::*;
    23	
    24	impl Kernel {
    25	    pub(crate) fn open_author(
    26	        &mut self,
    27	        pubkey: String,
    28	        can_send: bool,
    29	    ) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
    30	        match self.selected_author.as_mut() {
    31	            Some(interest) if interest.key == pubkey => {
    32	                interest.refcount = interest.refcount.saturating_add(1);
    33	            }
    34	            _ => {
    35	                self.selected_author = Some(ViewInterest {
    36	                    key: pubkey.clone(),
    37	                    refcount: 1,
    38	                });
    39	            }
    40	        }
    41	        self.author_request_pending = true;
    42	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
    43	        self.log(format!("open author view {}", short_hex(&pubkey)));
    44	
    45	        if can_send {
    46	            self.author_requests()
    47	        } else {
    48	            self.log("author view request queued until relay connects");
    49	            Vec::new()
    50	        }
    51	    }
    52	
    53	    pub(crate) fn open_firehose_tag(
    54	        &mut self,
    55	        tag: String,
    56	        can_send: bool,
    57	    ) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
    58	        let tag = tag.trim().trim_start_matches('#').to_lowercase();
    59	        if tag.is_empty() {
    60	            return Vec::new();
    61	        }
    62	        match self.diagnostic_firehose.as_mut() {
    63	            Some(interest) if interest.key == tag => {
    64	                interest.refcount = interest.refcount.saturating_add(1);
    65	                return Vec::new();
    66	            }
    67	            _ => {
    68	                self.diagnostic_firehose = Some(ViewInterest {
    69	                    key: tag.clone(),
    70	                    refcount: 1,
    71	                });
    72	            }
    73	        }
    74	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
    75	        self.log(format!("open diagnostic firehose #{tag}"));
    76	
    77	        if can_send {
    78	            self.firehose_requests()
    79	        } else {
    80	            self.log("diagnostic firehose queued until relay connects");
    81	            Vec::new()
    82	        }
    83	    }
    84	
    85	    pub(crate) fn claim_profile(
    86	        &mut self,
    87	        pubkey: String,
    88	        consumer_id: String,
    89	        can_send: bool,
    90	    ) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
    91	        let (inserted, refcount) = {
    92	            let consumers = self.profile_claims.entry(pubkey.clone()).or_default();
    93	            let inserted = consumers.insert(consumer_id.clone());
    94	            (inserted, consumers.len())
    95	        };
    96	        if inserted {
    97	            self.log(format!(
    98	                "claim profile {} consumer {} ref {}",
    99	                short_hex(&pubkey),
   100	                truncate(&consumer_id, 80),
   101	                refcount
   102	            ));
   103	        }
   104	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
   105	
   106	        if self.profiles.contains_key(&pubkey)
   107	            || self.requested_profiles.contains(&pubkey)
   108	            || self.pending_profiles.contains(&pubkey)
   109	        {
   110	            return Vec::new();
   111	        }
   112	
   113	        if can_send {
   114	            self.profile_claim_request(pubkey)
   115	        } else {
   116	            self.pending_profiles.insert(pubkey);
   117	            self.log("profile claim queued until indexer connects");
   118	            Vec::new()
   119	        }
   120	    }
   121	
   122	    pub(crate) fn release_profile(
   123	        &mut self,
   124	        pubkey: &str,
   125	        consumer_id: &str,
   126	    ) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
   127	        let mut remove_claim = false;
   128	        let mut remaining = 0;
   129	        if let Some(consumers) = self.profile_claims.get_mut(pubkey) {
   130	            consumers.remove(consumer_id);
   131	            remaining = consumers.len();
   132	            remove_claim = consumers.is_empty();
   133	        }
   134	        if remove_claim {
   135	            self.profile_claims.remove(pubkey);
   136	            self.pending_profiles.remove(pubkey);
   137	        }
   138	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
   139	        self.log(format!(
   140	            "release profile {} consumer {} ref {}",
   141	            short_hex(pubkey),
   142	            truncate(consumer_id, 80),
   143	            remaining
   144	        ));
   145	        Vec::new()
   146	    }
   147	
   148	    pub(crate) fn close_author(&mut self, pubkey: &str) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
   149	        let Some(interest) = self.selected_author.as_mut() else {
   150	            return Vec::new();
   151	        };
   152	        if interest.key != pubkey {
   153	            return Vec::new();
   154	        }
   155	        interest.refcount = interest.refcount.saturating_sub(1);
   156	        if interest.refcount > 0 {
   157	            self.changed_since_emit = true;
   158	            return Vec::new();
   159	        }
   160	
   161	        self.selected_author = None;
   162	        self.author_request_pending = false;
   163	        self.changed_since_emit = true;
   164	        self.log(format!("close author view {}", short_hex(pubkey)));
   165	        self.close_subscriptions_with_prefixes(&[
   166	            "author-profile-",
   167	            "author-notes-",
   168	            "author-relays-",
   169	        ])
   170	    }
   171	
   172	    pub(crate) fn firehose_requests(&mut self) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
   173	        let Some(tag) = self
   174	            .diagnostic_firehose
   175	            .as_ref()
   176	            .map(|interest| interest.key.clone())
   177	        else {
   178	            return Vec::new();
   179	        };
   180	        self.diagnostic_firehose_seq = self.diagnostic_firehose_seq.saturating_add(1);
   181	        vec![self.req(
   182	            RelayRole::Content,
   183	            &format!("diag-firehose-{}", self.diagnostic_firehose_seq),
   184	            &format!("diagnostic hashtag firehose #{tag}"),
   185	            json!({"kinds":[1],"#t":[tag],"limit":500}),
   186	        )]
   187	    }
   188	
   189	    pub(crate) fn pending_profile_claim_requests(&mut self) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
   190	        let authors = self.pending_profiles.iter().cloned().collect::<Vec<_>>();
   191	        let mut requests = Vec::new();
   192	        for author in authors {
   193	            if self.profile_claims.contains_key(&author)
   194	                && !self.profiles.contains_key(&author)
   195	                && !self.requested_profiles.contains(&author)
   196	            {
   197	                requests.extend(self.profile_claim_request(author));
   198	            } else {
   199	                self.pending_profiles.remove(&author);
   200	            }
   201	        }
   202	        requests
   203	    }
   204	
   205	    pub(crate) fn profile_claim_request(&mut self, pubkey: String) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
   206	        self.pending_profiles.remove(&pubkey);
   207	        if self.profiles.contains_key(&pubkey) || !self.requested_profiles.insert(pubkey.clone()) {
   208	            return Vec::new();
   209	        }
   210	        self.profile_req_seq = self.profile_req_seq.saturating_add(1);
   211	        vec![self.req(
   212	            RelayRole::Indexer,
   213	            &format!("profile-claim-{}", self.profile_req_seq),
   214	            &format!("claimed UI profile {}", short_hex(&pubkey)),
   215	            json!({"kinds":[0],"authors":[pubkey],"limit":1}),
   216	        )]
   217	    }
   218	
   219	    pub(crate) fn author_requests(&mut self) -> Vec<OutboundMessage> {
   220	        let Some(pubkey) = self
   221	            .selected_author
   222	            .as_ref()
   223	            .map(|interest| interest.key.clone())
   224	        else {
   225	            self.author_request_pending = false;
   226	            return Vec::new();
   227	        };
   228	
   229	        self.author_request_pending = false;
   230	        self.author_view_seq = self.author_view_seq.saturating_add(1);
   231	        self.requested_profiles.insert(pubkey.clone());
   232	        let mut requests = vec![
   233	            self.req(
   234	                RelayRole::Indexer,
   235	                &format!("author-relays-{}", self.author_view_seq),
   236	                &format!("selected author NIP-65 {}", short_hex(&pubkey)),
   237	                json!({"kinds":[10002],"authors":[pubkey.clone()],"limit":1}),
   238	            ),
   239	            self.req(
   240	                RelayRole::Indexer,
   241	                &format!("author-profile-{}", self.author_view_seq),
   242	                &format!("selected author kind:0 {}", short_hex(&pubkey)),
   243	                json!({"kinds":[0],"authors":[pubkey.clone()],"limit":1}),
   244	            ),
   245	            self.req(
   246	                RelayRole::Content,
   247	                &format!("author-notes-{}", self.author_view_seq),
   248	                &format!("selected author notes {}", short_hex(&pubkey)),
   249	                json!({"kinds":[1,6],"authors":[pubkey],"limit":100}),
   250	            ),
   251	        ];
   252	        requests.append(&mut self.maybe_open_thread_hydration());
   253	        requests
   254	    }
   255	}

   125	
   126	    /// Mutable access to the registry — view modules push interests through

   128	    pub fn registry_mut(&mut self) -> &mut InterestRegistry {

   130	    }
   131	
   132	    /// Register or replace the mailbox entry for `pubkey` (test helper /
   133	    /// M2-phase-2 path).
   134	    pub fn set_mailbox(&mut self, pubkey: String, write_relays: &[&str]) {
   135	        let snapshot = MailboxSnapshot {
   136	            write_relays: write_relays.iter().map(|r| r.to_string()).collect(),
   137	            read_relays: vec![],
   138	            both_relays: vec![],
   139	        };
   140	        self.mailboxes.put(pubkey, snapshot);
   141	    }
   142	
   143	    /// Compile counter (one increment per planner invocation).
   144	    pub fn compile_count(&self) -> u64 {
   145	        self.compile_count
   146	    }
   147	
   148	    /// Enqueue a trigger. Coalesced with siblings until the next `drain_tick`.
   149	    pub fn enqueue_trigger(&mut self, trigger: CompileTrigger) {

   151	    }
   152	
   153	    /// Recompile from current registry + mailbox state, diff against the
   154	    /// last-compiled plan, and return the WireFrame delta. Updates the
   155	    /// lifecycle gate; diverts REQs targeting auth-paused relays into the
   156	    /// pending-auth buffer.
   157	    pub fn recompile_and_diff(&mut self) -> Result<Vec<WireFrame>, PlannerError> {

   170	        }

   172	        let prior = self.current_plan.as_ref();

**REPORT**

Fixed and pushed: `5d157c6 fix(codex): correct T53 follow-up docs`.

Fixes:
- Corrected stale `canonical_filter_hash` docs in [plan.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a57091c921169bd11/crates/nmp-core/src/planner/plan.rs:96).
- Corrected the C10 proof path in [negentropy.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a57091c921169bd11/docs/perf/m4/negentropy.md:83).

Verification on pushed `origin/master`:
- `cargo test --workspace` passed.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` passed.

Orchestrator follow-up:
- **D2 production wiring gap:** [subs/mod.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a57091c921169bd11/crates/nmp-core/src/subs/mod.rs:168) runs the hook if installed, but `rg` finds `set_coverage_hook` only in tests/docs, and current kernel request builders still emit raw REQs via [requests/mod.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a57091c921169bd11/crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests/mod.rs:175) / [profile.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a57091c921169bd11/crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests/profile.rs:219). Suggested approach: wire the production actor/kernel plan-emission path through `SubscriptionLifecycle` with the NIP-77 hook, or downgrade the M4 “actor installs hook” status until that path exists.
- **Contract-suite guard mismatch:** [framework_magic_contract.rs](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a57091c921169bd11/crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs:3) and [framework-magic.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/.claude/worktrees/agent-a57091c921169bd11/docs/design/framework-magic.md:46) still say the canonical 14 tests live in `framework_magic_contract.rs`, but `cargo test -p nmp-testing --test framework_magic_contract -- --list` reports 13 tests and C10 now lives in `framework_magic_c10.rs`. Suggested approach: either add a contract-binary wrapper/meta-check for the sibling test, or revise the contract docs/scaffolding to define the framework-magic suite as multi-binary.
